2025-06-16 Other routine
39m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 6-16-25

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two variance requests (BZA 2025-006 and BZA 2025-007) filed by Eric Black and Kimberly Keenly for property at 1438 O'Bannonville Road. The first variance requested a shared 25-foot panhandle road frontage for two lots instead of the required 25 feet each, and the second requested a reduced lot size for the front lot since the panhandle area does not count toward the minimum 40,000 sq ft lot area in the R1 district. Both variances were unanimously approved, allowing the applicants to consolidate three existing parcels into two buildable lots.

Variance for shared 25-foot panhandle road frontage (BZA 2025-006)Variance for reduced lot size in R1 district (BZA 2025-007)Consolidation of three parcels into two buildable lots at 1438 O'Bannonville RoadFlag/panhandle lot regulations in R1 zoning districtNeed for two alternate BZA board membersUpcoming chicken variance case for exceeding four-chicken limit on small lot

Decisions (4)

  • Approved video minutes of May 19th meeting (4 yes, 1 abstain)
  • BZA 2025-006 variance approved unanimously - shared 25-foot panhandle road frontage for both lots
  • BZA 2025-007 variance approved unanimously - reduced lot size for lot 1 (35,785 sq ft vs 40,000 sq ft minimum)
  • Motion to adjourn approved unanimously

Action Items (5)

  • Staff to send notice of action and BZA findings of fact sheet to applicants (Eric Black and Kimberly Keenly)
  • Staff to look into addressing/numbering for the two new parcels
  • Board members to recruit two alternate members for open positions
  • Zoning workshop scheduled at 6:00 PM (date not specified)
  • BZA application deadline of July 2nd for potential July 21st meeting cases

Financials

  • 40,000 square feet - minimum lot area required in R1 zoning district
  • 43,560 square feet - proposed lot 1 total area including panhandle
  • 35,785 square feet - proposed lot 1 area excluding panhandle (approximately 4,215 sq ft under minimum)
  • 42,011 square feet - proposed lot 2 area (meets minimum)
  • 25 feet - panhandle width
  • 544 feet - panhandle length
  • 2.01 acres - total area of three existing parcels
  • 36,000 square feet - 10% relief threshold staff could have granted

16m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 6-10-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on June 10, 2025. Key actions included accepting two firefighter/paramedic resignations, approving a resolution to authorize the purchase of a new fire truck (quint), hiring two part-time firefighter EMTs, declaring surplus items for sale on GovDeals, and scheduling a special zoning workshop for June 25, 2025. Trustees also recognized fiscal officer Laura for her outstanding work during the biannual audit and expressed condolences to the Swift Pappas family.

Station 18 roof construction progressCPA class number 13 graduation with 4 graduatesBiannual audit in progressSuccessful community cleanup dayTwo full-time firefighter/paramedic resignationsAuthorization to purchase new fire truck (quint)Scheduling zoning workshop with Zoning Commission, BZA, and attorneySurplus equipment disposal via GovDealsHiring two part-time firefighter EMTsUpcoming community events: Millionare (June 27 - July 7), CPAAA fundraiser at Sprinkles (July 2), Faith Family Fun fireworks at Goshen High SchoolCondolences to Swift Pappas family

Decisions (11)

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for May 27 through June 10, 2025
  • Declared surplus/obsolete items for sale on GovDeals (projector, armrests, old chair, etc.)
  • Scheduled special meeting/zoning workshop for June 25, 2025 at 6 PM with Zoning Commission, BZA, and Attorney Kevin Maloff
  • Accepted resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Ryan Petronio effective June 21, 2025
  • Accepted resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Brandon Kilgore effective June 16, 2025
  • Approved Resolution R-013-2025 authorizing purchase of a new fire truck (quint)
  • Adjourned into executive session at 6:15 PM per ORC 121.22G1
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT Josh Libsdorf at $17.50/hr effective June 15, 2025
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT Ryan West at $17.50/hr effective June 15, 2025
  • Adjourned meeting at 6:46 PM

Action Items (3)

  • Administrator Rose and Chief Myers to sign documents related to the quint fire truck purchase
  • Evan to continue cleaning out audio/video room and list surplus items on GovDeals
  • Administrator Rose to coordinate zoning workshop on June 25 with Zoning Commission, BZA, Attorney Kevin Maloff, and Director Langenheim

Financials

  • $17.50/hr pay rate for part-time firefighter EMT Josh Libsdorf
  • $17.50/hr pay rate for part-time firefighter EMT Ryan West
  • $100 donation from Ken Lane to the fire department

ZC 6-3-25

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases. The Commission administered the oath of office to new member Tammy Purcell. They approved a zone change from M1 to PDM for PLK Communities' proposed 274-unit multifamily development on Smith Road with stipulations requiring a traffic study, parking density corrections, and unit density compliance. They also reviewed the final development plan for Woodhall Crossing Section 2, a 31-lot single-family residential phase.

Oath of office for new Zoning Commission member Tammy PurcellZone change from M1 to PDM for PLK Communities multifamily development at 6300 Smith RoadConcerns about traffic congestion on State Route 28 and surrounding roadsDiscussion about rental vs. condominium ownership modelConcern about changing zoning before preliminary plan approval (cart before the horse)Final development plan for Woodhall Crossing Section 2 single-family residentialODOT traffic signal requirements and traffic impact studiesWoodhall Crossing architectural standards and phasing schedule

Decisions (2)

  • Approved March 4, 2025 meeting minutes
  • Approved ZC-2025-001 zone change from M1 to PDM for Parcel 112208B127 with stipulations requiring traffic study, addressing parking space density, and addressing unit density issues per Chapter 6 (unanimous vote)

Action Items (5)

  • PLK Communities to provide traffic impact study and address ODOT requirements before preliminary plan submission
  • PLK Communities to resolve unit density issue (currently 9.528 units/acre vs. 8 units/acre maximum allowed)
  • PLK Communities to resolve parking space shortfall (417 provided vs. 548 required at 2 stalls per unit)
  • PLK Communities to address jurisdictional wetland delineation findings affecting building footprints
  • Woodhall Crossing developer to apply for ODOT traffic signal as part of Section 3 submittal (required at 85 lots)

Financials

  • 274 apartment and townhome units proposed by PLK Communities
  • 28.77 acres total site area for PLK Communities project
  • 417 total parking stalls provided vs. 548 required (2 per unit)
  • 9.528 units per acre proposed vs. 8 units per acre maximum allowed
  • 65% total open space proposed (25% minimum required)
  • 10.1 acres (35%) proposed impervious area
  • 31 buildable lots on 10.38 acres for Woodhall Crossing Section 2
  • 225 total buildable lots for entire Woodhall Crossing development

2025-05-27 Regular Meeting ceremonial
1h 0m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 5-27-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring the promotion of Officer Jamie McFarland to Sergeant and the swearing-in of three new police officers (Chris Whitaker, Jason Franklin, and Garrett Vanover). The board declared intent to purchase a 2026 E-1 75-foot quint aerial apparatus for approximately $1.194 million, approved OPWC grant applications for road paving totaling nearly $1.19 million, accepted several donations, approved zoning text amendments regarding property maintenance and minimum lot sizes, and promoted John Langenheim to full-time Planning and Zoning Director.

Promotion of Officer Jamie McFarland to Sergeant after 27 years of serviceSwearing-in of three new police officers: Chris Whitaker, Jason Franklin, and Garrett VanoverStation 18 construction progress - steel structure completion and roof starting June 2Memorial Day parade and cemetery commentsOPWC grant application for Gibson Road and Belfast Road pavingAerial apparatus (quint) purchase presentation and intent to purchaseZoning text amendments for property maintenance enforcement and minimum lot sizesPromotion of John Langenheim to full-time Planning and Zoning DirectorTermination of two part-time firefighter EMTsTax Incentive Review Council establishmentCPAAA donations and t-shirt logo authorizationFlags for Heroes program by Goshen Lions Club

  • Motion to accept $200 Kroger gift card from CPAAA to fire department - passed

  • Motion to accept resignation of Tristan Smiley effective April 17, 2025 - passed
  • Motion to hire part-time firefighter EMT Abigail Maines at $17.50/hour effective June 1, 2025 - passed
  • Motion to approve updated records retention schedule - passed
  • Motion to approve flags for heroes on Township property by Goshen Lions Club - passed
  • Motion to authorize CPAAA to use Township logo on t-shirts - passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-009-2025 for OPWC application for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving - passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-010-2025 establishing Tax Incentive Review Council with Administrator Rose and Trustee Allen as representatives - passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-011-2025 for zoning text amendments on property maintenance (grass, junk, debris, unregistered vehicles) - passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-012-2025 for zoning text amendments on minimum lot sizes for R1-R5 zones - passed
  • Motion to declare intent to purchase 2026 E-1 75-foot quint at $1,194,188 - passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:19 PM - passed
  • Motion to renew David Frisby Jr. as Zoning Commission alternate with term expiring June 13, 2027 - passed
  • Motion to promote John Langenheim to full-time Planning and Zoning Director - passed
  • Motion to terminate part-time firefighter EMT Scott Harley effective May 13, 2025 - passed
  • Motion to terminate part-time firefighter EMT Austin Cannon effective May 13, 2025 - passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:32 PM - passed

2025-05-22 Special Meeting contentious
1h 48m ▶ Vimeo

Special BOT Zoning Workshop 5-22-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special zoning workshop with the Zoning Commission, Board of Zoning Appeals, and Zoning Inspector Kevin Maylock to discuss potential amendments to the Zoning Resolution. Key topics included increasing R1 minimum lot sizes from approximately 1 acre to 5 acres, adjusting side yard setbacks, correcting an R5 lot size typo, incorporating property maintenance provisions into the zoning code under House Bill 315, and debating whether to eliminate or modify the Planned Unit Development (PUD) residential provisions. Direction was given to prepare revised lot size and setback numbers for the next regular meeting.

R1 minimum lot size increase from ~1 acre to 5 acresRestructuring minimum lot sizes for R2 through R5 zoning districtsSide yard setback increases across residential zonesR5 lot size typo correction (18,000 should be 8,000 sq ft)Possible elimination or modification of Planned Unit Development (PUD/PDR) residential provisionsLand use plan as critical enforcement tool for zoning decisionsProperty maintenance code incorporation into zoning code under House Bill 315New Community Authority program research (House Bill 315)History of high-density subdivision lawsuits (Cameron Crossing, Woodland Streams)Warren County 5-acre minimum comparisonApartments vs. condominiums for tax base purposesLot width minimums in relation to setback changes

Decisions (6)

  • Agenda adopted unanimously
  • Direction given to draft R1 minimum lot size change to 5 acres for next meeting
  • Direction given to draft proposed lot sizes: R5 at 12,000 sq ft, R4 at 15,000 sq ft, R3 at 25,000 sq ft, R2 at 40,000 sq ft, R1 at 5 acres
  • Direction given to draft side yard setbacks: 20 feet for R1-R3 and 15 feet for R4-R5
  • Dan Hodges' motion for 5 acres without sewer / 3 acres with sewer was withdrawn and tabled to next meeting
  • Final consensus settled on a flat 5-acre R1 minimum without sewer distinction

Action Items (7)

  • Kevin Maylock (Zoning Inspector) to prepare revised Table 5-2 with new lot sizes and setback numbers for next trustees meeting
  • Kevin Maylock to work with Julia Carney at prosecutor's office on resolution language for lot size and setback changes
  • Kevin Maylock to prepare property maintenance code inclusion into zoning code under House Bill 315 for further review
  • Kevin Maylock to continue researching New Community Authority program and awaiting response from Dublin
  • Kevin Maylock to prepare a township zoning map showing quadrants and zoning districts for next workshop meeting
  • Kevin Maylock to research PUD elimination vs. minimum removal with legal counsel for future discussion
  • Board to continue working on land use plan updates as a future priority

Financials

  • Current R1 minimum lot size: 43,400 sq ft (~0.96 acres)
  • Proposed R1 minimum lot size: 5 acres
  • Proposed R5 minimum lot size: 12,000 sq ft (currently listed as 18,000, believed typo for 8,000)
  • Proposed R4 minimum lot size: 15,000 sq ft
  • Proposed R3 minimum lot size: 25,000 sq ft
  • Proposed R2 minimum lot size: 40,000 sq ft
  • Current PD minimum lot size: 5,000 sq ft
  • Average 5-acre lot in Goshen Township selling for $194,000
  • Average 5-acre lot in Warren County selling for $265,000
  • Proposed side yard setbacks: 20 feet for R1-R3, 15 feet for R4-R5
  • New Community Authority board: up to 13 members (7 elected, 6 developer-appointed)
  • Property maintenance grass height discussed at 12 inches (previously 8 inches)

BZA 5-19-25

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two cases: BZA 2025-004 (conditional use for an accessory dwelling unit) and BZA 2025-005 (variance for exceeding maximum square footage on an accessory dwelling). Both cases were filed by Keith Benhase of 5507 Aviator Avenue for a proposed apartment within his existing pole barn for his parents. Both the conditional use and the variance were approved unanimously.

  • Approved variance (BZA 2025-005) to exceed 800 sq ft maximum by 204 sq ft for the accessory dwelling unit at 5507 Aviator Avenue
  • Motion to adjourn approved; BZA closed at 6:57 PM

2025-04-22 Regular Meeting routine
1h 14m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 4-22-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting where Dries Homes presented a major mixed-use development concept called Eagle's Nest on the 164-acre former golf course property, featuring a big box anchor store, 300 apartments, townhomes, and single-family homes. The board approved a 12-month temporary zoning moratorium on large-scale residential developments to address zoning code concerns, authorized the purchase of a new ambulance for $368,326, and approved several other items including an SRO contract with local schools and junk vehicle resolutions for a chronic nuisance property on Linton Road. A zoning workshop was scheduled for May 22, 2025 to begin updating the township's zoning resolution.

Eagle's Nest mixed-use development presentation by Dries Homes on former golf course12-month temporary zoning moratorium on large-scale residential developmentsFire Station 18 construction progress – steel delivery expected this weekPurchase of new Lifeline ambulance to replace 2015 unit with 142,000 milesTIF (Tax Increment Financing) concerns on Eagle's Nest property and township revenue impactLot size and setback concerns for proposed residential developmentTraffic concerns on State Route 28 and Fay RoadSRO contract renewal with Goshen Local SchoolsJunk vehicle nuisance resolutions for 6833 and 6834 Linton Road (ongoing since 2019)Memorial Day parade planning with Grand Marshal Steve Endicott and speaker Troy DayDepartment of Development grant application for land use planningZoning workshop scheduled for May 22 with Zoning Commission, BZA, and attorney Kevin MayloffChamber of Commerce bowling fundraiser eventThree Ohio fire departments lost members during the week

Decisions (15)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (April 9–22, 2025) – passed unanimously
  • Motion to authorize Chief Myers to purchase F-550 Lifeline ambulance for $368,326 with 36-month delivery – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve Resolution 006-2025 temporary 12-month zoning moratorium on large-scale residential developments (emergency) – passed unanimously
  • Motion to schedule Board of Trustees special meeting/zoning workshop for May 22, 2025 at 6 PM – passed unanimously
  • Motion to nominate Alan Freeman and Tom Peck to District 10 Public Works Integrating Committee – passed unanimously
  • Motion to partner with Clermont County Park District to host trick-or-treat at Sagamore Park – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve Memorial Day parade on May 26 at 10 AM with Steve Endicott as Grand Marshal and Troy Day as guest speaker – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve SRO contract with Goshen Local Schools for 2025-2026 school year at $153,000 – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve $700 table sponsorship at annual law enforcement awards banquet – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve Resolution 007-2025 determining junk motor vehicles at 6833 Linton Road – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve Resolution 008-2025 determining junk motor vehicles at 6834 Linton Road – passed unanimously
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:12 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1) – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve Department of Development zoning grant application for a land use plan – passed unanimously
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:48 PM – passed unanimously

Action Items (11)

  • Administrator Rose and Director Langenheim to research legal implications of moratorium on mixed-use developments like the Eagle's Nest proposal
  • Trustees to meet individually with Administrator Rose to provide feedback on the Eagle's Nest development proposal

  • Donna Lusk to contact Sandy Graham for historical Memorial Day parade documentation
  • Staff to investigate status of TIF on Eagle's Nest property with Clermont County

  • $85,000 budgeted in 2025 for ambulance chassis payment
  • $153,000 SRO contract with Goshen Local Schools for 2025-2026

BOT 4-8-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering bill payments, a fire department equipment grant for pagers and radios, a no-parking resolution for Marsh Circle, endorsement of a living history event at Cook Cabin, and several personnel actions following an executive session. Key personnel decisions included rehiring a part-time firefighter/paramedic, hiring a full-time police officer, and promoting an officer to sergeant.

Condolences to the Hauserman family for loss of a loved oneOffer to assist Village of Richmond with flood cleanupTimber Ridge subdivision fiber optic trunk line damage from 2022 boringCCTA special meeting with state representatives Schmidt and Adam Burke on pending legislationOhio State Fire Marshal grant for pagers and radiosNo-parking resolution for Marsh Circle in Woodland Streams neighborhood due to visibility issuesEndorsement of 1840 Living History Camp at Cook CabinExecutive session for personnel mattersRehire of part-time firefighter/paramedicHiring of full-time police officer to fill last vacancyPromotion of Officer McFarland to police sergeant

Decisions (11)

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for March 25 through April 8, 2025
  • Approved increase in revenue and appropriations for Ohio State Fire Marshal grant for pagers and radios
  • Approved increase in appropriations of $14,898.93 for 2025 equipment grant (2193-220-420-0008)
  • Approved Resolution 005-2025 establishing a no-parking area on the north side of Marsh Circle
  • Approved endorsement of the 1840 Living History Camp at Cook Cabin on April 26
  • Approved adjournment into executive session at 6:16 PM under ORC 121.22(G)(1)
  • Approved rehire of part-time firefighter/paramedic Robert Coy Webster at $22.50/hour effective April 20, 2025
  • Approved hiring of Garrett Vanover as full-time police officer contingent upon pre-employment screening
  • Approved promotion of Officer James McFarland to police sergeant effective May 4, 2025
  • Approved adjournment at 7:10 PM

Action Items (6)

  • Trustees to decide on Grand Marshal for upcoming Memorial Day parade by April 22 meeting
  • Trustees to decide on speaker for Memorial Day service on May 26
  • Bob Meadows (Service Director) to coordinate sending a truck and crew to assist Village of Richmond with flood cleanup when waters recede
  • Administrator Rose to post 1840 Living History Camp event on Facebook page and digital sign
  • Administrator Rose to complete pre-employment screening process for Garrett Vanover
  • Bob Meadows to continue monitoring Timber Ridge subdivision fiber optic/trunk line issue with Jay Daniels

Financials

  • $14,898.93 - Ohio State Fire Marshal grant for pagers and radios (equipment grant)
  • $22.50/hour - pay rate for rehired part-time firefighter/paramedic Robert Coy Webster
  • 70 feet - no-parking distance established on Marsh Circle (47 feet from stop sign to tree for visibility)
Trustee AllenTrustee HausermannTrustee HodgesAdministrator RoseBob Meadows (Service Director)Police Chief Bob SeyfriedOfficer James McFarlandJulia (public attendee)Brian (public attendee)Dennis Cooper (Washington Township Trustee, mentioned via email)Robert Coy Webster (rehired firefighter/paramedic)Garrett Vanover (newly hired police officer)

2025-04-03 Special Meeting urgent
1h 16m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Special Zoning Workshop 4-3-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special zoning workshop with the Zoning Commission, Board of Zoning Appeals, and attorney Kevin Mayloff to discuss tools for managing residential development. Key topics included New Community Authorities (NCAs) as a mechanism to assess taxes on new subdivisions to offset community impacts, potential increases to minimum residential lot sizes, setback requirement changes, a possible moratorium on Residential Planned District (RPD) applications, and the need for a comprehensive land use plan. Trustee Hauserman was absent, so major decisions were deferred to a future meeting.

New Community Authority (NCA) under ORC 349 as a tool to assess taxes on new subdivisionsMinimum residential lot size increases (two-acre and five-acre lot discussions)Residential single-family setback requirement changes (particularly side setbacks from 10 to 15-20 feet)Potential moratorium on Residential Planned District (RPD/PD) applicationsOpposition to TIFs and CRAs for residential developmentNeed for a comprehensive land use plan for the townshipSewer and water expansion concerns and their impact on development pressureTownship identity discussion: rural vs. suburban vs. urbanStream/waterway setback requirements proposed by Clermont County Water and SoilBalancing farmer property rights with community growth management

Decisions (2)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed (Allen: Yes, Hodges: Yes)
  • Motion to adjourn the special meeting at 7:18 PM passed (Allen: Yes, Hodges: Yes)

Action Items (9)

  • John Langenheim to research legality and process for implementing a moratorium on RPD/PD applications
  • John Langenheim to develop verbiage for changing PUD side setback requirements and two-acre lot corridor east of Goshen Road
  • Kevin Mayloff to provide list of NCA success stories and connect with attorney Emmett Kelly at Brown Todd Columbus office for further education
  • John Langenheim to obtain sewer and water boundary maps from Clermont County Water and Sewer and Western Water Services
  • John Langenheim to follow up with John McManus at Clermont County Water and Soil on stream/water setback requirement text
  • Trustees to reach out to attorney Ben Yoder regarding moratorium guidance
  • John Langenheim to forward Ohio Bar Association article on NCAs tied to TIFs/CRAs to trustees
  • Schedule next special meeting soon with all three trustees present to continue discussions
  • David Dinwoody suggested Zoning Commission schedule special meetings to expedite verbiage and voting on zoning changes

Financials

  • $50,000 estimated cost for a septic system on a two-acre parcel
  • $66,000 per lot estimated cost for sewer and road infrastructure in a 25-lot development
  • $250,000 estimated cost to bring natural gas down Smith Road (cited by Duke Energy)
  • $150,000 potential cost per two-acre lot to a developer
  • $80,000 potential lot cost if NCA is used to spread infrastructure costs over 30 years
  • $200,000 potential cost per two-acre lot without NCA assistance
  • $250,000-$500,000 estimated cost to bring sewer to a development site

BOT 3-25-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring the swearing-in of two new firefighter/paramedics (Jerry Burns and Ryan Petronio), addressed multiple personnel matters including resignations and new hires, approved the purchase of a station vehicle, accepted a donation and a grant, scheduled a zoning workshop, and delivered a detailed public rebuttal to allegations made by county commissioner candidate Logan Bixler.

Swearing-in ceremony for firefighter/paramedics Jerry Burns and Ryan PetronioFire Station 18 construction progress update - foundation digging scheduled for week of April 7New T-66 equipment delivered for road departmentPersonnel resignations of part-time firefighters Brody Green and Thomas JetterPurchase of 2023 Ford F250 station vehicleDetailed public rebuttal by Administrator Rose to allegations by county commissioner candidate Logan BixlerScheduling of zoning workshop special meeting for April 3, 2025Lateral hire of full-time firefighter paramedic Jameson BenoitHire of part-time fitness trainer Paul Hilliard

  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:45pm passed

  • Motion to adjourn at 7:16pm passed

  • Process lateral hire of Jameson Benoit as full-time firefighter paramedic effective April 13, 2025

  • $5,000 donation from Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association to Police Department

BZA 3-17-2025

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2025-003, a variance request by Brandon Ockey at 6608 Goshen Road to add a 36x40 building addition and two 27x8 additions to an existing accessory structure previously approved by the BZA in 2023. A neighbor spoke in favor of the project, no opposition was presented, and the variance was unanimously approved 4-0. Staff also announced the hiring of John Langenheim as part-time zoning director and noted open alternate positions on the BZA and Zoning Commission.

  • Approved area variance for BZA 2025-003 allowing building additions to existing accessory structure at 6608 Goshen Road (4-0)
  • Motion to adjourn passed (4-0)

2025-03-11 Regular Meeting contentious
25m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 3-11-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on March 11, 2025. A resident presented allegations of corruption regarding Administrator Bob Rose's contract, which the board addressed by noting no legal concerns have been raised by their attorneys. The board accepted firefighter resignations, approved a State Fire Marshal equipment grant, accepted a $45,732 donation from Day Heights Firefighters Association for fire gear, adopted resolutions for cannabis revenue allocation and the Clermont County 9-1-1 plan, and made several zoning board appointments including hiring John Langenheim as Planning and Zoning Director.

Public complaint alleging Administrator Bob Rose's contract is illegal with formal complaints filed to Ohio Ethics Commission and Attorney GeneralStation 18 construction update - completion pushed to April 2026 due to cold weather and permittingAcceptance of Day Heights Firefighters Association donation for fire gearOhio State Fire Marshal Equipment Grant acceptanceCannabis revenue fund allocation resolution (from Ayer Wellness dispensary)Clermont County 9-1-1 plan approval and funding distributionZoning Commission and BZA appointments including hiring of new Planning and Zoning DirectorIndigent burial approvalFirefighter resignations - Chris Spinozzi to City of Fairfield, Adam Reed Daniel to Hamilton Township

Decisions (16)

  • Adopted the agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for February 26 through March 11, 2025
  • Accepted resignation of firefighter paramedic Chris Spinozzi effective March 4, 2025
  • Accepted full-time resignation of firefighter paramedic Adam Reed Daniel and changed classification to part-time at $23.50/hr effective March 28, 2025
  • Accepted Ohio State Fire Marshal Equipment Grant of $14,898.93
  • Accepted donation from Day Heights Firefighters Association of $45,732
  • Increased revenue in gifts and donations account 2111-801-0000 by $45,732
  • Increased appropriations in small tools and minor equipment account 2111-220-430-0000 by $45,732
  • Approved indigent burial of Walter N. Burnett at $903 to Advantage Cremation Care
  • Adopted Resolution R-003-2025 to accept and allocate cannabis revenue funds to general fund
  • Adopted Resolution R-004-2025 approving Clermont County 9-1-1 plan
  • Entered executive session at 6:23 PM per ORC 121.22 G1
  • Appointed Tammy Purcell as alternate on Zoning Commission with term ending March 11, 2027
  • Accepted resignation of John Langenheim from BZA effective March 4, 2025
  • Moved John Purcell from BZA alternate to BZA member with term ending December 31, 2025
  • Adjourned at 7:09 PM

Action Items (5)

  • Administrator Rose to process cannabis revenue funds into the general fund upon receipt of first check
  • Resolution R-004-2025 to be sent back to the 9-1-1 center (Jessica) after passage
  • Chief Myers/Deputy Chief Lynch to purchase 59 pairs of gloves, 13 pagers, and one car radio with Fire Marshal grant
  • Director Miles to process indigent burial reimbursement with Terry Shieldmeyer
  • Finalize hiring of John Langenheim as Planning and Zoning Director

Financials

  • $14,898.93 - Ohio State Fire Marshal Equipment Grant for gloves, pagers, and car radio
  • $45,732 - Donation from Day Heights Firefighters Association for fire gear
  • $903 - Indigent burial cost for Walter N. Burnett
  • $23.50/hr - Part-time rate for Adam Reed Daniel
  • $3.60 per $100 of adult-use cannabis sales goes to host community (3.6% of 10% excise tax)
  • $3,600 - Referenced past overtime pay issue with Bob Rose (historical)

ZC 3-4-25

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held its regular meeting to approve prior meeting minutes, adopt the 2025 meeting schedule, elect officers for 2025, and consider the final development plan for Cove of Barkley Woods Section 2. The FDP for Section 2, consisting of 32 buildable lots on 10.85 acres, was approved with a condition requiring additional landscaping for buffering along lots 69 through 73.

Approval of 2025 meeting scheduleElection of Zoning Commission officers for 2025Cove of Barkley Woods Section 2 Final Development Plan (ZC 2022-003)Buffering and landscaping requirements for perimeter lots 69-73PUD history including preliminary plan approval from 2018 and prior section approvalsDiscussion of second access/egress point and emergency access historyOngoing need for alternate members for Zoning Commission and BZA

Decisions (7)

  • Approved video meeting minutes from January 7, 2025
  • Adopted the 2025 Zoning Commission meeting schedule (first Tuesday of the month at 6:30 PM)
  • Elected Mr. Pegram as Zoning Commission Chairperson for 2025
  • Elected Lee Lewis as Zoning Commission Vice Chairperson for 2025
  • Elected Dave Curie as Zoning Commission Secretary for 2025
  • Approved Zoning Commission case 2022-003 Cove of Barkley Woods Section 2 FDP with condition requiring additional landscaping for near 100% buffer screening along lots 69-73 per Section 10.4 of the zoning resolution
  • Motion to adjourn approved

Action Items (3)

  • Applicant (Kirstie Mormon / developer) to update the final development plan with additional landscaping along lots 69-73 to provide near 100% buffer screening and submit a planting list per Section 10.4
  • Staff (Alyssa) to distribute materials for the upcoming April 1st case to commission members as soon as possible
  • Continue search for alternate members for the Zoning Commission and BZA

Financials

  • 32 buildable lots in Section 2
  • 10.85 acres total area for Section 2
  • 8.87 acres in lots
  • 1.38 acres in right-of-way
  • 0.60 acres open space (5.5%)
  • 2.95 units per acre proposed density
  • Minimum lot size 8,520 square feet
  • Minimum lot width 60 feet
  • Front yard setback 40 feet
  • Minimum rear yard setback 35 feet
  • Minimum side yard setback 5 feet (20 feet total between lots)
  • Minimum 1,400 square feet floor area for homes
  • 20-foot perimeter buffer setback
  • Nearest home approximately 135 feet from lot 78

44m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 2-25-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting featuring recognition of police officers, including the swearing-in of returning Officer Dave Perkins, drug interdiction awards, and Sergeant Skates as Officer of the Year. The Day Heights Memorial Firefighters Association presented donations totaling over $55,000 to local organizations including the fire department. The board approved multiple personnel appointments including two full-time police officers and a part-time planning and zoning director, along with equipment purchases including a 2025 Ford cruiser and a treadmill donation from the CPAAA.

  • Motion to approve out-of-town travel to Houston, Ohio (Aug 18-20) for Officer Sites at $600 registration - passed

  • Motion to approve revision to surplus items sale on GovDeals (removing .40 cal ammo) - passed
  • Motion to purchase three recycled plastic picnic tables from U-Line not to exceed $4,361.31 - passed
  • Motion to authorize $1,654.82 payment to Goshen Township Historical Society for tornado-damaged garage - passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:06 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1) - passed

  • Motion to appoint Jason Franklin as full-time police officer upon pre-employment screening - passed
  • Motion to appoint John Langenheim as part-time planning and zoning director at $28/hour - passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:44 PM - passed

BZA 2-24-25

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on two variance requests (BZA 2025-001 and BZA 2025-002) for a single property on State Route 48. The applicant, Victor Capitula, requested a variance to create a panhandle lot in an R3 zoning district (panhandle lots are only permitted by right in R1) and a front yard setback reduction from 50 feet to 35 feet on the newly created rear lot. Both variances were approved unanimously 5-0 with no public opposition.

Area variance for panhandle lot creation in R3 zoning district (BZA 2025-001)Front yard setback reduction variance from 50 to 35 feet (BZA 2025-002)Lot split of parcel 112207K105 on State Route 48Discussion of zoning code change from previous code allowing panhandle lots in all districts to 2017 code restricting to R1Findings of fact review criteria for BZA variancesNew subdivision (All Crossing) approved nearby

Decisions (4)

  • BZA 2025-001: Approved as submitted - area variance to allow a panhandle lot in an R3 zoning district for parcel 112207K105 (5-0)
  • BZA 2025-002: Approved - front yard setback reduction from 50 feet to 35 feet on the rear split lot of parcel 112207K105 based on findings of fact (5-0)
  • Approval of video minutes from January 27th meeting (5-0)
  • Motion to adjourn (5-0)

Action Items (2)

  • Applicant Victor Capitula to follow up with township ladies/staff regarding approvals
  • Zoning staff to send applications to BZA members once received for potential March 17th cases (deadline February 27th for applications)

  • Front lot after split approximately 12,875 square feet
  • Panhandle/flag lot after split approximately 18,289 square feet

  • Panhandle width: 35 feet, length: 140 feet

  • Proposed homes approximately 1,600-2,000 square feet

23m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 2-11-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting on February 11, 2025 at 6:00 PM. The board approved payment of bills, authorized a cleanup day at the old Green School property, adopted a policy to charge for video public records per Ohio House Bill 315, expanded acceptance of credit card payments via Resolution R-002-2025, and approved out-of-town training for Detective Sergeant Chris McMillan. The board also discussed picnic table replacement/refurbishment but deferred action pending further inspection, and adjourned into executive session regarding employee appointment, employment, or compensation.

Staggy Road speed limit signs and speed study (previously passed resolution; signs now posted at 40 mph)Cleanup day at old Green School property with owner permission and insurance coverageVideo public records charging policy under Ohio House Bill 315Resolution R-002-2025 expanding credit card payment acceptancePicnic table replacement/refurbishment discussion and deferralOut-of-town travel approval for detective sergeant supervisor trainingExecutive session regarding appointment, employment, or compensation of a public employeeCommunity positivity and support including Fight for Megan's blue ribbons on police carsKevin Maloff nominated for Chamber Salute to Leaders

Decisions (9)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (January 19, 2025 through February 11, 2025) – passed
  • Motion to approve cleanup day at the old Green School property – passed
  • Motion to charge for video public records as authorized by Ohio House Bill 315 at staff actual cost of $46.90 per hour – passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-002-2025 expanding acceptance of payments by financial transaction devices – passed
  • Picnic tables discussion – deferred pending further inspection by the service department
  • Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Detective Sergeant Chris McMillan for supervisor training in Columbus, Ohio ($2,150 registration) – passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:22 PM per ORC 121.22 – passed
  • Motion to adjourn the meeting at 7:46 PM – passed

Action Items (4)

  • Director Meadows/Chad Johnson to inspect the condition of picnic table frames (especially rust on bottoms) and report back at a future meeting
  • Service department to deliver wood chips to old Green School property for cleanup day
  • Chief Rose to implement video public records charging policy at $46.90/hour staff actual cost
  • Detective Sergeant Chris McMillan to attend three-week supervisor training and education program in Columbus, Ohio in 2025

Financials

  • $46.90 per hour – staff actual cost rate for charging video public records requests
  • $2,150 – registration cost for Detective Sergeant McMillan's supervisor training program

BOT 1-28-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering the 2024 fiscal year true-up, which showed overall unencumbered fund growth of $500,000 to $5.1 million. The board approved the purchase of a new Bobcat skid steer for the service department, a Chevrolet Tahoe for the fire department, declared surplus police items for sale, amended the Station 18 construction budget, and hired one full-time and six part-time firefighter/EMTs after an executive session.

Electrical aggregation inquiry - positive feedback from other Clermont County communities2024 fiscal year true-up across all township fundsStation 18 fire station demolition and rebuild progress - Conger to deliver trailer week of Feb 24, foundation drawings expected March 6Surplus police equipment and vehicle disposalService department equipment: new Bobcat skid steer purchase and future backhoe-to-front-loader replacement discussionFire department Chevrolet Tahoe purchase for staff vehicle replacementTownship road mileage certification (47.1 miles)Salute to Leaders nomination discussion - Kevin Maloff for Homearama and Clermont County Land Bank mentionedHiring of one full-time and six part-time firefighter/EMTsResignation of part-time firefighter EMT Austin Taylor to Anderson Township2022 tornado reimbursement to assisting communities completedARPA funds fully expended by end of 2024Opioid settlement fund discussionGovernment Finance Officers Association unencumbered fund recommendations (16.7% minimum vs. township's 54.5%)Ohio Township Association Conference upcoming in Columbus

Decisions (19)

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for January 15-28, 2025
  • Declared surplus police items (ammunition, magazines, 2014 Ford Fusion) for sale on GovDeals
  • Amended Station 18 demolition and rebuild budget (Fund 4901) for 2025 to $8,720,260.84
  • Increased appropriations in motor vehicles fund by $74,167.39 for Bobcat skid steer purchase
  • Approved purchase of 2024 T-66 Bobcat compact tracked loader skid steer with accessories for $74,167.39 (Trustee Hodges abstained)
  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Austin Taylor effective 1/31/25
  • Approved purchase of 2024 Chevrolet Tahoe from McCluskey Chevrolet at fleet price of $54,445
  • Certified township road mileage at 47.1 miles as of 12/31/2024
  • Entered executive session at 6:51 p.m. per ORC 121.22 G1
  • Credited part-time firefighter EMT Rick Holland with 119 hours of PTO
  • Hired full-time firefighter paramedic Brandon Kilgore at step one with one-year probation
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT Jason Stevens at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT Tyler Pilcher at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT Tristan Smiley at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT James Jansen at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT Damian Blum at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT Thomas Jetter at $17.50/hr effective 2/9/2025
  • Adjourned at 7:45 p.m.

Action Items (6)

  • Bob Rose to meet individually with trustees to finalize Salute to Leaders selection before the deadline
  • Chad Meadows to research Bobcat front loader options (new and slightly used) with pricing, extended warranty, and Sourcewell discount through rep Bernie
  • Chad Meadows to explore selling the 2017 Case 580 backhoe on GovDeals vs. trade-in and bring dollar amounts for both options back to the board
  • Staff to verify lighting district reassessment took effect by comparing first 2025 auditor check to prior year
  • Chief Myers to price upfitting costs for the new Chevrolet Tahoe
  • Chief Rose to process surplus items for sale on GovDeals

Financials

  • $342,000 total reimbursement checks to entities that assisted with 2022 tornado recovery (75% from Ohio EMA, 25% township)
  • $122,000 decline in general fund due to tornado reimbursement expense
  • $350,000 increase in police unencumbered funds in 2024
  • $50,000 grant for body-worn cameras offsetting planned expense
  • $917,000 ending unencumbered balance for service department funds
  • $5.1 million total ending unencumbered balance (excluding special funds)
  • $12.4 million actual total revenue for 2024
  • $9.7 million actual total expenses for 2024
  • $8,720,260.84 revised Station 18 demolition and rebuild budget
  • $7.4 million Conger construction contract for Station 18
  • $1.1 million contracted services for Station 18 (architect, exhaust, generator, etc.)
  • $108,000 for Station 18 furnishings and operational items
  • $74,167.39 for 2024 T-66 Bobcat skid steer with accessories
  • $54,445 fleet price for 2024 Chevrolet Tahoe
  • $55,000 accumulated in opioid settlement fund
  • $44,000 trade-in value offered for 2017 Case 580 backhoe
  • $8,000 spent on backhoe repairs in last year and a half
  • $361,000 ARPA funds spent down to zero in 2024
  • $17.50/hour pay rate for part-time firefighter EMTs
  • $1,500 saved on Bobcat through Sourcewell membership

2025-01-27 Regular Meeting routine
10m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 1-27-25

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held its first meeting of 2025, focusing on organizational matters. The board adopted the 2025 meeting schedule with a correction to the October date, elected officers for the year, and briefly discussed upcoming accessory dwelling unit cases and potential zoning language amendments. No old or new case business was presented.

2025 BZA meeting schedule adoption with October date correctionElection of 2025 BZA officers (Chair, Vice Chair, Secretary)Upcoming accessory dwelling unit variance applications in Stone Pillars and elsewherePotential zoning language amendment for accessory dwelling unitsMr. Langenheim's possible future conflict of interest and potential departure from the board

Decisions (6)

  • Approved minutes from previous meeting (unanimous)
  • Adopted 2025 BZA meeting schedule with correction of October date to the 20th (unanimous)
  • Elected Mr. Siegler as BZA Chairperson for 2025 (unanimous)
  • Elected Mr. Jesse Shaw as BZA Vice Chairperson for 2025 (unanimous)
  • Elected Mr. Pruitt as BZA Secretary for 2025 (unanimous)
  • Motion to adjourn carried (unanimous)

Action Items (4)

  • Harry Engel (zoning) to process any new BZA applications received before February 4th deadline
  • Harry Engel to follow up with contractors regarding accessory dwelling unit applications in and outside Stone Pillars
  • Harry Engel to bring accessory dwelling unit zoning language amendment to trustees for rewording discussion
  • Dennis to fix meeting document layout issues for next meeting

Financials

  • None recorded

2025-01-14 Regular Meeting ceremonial
45m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 1-14-25

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their organizational and regular meeting for January 2025. The board delivered a comprehensive state of the township address highlighting 2024 accomplishments including nearly $7 million in secured funding, Fire Station 18 reconstruction, major paving projects, and infrastructure improvements. Trustee Lisa Allen was elected as the 2025 board chairperson and Trustee Bob Hauserman as vice chair. The board also addressed organizational items for 2025, accepted personnel changes, and heard a resident proposal to explore an electrical aggregation program for cost savings.

2025 organizational meeting: election of chair and vice chairState of the Township address summarizing 2024 accomplishmentsSnow removal operations and costs for two major snowstorms (13-15 inches total)Resident proposal for electrical aggregation program to reduce electricity costsFire Station 18 reconstruction update - foundation work expected first week of MarchClermont County Auditor's office outreach and open data resourcesOfficer resignation (Beckelheimer to City of Lebanon PD)Hiring of three part-time firefighters/EMTsNew part-time planning and zoning director position creationAmerican Legion donation to canine programCommunity recognition of service department snow removal effortsHomearama event coming to Goshen Township at Reserves at Stone Pillars FarmClermont County Township Association meeting Thursday at Batavia Township2025 cleanup day scheduled for June 7

Decisions (24)

  • Adopted the agenda for the meeting
  • Approved payment of bills for December 17, 2024 through January 14, 2025
  • Nominated and elected Trustee Lisa Allen as 2025 Board of Trustees chairperson
  • Nominated and elected Trustee Bob Hauserman as 2025 vice chair
  • Adopted the 2025 board of trustees regular meeting schedule (second and fourth Tuesdays at 6 PM)
  • Set 2025 standard mileage rate at 70 cents per business mile per IRS guidelines
  • Approved Resolution R-001-2025 authorizing sale of surplus items via govdeals.com
  • Set community forum rules with five-minute time limit before agenda items
  • Directed Administrator Rose to attend Sunshine Law certified training on behalf of elected officials
  • Set 2025 zoning commission and board of zoning appeals member compensation at $50 per month, not to exceed $599 annually
  • Adopted 2025 zoning and service department fee schedules with no changes from 2024
  • Set trick-or-treat date to be determined by Clermont County Township Association
  • Scheduled Goshen cleanup day for Saturday, June 7, 2025
  • Accepted resignation of Officer James Beckelheimer effective January 11, 2025
  • Approved Officer McAllister to attend subject control instructor training ($1,000 registration)
  • Approved SROs Alcorn and Groves to attend SRO conference in Sandusky, Ohio July 7-9, 2025 ($750 per officer)
  • Accepted $2,500 donation from American Legion Post 256 for canine program
  • Adjourned into executive session at 6:41 PM per ORC 121.22 G1
  • Accepted donation of six $25 Texas Roadhouse gift cards from Haas Homes and Bailey Families to service department
  • Approved job description and advertisement for part-time planning and zoning director
  • Approved hire of part-time firefighter paramedic Nathan at $22.50 per hour pending pre-employment screening
  • Approved hire of part-time firefighter EMT Grant Brown at $17.50 per hour pending pre-employment screening
  • Approved hire of part-time firefighter EMT Will Pisani at $17.50 per hour pending pre-employment screening
  • Adjourned at 7:44 PM

Action Items (6)

  • Administrator Bob Rose to investigate electrical aggregation program, consulting with Loveland and Miami Township administrators
  • Administrator Rose to attend Sunshine Law certified training on behalf of elected officials
  • Service Director Chad Meadows and team to develop plan for cleanup day location for June 7, 2025
  • Officer McAllister to attend subject control instructor training January 27 - February 14
  • Advertise for part-time planning and zoning director position
  • Trustee Hauserman to discuss electrical aggregation process at Thursday Township Association meeting

Financials

  • $67,350.49 estimated cost for two snowstorms (144 overtime hours, 115 straight hours, 420 gallons fuel, 308 tons of salt, 2,045 miles)
  • $90 per ton for salt
  • 70 cents per mile 2025 IRS mileage rate
  • $50 per month compensation for zoning commission and BZA members, not to exceed $599 annually
  • $1,000 registration for subject control instructor training
  • $750 per officer for SRO conference registration
  • $2,500 donation from American Legion Post 256 for canine program
  • $150 in Texas Roadhouse gift cards donated to service department (six $25 cards)
  • $831,940 for paving projects completed fall 2023 (70%+ grant funded)
  • $1.7 million received from Federal American Rescue Plan (ARPA) in 2022
  • Nearly $7 million in federal, state, and county funds secured over past two years
  • $60,000+ saved through renegotiated contracts (healthcare, IT, internet, payroll, unemployment)
  • $97,000 additional interest revenue from new interest-earning savings account (18,000% increase)
  • $22.50 per hour rate for part-time firefighter paramedic Nathan
  • $17.50 per hour rate for part-time firefighter EMTs Grant Brown and Will Pisani
  • Estimated $29.76 per month savings per household under electrical aggregation proposal
  • Estimated ~$200,000 per month community-wide savings under electrical aggregation

2025-01-07 Other routine
49m ▶ Vimeo

ZC 1-7-25

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on three zoning cases. They approved the HealthSource Addition Final Development Plan, approved Section 1 of Woodall Crossing Final Development Plan with conditions including privacy fences and garage setback modifications, and approved Section 3 of Cova-Barclay Woods Final Development Plan with conditions requiring Section 2 approval before permits and additional landscaping buffering. Discussion also covered upcoming zoning workshops and code review topics.

HealthSource Addition Final Development Plan approval including sidewalk additionsWoodall Crossing (formerly Reserves of Barclay Woods) Section 1 Final Development Plan with garage setback modification discussionCova-Barclay Woods Section 3 Final Development Plan and issue of Section 2 never receiving formal FDP approval despite homes being builtTraffic impact study and ODOT traffic signal application requirement for Woodall CrossingLandscaping and buffering requirements for residential developmentsHOA maintenance concerns raised regarding snow removal in Cova-Barclay Section 2Upcoming zoning code workshops including accessory dwelling units and garage standards

Decisions (4)

  • Approved video meeting minutes from November 12, 2024
  • Approved Final Development Plan for HealthSource Addition (ZC 2024-007)
  • Approved Final Development Plan for Woodall Crossing Section 1 (ZC 2023-007) with conditions including privacy fences, Board of Trustees stipulations from 12-12-23, and garage setback modification at staff discretion
  • Approved Final Development Plan for Cova-Barclay Woods Section 3 (ZC 2022-003) with conditions: no permits until Section 2 FDP approved, additional landscaping for 100% screening along lots 113-134, planting list per Section 10.04

Action Items (6)

  • Cova-Barclay developer to submit Section 2 Final Development Plan by early February for March Zoning Commission meeting
  • Cova-Barclay developer to update Section 3 plan with additional landscaping for near 100% screening in buffer area along lots 113-134
  • Alyssa (staff) to send deadline date for March meeting submissions to applicant
  • Christy Norman to relay HOA snow removal/maintenance concerns from Section 2 residents to developer
  • Staff to begin planning zoning workshops for late February/March timeframe
  • Board members to provide recommendations on zoning code topics for workshops

Financials

  • 54 buildable lots on 10 acres for Woodall Crossing Section 1
  • 42 buildable lots for Cova-Barclay Woods Section 3
  • 7,625 sq ft minimum lot area for Woodall Crossing Section 1
  • 8,520 sq ft minimum lot area for Cova-Barclay Section 3
  • 1,400 sq ft minimum floor area for Cova-Barclay homes
  • 20-foot perimeter buffer area required for Cova-Barclay Section 3
  • 85 lots allowed before ODOT traffic signal application required for Woodall Crossing

2024-12-17 Regular Meeting routine
21m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 12-17-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering end-of-year business including budget amendments, new hires, and grant acceptance. A new service department building was celebrated as recently completed, an ARPA sub-recipient agreement was approved for cleanup of blighted properties on State Route 28, and a traffic enforcement grant was accepted due to rising fatal crashes. Several full-time firefighter/paramedic hires were approved, and the organizational meeting for 2025 was scheduled.

New service department building completed and in useARPA sub-recipient agreement for blighted commercial property cleanup on State Route 28Fatal crash statistics and 2025 summer traffic enforcement grantAmbulance crash restitution from uninsured at-fault driverResignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Tyler MartinHiring of three full-time firefighter/paramedics and one volunteer for fire schoolStation 18 construction updateCP Triple A tree lighting fundraiser and Shop with a Cop recapServices department logo/insignia creation2025 organizational meeting scheduledLighting district budget shortfall and 2025 fixTax levy certification resolution R-016-2024

Decisions (18)

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills from November 20 through December 17, 2024
  • Amended the 2025 Fire EMS budget with a net $17,000 positive change
  • Entered into ARPA sub-recipient agreement with Clermont County Land Bank for $50,000 contribution toward State Route 28 property cleanup
  • Set the organizational meeting for January 14, 2025 at 6pm
  • Approved request for advance of taxes collected by Clermont County Auditor for 2025
  • Increased appropriations in lighting district account by $1,301.99
  • Approved seeking deductible restitution from Jose Luis Lopez Diaz related to ambulance crash
  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Tyler Martin effective December 6, 2024
  • Accepted $200 donation from Garnett Boyd to Goshen Fire and EMS
  • Accepted 2025 summer traffic enforcement grant from Ohio Traffic Safety Office for $26,410.99
  • Entered executive session per ORC 121.22(G)(1) and (G)(4)
  • Adopted Resolution R-016-2024 accepting amounts and rates from Budget Commission and certifying tax levies
  • Hired full-time firefighter paramedic Jared Acres effective January 12, 2025
  • Hired full-time firefighter paramedic Ryan Petronio effective January 12, 2025
  • Hired full-time firefighter EMT Jared Burns effective January 12, 2025
  • Hired Luna Alvarez as a volunteer to be sponsored by Goshen Township in fire and EMS school
  • Adjourned meeting at 7:22 PM

Action Items (5)

  • Chief Myers to send email update on Station 18 after Thursday meeting with structural engineer
  • Chad and the services department to design a services department logo/insignia and check with Administrator Rose
  • Chad to get black gutters installed on new service building to match other buildings
  • Administrator Rose to send draft 2025 meeting dates for trustees to consider
  • Law firm to pursue deductible restitution from uninsured motorist Jose Luis Lopez Diaz

Financials

  • $17,000 net positive change to fire EMS budget amendment
  • $50,000 ARPA contribution to Clermont County Land Bank for State Route 28 cleanup
  • $450,000 contributed by land bank with brownfield grant for property cleanup
  • $1,301.99 increase in lighting district appropriations
  • $500 deductible to be sought as restitution from uninsured motorist (33.3% attorney fee)
  • $200 donation from Garnett Boyd to Goshen Fire and EMS
  • $26,410.99 summer traffic enforcement grant from Ohio Traffic Safety Office
  • Million dollars worth of trucks housed in new service building

2024-11-19 Regular Meeting routine
1h 37m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 11-19-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering two zoning cases, the 2025 budget adoption, and a personnel matter. The Board approved a PUD zoning amendment for a 107-lot expansion of Lakefield Place subdivision with conditions requiring an emergency secondary access road. A major modification for HealthSource's building expansion from 7,700 to 16,234 square feet was approved with conditions including sidewalks and landscaping improvements. The 2025 budget was adopted after department heads made cuts totaling over $200,000, with a fire department station vehicle added back in at $50,000. The Board also accepted the resignation of Officer Chris Davidson.

ZC-2024-006: Lakefield Place PUD expansion – 107 new lots, secondary emergency access road requirement, open space calculationsZC-2024-007: HealthSource building expansion from 7,700 to 16,234 sq ft with Appalachia grant funding, new pharmacy with public access2025 budget adoption with Township Budgeting 101 presentation explaining general fund, levies, and unencumbered fundsDepartment budget cuts and deferred capital purchases (skid steer, copier, station vehicle)Paving strategy and potential $1.2 million grant opportunity for $255,000 township investmentSidewalk and pedestrian connectivity along SR 28 corridorLoveland/Goshen Township addressing and branding concernsOfficer Chris Davidson resignation to Union Township Police DepartmentStation 18 permits still pending county approvalReference to tragedy in Bethel earlier that dayHolly Fair event November 23 with paradeSnack Pack Foundation mental health awareness gala

Decisions (7)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (Nov 15-19, 2024) – passed unanimously
  • ZC-2024-006: Motion to approve Lakefield Place PUD expansion (R1 to PUD, 107 lots) with conditions requiring emergency secondary access road and revised preliminary plan showing PUD zoning on entire parcel 112209H018 – passed unanimously
  • ZC-2024-007: Motion to approve HealthSource major modification with conditions including sidewalks along Charles Snyder Road north to SR 28 (stipulated by ODOT), sidewalk connecting Shade Drive to Woodland Stream, lighting fixture height of 27.6 feet approved, landscaping screening of parking from SR 28, and removal of existing pavement between parking and SR 28 – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve 2025 budget as presented with inclusion of $50,000 for fire department station vehicle – passed unanimously
  • Motion to accept resignation of Officer Chris Davidson effective November 30, 2024 – passed unanimously
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:37 p.m. – passed unanimously

Action Items (12)

  • Alyssa to verify combined open space percentage for Lakefield Place expansion including outlot
  • Lakefield Place developer (Joe Perugia) to adjust preliminary plan to include emergency secondary access road and show PUD zoning on entire parcel
  • Lakefield Place developer to complete updated traffic study required by Clermont County Engineer
  • HealthSource (James Broussard) to install sidewalk along Charles Snyder Road north toward SR 28 and connecting to Shade Drive/Woodland Stream subdivision
  • HealthSource to remove existing pavement between proposed parking and SR 28, replace with grass lawn
  • HealthSource to add landscaping screening of parking area from SR 28 per Article 10
  • James Broussard to ask VP of Marketing about including 'Goshen Township' in HealthSource signage/advertising
  • Administrator Rose to present budget true-up in January 2025
  • Chad Meadows (Service Director) to revisit skid steer purchase after first quarter true-up
  • Administrator Rose to revisit copier replacement after true-up, potentially third or fourth quarter 2025
  • Chief Rose to begin process of filling Officer Davidson's vacancy
  • Crosswalk discussion to be added to 2025 agenda topics

Financials

  • $50,000 added to fire department budget for station vehicle (pickup truck)
  • $157,000 approximate total budget deficit after adding fire vehicle (down from over $300,000)
  • $107,501.59 budget deficit before fire vehicle addition
  • $76,000 for skid steer and attachments (deferred to true-up)
  • $10,000 for copier replacement (deferred to true-up)
  • $255,000 proposed paving budget (up from $150,000) to leverage potential grant match for $1.2 million project
  • $50,000 resignation - Officer Davidson's position
  • $12,065 for new police cruiser (in budget)
  • $171,000 in fire department capital items
  • $2.5 million set aside for Station 18 construction
  • ~$5.47 million in general fund unencumbered (54.8% of revenue)
  • $88,000 spent to supplement mutual aid partners
  • $16,500 raised at Snack Pack Foundation gala
  • $8,000 cut from admin contracted services (from $88K to $82K)
  • $8,000 cut from police contracted services
  • $50,000 reduction in police salary estimates
  • 16,234 square feet proposed new size for HealthSource building (expanded from 7,700 sq ft)
  • 107 proposed lots for Lakefield Place expansion on 40.85 acres
  • $1,923,000 police department budget request for 2025
  • Over $2 million in police department unencumbered funds

2024-11-14 Special Meeting ceremonial
1h 18m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 11-14-24

Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting for regular business, featuring a comprehensive 2025 budget presentation by Administrator Rose covering all township departments. The board discussed HSA contribution increases for employees, approved several personnel actions, and celebrated recent successes including the passage of a fire levy and the Station 18 groundbreaking. An executive session was held to discuss employee compensation, after which the board approved 2025 non-union wages, a multi-year employment agreement for Bob Rose, and a one-time ARPA-funded bonus for department heads.

2025 budget presentation covering all township departmentsMatthew Hammond's proposed winery/wedding venue at 6550 Smith RoadFire levy passage celebration and Station 18 construction updateHSA employer contribution increase from $500/$1,000/$1,500 to $1,500/$2,500Supplemental insurance and EAP review - staying with current providersOPWC grant application for Belfast and Gibson road pavingT66 Bobcat skid steer purchase request for service departmentLighting district revenue correction and annual review processARPA funds exhaustion by end of 2024Opioid settlement funds strategy for future ambulance purchasePart-time firefighter paramedic retention challenges ($19/hr vs $25/hr competitors)MDC computer lease program for fire departmentInterest investment strategy generating significant returnsBob Rose multi-year employment agreement 2025-20292025 non-union wage approvalOne-time ARPA bonus for department headsLight Up Goshen Parade and Holly Fair November 23Clermont County Land Bank good news on blighted propertyCannabis revenue anticipated but not yet in state fiscal budgetChief Myers engagement congratulations

Decisions (14)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills from October 22 through November 14, 2024 - passed
  • Motion to increase HSA employer contributions to $1,500 single and $2,500 family effective first pay date January 2025, moving from 3 categories to 2 - passed
  • Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Chief Myers to attend FDIC in Indianapolis April 8-12, 2025 - passed
  • Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Matthew Lieber effective November 19, 2024 - passed
  • Motion to hire part-time firefighter paramedic Keaton Hall at $17.50/hour effective November 17, 2024 - passed
  • Motion to authorize Administrative Assistant Evan Corddry to assist zoning inspector - passed
  • Motion to approve township building duct cleaning by Stanley Steamer at $3,750 - passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:15 p.m. - passed
  • Motion to approve 2025 non-union wages - passed
  • Motion to adjust HR Director Melinda Eichenhorst's annual vacation accrual to 120 hours effective January 1, 2025 - passed
  • Motion to approve employment agreement with Bob Rose as Police Chief/Administrator/Public Safety Director for 2025-2029 - passed
  • Motion to approve use of remaining ARPA funds for one-time bonus to department heads - passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:23 p.m. - passed

Action Items (7)

  • Administrator Bob Rose to contact Matthew Hammond regarding his proposed winery/wedding venue project at 6550 Smith Road and relay individual trustee feedback
  • Department heads to review budgets and look for areas to trim to reduce the projected $300,000 deficit
  • Administrator Rose to prepare a breakout of capital expenditures separate from operating budgets for future budget discussions
  • Chad Meadows to coordinate putting up decorations before the Light Up Goshen Parade on November 23
  • Chief Myers to follow up with Clermont County Building Department on Station 18 building permits
  • Laura to submit lighting district evaluation to auditor annually every August going forward
  • Administrator Rose to provide detailed breakdown of contracted services line item in general fund

Financials

  • $4 million+ total starting unencumbered (excluding Station 18)
  • $9.8 million projected total revenue without Station 18
  • $10.1 million projected total expenses without Station 18
  • $2 million estimated project cost for Matthew Hammond's proposed venue
  • $543,000 general fund property tax revenue (up from $427,000 budgeted)
  • $71,000 interest earned in 2024 vs. $538 in 2013
  • $91,200 projected interest revenue for 2025 including $54,000 from Station 18 invested funds
  • $2.5 million received from state for Station 18, $1.5 million invested
  • $342,000 tornado mutual aid reimbursements sent out
  • $88,000 tornado expenses from township funds (25% local match)
  • $38,500 received in opioid settlement funds vs. $5,000 budgeted
  • $76,000 requested for T66 Bobcat skid steer with attachments

2024-11-12 Regular Meeting contentious
1h 20m ▶ Vimeo

ZC 11-12-24

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on three zoning cases. The Woodhall Crossing subdivision final development plan (ZC-2023-007) was tabled due to unresolved issues including incomplete preliminary requirements, fence placement discrepancies, traffic signal timing, and the developer's absence. The Lakefield Place PUD expansion (ZC-2024-006) was approved as a new PUD with conditions regarding a second entrance per Fire Department requirements. The Health Source Solutions major modification (ZC-2024-007) was approved with staff-recommended conditions for concrete removal, additional landscaping, and uniform lighting.

Woodhall Crossing final development plan Section 1 - tabled due to multiple unresolved issuesDeveloper absence and accountability concerns for Woodhall Crossing (Coach Development/Robert Gerwin)Traffic signal timing and vertical curb improvements on State Route 48 for Woodhall CrossingPrivacy fence and screening requirements along Cornwell properties not matching preliminary approvalConstruction activity occurring before final development plan approval concernsResident complaints about developer communication and HOA management in existing Coach Development subdivisionLakefield Place Phase 2 PUD expansion with 107 additional lotsFire Department requiring secondary emergency access road for Lakefield PlaceHealth Source Solutions building expansion funded by Appalachia Community Grant ProgramState Route 28 corridor plan compliance and landscaping screeningTree survey requirement from preliminary approval not fulfilledFuture concerns about buffering between residential and manufacturing properties (Phoenix Drive)

Decisions (5)

  • Motion to table Zoning Case 2023-007 (Woodhall Crossing final development plan Section 1) passed unanimously to allow clarification on outstanding issues
  • Motion to approve Zoning Case 2024-006 (Lakefield Place PUD expansion) as a new PUD with condition that second entrance/emergency access be addressed per Fire Department concerns passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve Zoning Case 2024-007 (Health Source Solutions major modification) with staff conditions (removal of concrete entrance, additional landscaping, uniform lighting) passed unanimously
  • December 3rd meeting canceled due to lack of agenda items
  • Approval of October 1, 2024 video meeting minutes passed unanimously

Action Items (11)

  • Max Merritt to share chairperson's questions with Woodhall Crossing applicant/developer
  • Woodhall Crossing developer to clarify fence placement along Cornwell properties to match Board of Trustees preliminary approval conditions
  • Woodhall Crossing developer to address tree survey requirement from preliminary approval
  • Woodhall Crossing developer to clarify timeline for traffic signal installation and vertical curb improvements on State Route 48
  • Woodhall Crossing developer to provide floor plans meeting Section 6.02 C3C requirements
  • Lakefield Place developer to address secondary emergency access road requirement from Fire Department

  • Health Source Solutions to add landscaping to screen parking area from State Route 28 per corridor plan
  • Health Source Solutions to discuss cross-access easement with Clermont County engineers
  • January meeting to be scheduled after Board of Trustees completes reorganization and 2025 schedule

Financials

  • Woodhall Crossing: 5 acres of open space, 27% of site
  • Woodhall Crossing: 180-foot vinyl privacy fence proposed along Cornwell properties
  • Woodhall Crossing: Traffic signal required before 132nd home is occupied per ODOT
  • Woodhall Crossing: 43 of 54 lots less than 8,000 square feet
  • Lakefield Place expansion: 107 lots proposed on 40+ acres
  • Lakefield Place: 2.62 units per acre density
  • Lakefield Place: 13 acres (32%) open space, slightly below 35% required
  • Health Source Solutions: Building expansion from 7,700 sq ft to 16,234 sq ft
  • Health Source Solutions: 98 parking spaces proposed, 81 required
  • Health Source Solutions: Approximately 10,000 sq ft of existing leftover pavement to be removed

2024-10-22 Regular Meeting ceremonial
1h 5m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 10-22-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring extensive ceremonial swearing-in of new firefighters, police officers, and promotions, as well as multiple life-saving awards presented to police and fire personnel. The board approved a guaranteed maximum price construction agreement with Conger Construction for Fire Station 18, which is under budget with no local levy tax dollars being used. Other business included hiring a part-time firefighter EMT, accepting a detective's resignation to part-time status, approving travel to the Ohio Township Association Winter Conference, and supporting the annual Light Up Goshen parade.

Swearing-in of new firefighters: Alyssa Ladd, Shailer Newman, Carly Hargis, Jack PegramPromotion of Dustin Englund to Lieutenant, John Fiela to Captain, Kevin Lynch to Deputy ChiefSwearing-in of new police officers Gary Moen and Wesley LyonsPromotion of Kyle Howell to Police SergeantLife-saving awards for Officers Tyler Smith and Jamie McFarland (armed man de-escalation incident)Life-saving award for Sergeant Cody Collier (motorcycle crash tourniquet application on SR 28)Life-saving awards for multi-agency team response to cardiac event on Woodville PikeCertificate of Appreciation for Chaplain Aaron EllermannFire Station 18 construction update – under budget, groundbreaking imminentGMP agreement with Conger Construction for Station 18Homorama community partner recognitionHeavy rescue training event successDetective Steve Maynard transitioning to part-time retirement roleLight Up Goshen parade supportOhio Township Association Winter Conference travel approvalZoning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals reappointmentsDisciplinary action against firefighter paramedic Gabe Grooms

Decisions (12)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (October 10–22, 2024) – passed
  • Motion to enter into GMP agreement with Conger Construction for Station 18 construction – passed
  • Motion to hire part-time firefighter EMT Tyler Harding at $16/hour effective November 3, 2024 – passed
  • Motion to accept full-time resignation of Detective Steve Maynard effective January 3, 2025 and appoint him to part-time police detective position – passed
  • Motion to approve out-of-town travel for OTA Winter Conference (1/29/25–1/31/25 in Columbus) – passed
  • Motion to authorize township resources for Light Up Goshen parade on November 23 – passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:29 PM per ORC 121.22G1 – passed
  • Motion to reappoint Dave Curie to Zoning Commission with term expiring December 31, 2029 – passed
  • Motion to reappoint Gary Theodore to Board of Zoning Appeals – passed
  • Motion to suspend full-time firefighter paramedic Gabe Grooms for two 24-hour shift days (48 hours) without pay per CBA Article 25 – passed
  • Motion to adjourn – passed

Action Items (4)

  • HR Eckenhorst to finalize supplemental benefit package for the board, possibly by next meeting
  • Administrator Rose to schedule OTA Winter Conference travel for seven or more staff and elected officials
  • Police department to coordinate traffic management and vehicle participation for Light Up Goshen parade on November 23
  • Determine Detective Maynard's part-time wage when 2025 non-union salaries are approved

Financials

  • $2.5 million from state capital fund for Station 18 construction
  • 75% of remaining Station 18 costs covered by Ohio Emergency Management Agency
  • No local levy tax dollars used for Station 18 construction
  • $16/hour wage for part-time firefighter EMT Tyler Harding
  • 48 hours without pay suspension for firefighter paramedic Gabe Grooms
  • 105 hours worked by staff during Homorama event
