2024-10-08 Regular Meeting urgent
44m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 10-8-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on October 8, 2024. A major portion of the meeting featured a presentation by Steve Pegram, treasurer of the Goshen Fire and EMS 2024 levy campaign, explaining the critical importance of the replacement levy on the November 5th ballot, which funds over 60% of fire and EMS operations. The board approved multiple donations for fire training exercises, accepted food gift cards from Mount Carmel Christian Church, approved the purchase of 10 AEDs using remaining ARPA funds, renewed property and risk management insurance with Highland, and entered executive session regarding personnel matters.

Fire and EMS replacement levy presentation for November 5, 2024 electionStation 18 reconstruction progress update – building permit pending, steel orderedCatastrophic consequences if levy fails: 35+ layoffs, staffing from ~50 to 14, station closureExtrication training event scheduled October 17-19 using donated vehiclesPurchase of 10 AEDs for township vehicles and facilities using ARPA fundsARPA fund amendments returning unused appropriationsPolice officer Shane Young resignation before startingIndigent burial approval for Jack Thomas SullivanInsurance renewal with Highland for 2024-2025Flags for Heroes display on township property approved for Lions ClubHomecoming parade rescheduled to Friday; trick-or-treat October 31, 6-8 PMTrunk or treat partnership with Clermont County Park District at Stagamore ParkAdministration building lobby floor renovation completedLeave of absence granted for part-time firefighter EMT Seth Daniels

Decisions (21)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (Sept 10 – Oct 8, 2024) – passed
  • Motion to accept donation of $180 in food gift cards from Mount Carmel Christian Church – passed
  • Motion to enter into memorandum of understanding amending contract with Medi-Count Management for third-party Medicare/Medicaid EMS billing – passed
  • Motion to accept $750 donation from Goshen Lions Club for fire prevention materials – passed
  • Motion to accept $750 donation from Goshen Chamber of Commerce for fire prevention materials – passed
  • Motion to accept donation of semi trailer from R&L Trucking and approve disposal via scrap after training – passed
  • Motion to accept donation of semi tractor from City Servicing and approve disposal via scrap after training – passed
  • Motion to accept donation of multiple cars from Phipps Towing, Dakota's Towing, After Warranty Auto Repair, and John's Auto and approve disposal via scrap after training – passed
  • Motion to approve indigent burial of Jack Thomas Sullivan with Evans Funeral Home – passed
  • Motion to approve renewal of Ohio Plan property and risk management insurance with Highland for 2024-2025 – passed
  • Motion to accept resignation of Shane Young effective 9/23/24 – passed
  • Motion to amend ARPA appropriations for Goshen Cleanup Day from $13,500 to $10,309.18 – passed
  • Motion to amend ARPA appropriations for admin chairs returning unused funds – passed
  • Motion to amend ARPA appropriations for administration parking lot and sidewalks returning unused funds – passed
  • Motion to appropriate $20,222.48 into ARPA fund for purchase of AEDs in township vehicles – passed
  • Motion to approve purchase of 10 AED machines from Stryker for $20,222.48 from ARPA fund – passed
  • Motion to approve Flags for Heroes on township property for Goshen Lions Club – passed
  • Motion to enter executive session at 6:43 PM regarding personnel matters – passed
  • Motion to grant part-time firefighter EMT Seth Daniels a six-month leave of absence – passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:10 PM – passed

Action Items (6)

  • Post banners on Route 28 clarifying that the fire station construction is not funded by the levy
  • Levy committee to address homestead exemption question on Facebook
  • Administrator Rose to find projects for remaining ~$3,490 in ARPA funds before end of year
  • Levy committee to distribute flyers and candy about the levy on Halloween
  • Historical society funding request to be discussed during November 2025 budget discussions using general funds

Financials

  • $180 in food gift cards donated by Mount Carmel Christian Church ($50 to police, remainder to fire)
  • $750 donation from Goshen Lions Club for fire prevention materials
  • $750 donation from Goshen Chamber of Commerce for fire prevention materials
  • $20,222.48 for purchase of 10 AED machines from Stryker using ARPA funds
  • $1,738.41 approximate cost per standard AED unit
  • Insurance premium increase of $17,563 (~14.6% increase) for Highland property and risk management renewal
  • $196,000 average home value in Goshen Township per Clermont County Auditor
  • $106.11 per year additional cost for average $200,000 home for fire/EMS levy
  • 4.4 mil replacement fire and EMS levy on November 5 ballot
  • ~$8 million new firehouse funded 100% by insurance and state aid, zero local tax dollars

2024-10-01 Special Meeting contentious
55m ▶ Vimeo

ZC 10-01-2024

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on zoning case ZC 2023-007 regarding the final development plan for Phase 1 of Woodhall Crossing subdivision. The developer was not present and had requested a continuance because several stipulations from the preliminary approval in December 2023 were still unmet, including tree preservation evidence, building elevations/materials, and complete landscape plans. Residents from both the proposed development area and the adjacent Barkley Woods subdivision voiced concerns about traffic safety on State Route 48, tree clearing, dust, HOA mismanagement by developer Robert, and code enforcement issues. The commission voted to continue the hearing to November 12, 2024.

Woodhall Crossing subdivision final development plan review and continuanceTraffic impact study approval from ODOT and traffic signal requirements at SR 28 and SR 48Site distance concerns and hill cutting evaluation on State Route 48Tree clearing and dust issues on the development propertyScreening and mounding requirements for adjacent M1/M2 commercial propertiesBarkley Woods HOA financial transparency and mismanagement complaints against developer RobertCode enforcement issues in Barkley Woods including business operations from homes, parking violations, and HOA non-complianceRoad paving and dedication timeline for new subdivisionsUpcoming major modifications for Lakefield Place and Health Source in November

Decisions (3)

  • Approved minutes from previous hearing on June 4, 2024
  • Approved continuance of zoning case ZC 2023-007 public hearing to November 12, 2024 at 6:30 PM
  • Approved motion to adjourn

Action Items (9)

  • Developer must provide evidence of trees to be preserved on the Woodhall Crossing property
  • Developer must provide building information including elevations and construction materials for final development plan
  • Developer must complete landscape plan details for screening and entrance
  • Developer must submit HOA documents for Woodhall Crossing
  • Developer must provide a development timeline for each phase of the project
  • Staff to provide traffic impact study to residents upon request
  • Staff to clarify whether traffic signal requirement should be tied to a timeline rather than just 132-home threshold
  • Max Merritt to send materials for two major modifications (Lakefield Place and Health Source) to board members for November meeting
  • Staff to confirm number of phases in Barkley Woods/Colville Barkley development and provide info to resident via email

Financials

  • $250 per home paid at closing for Barkley Woods HOA emergency reservation fund (23 homes)
  • $106 credit mentioned by one Barkley Woods resident
  • $20 fee required for improvement approvals (deck, fence) in Barkley Woods
  • 132 homes is the threshold triggering traffic signal requirement at SR 28 and SR 48
  • 250-foot minimum left turn lane required on State Route 48

2024-09-10 Regular Meeting routine
1h 3m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 9-10-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of business including approval of a police sidearms upgrade to 9mm with red dot sights funded by ARPA, acceptance of multiple fire department resignations, approval of a new HSA vendor, and a 0% increase renewal on healthcare/dental/vision benefits for 2025. The board also approved flooring replacement in the administration building, accepted vehicle and equipment donations for fire training, made several police and fire department promotions and reassignments, and discussed spending remaining ARPA funds before year-end.

2024-08-19 Other routine
32m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 8-19-24

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2024-004, an area variance request for property at 2588 McHenry Road. The applicant, Mr. Helton, sought to reduce the required side yard setback from 10 feet to 5 feet in order to build a new home (two bedrooms and a two-car garage) on his R1-zoned property. Discussion centered on whether the new structure would be attached or detached from an existing workshop/garage structure, with the board advising the applicant that a detached structure would require a new single-family home permit. The board discussed conditions including limiting the structure to no more than 60 feet in length to maintain setback compliance, and the variance was considered for approval.

Area variance request BZA 2024-004 for reduced side yard setback at 2588 McHenry RoadDiscussion of whether new structure should be attached or detached from existing buildingDiscrepancies between submitted site plan and floor plan dimensionsExisting structure not meeting minimum 1,400 sq ft residential standardAccessory structure rules for properties over two acres in residential zoningRecruitment of alternates for BZA and Zoning Commission boards

Decisions (3)

  • Approval of previous meeting minutes from July 15, 2024 (passed with one abstention from Mr. Segal)
  • Area variance BZA 2024-004 to reduce side yard setback from 10 feet to 5 feet for property at 2588 McHenry Road appears to have been granted with condition that structure not exceed 60 feet in length
  • Motion to adjourn passed

Action Items (3)

  • Max Merritt (Zoning Consultant) to review final submitted plans to ensure compliance with 5-foot side yard and 10-foot opposite side yard setbacks and 60-foot length maximum
  • Township zoning staff to process applicant's permit - either as building addition ($200) or new home ($750) depending on whether structure is attached or detached
  • Board members to recruit two alternates for BZA and Zoning Commission

Financials

  • $200 building addition permit fee
  • $750 new home permit fee
  • $550 difference between addition and new home permit
  • 1,400 square feet minimum floor area required for single-family dwelling
  • 723 square foot two-car garage proposed in updated plan
  • 2,632 total square feet proposed (attached version)
  • 5-foot proposed side yard setback (reduced from 10-foot requirement)
  • 75-foot lot width
  • 60-foot maximum structure length discussed as condition

2024-08-13 Regular Meeting routine
1h 12m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 8-13-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering the Station 18 fire station replacement project, allocation of remaining ARPA funds for various township improvements, personnel actions including promotions and hires, and discussion of potential state legislation threatening host community cannabis fund revenues. MSP Architects and Conger Construction presented the design development budget for the new Station 18, and the board authorized demolition of the old station and early material procurement.

Station 18 fire station design presentation by MSP Architects and Conger ConstructionStation 18 design development budget review and GMP amendment for demolitionARPA fund remaining balance allocation and spending planDeerfield Road/Woodville Pike roundabout partnership with Miami Township and countyService department outbuilding roof replacementPurchase of 2023 Ford F-550 plow spreader truckHost community cannabis fund revenue and threatened state legislationPersonnel promotions: three part-time firefighter EMTs to full-timeNew hire of part-time firefighter EMT Jacob MilesAppointment of Evan Cordray as part-time administrative assistantCommunity member Gary Theodore's suggestion for business recruitment committeeTragic events in Goshen community including fatal auto accident and deathsAdmin building flooring and chair replacement plansCancellation of August 27 regular meetingHomorama event beginning August 24

Special BOT 7-19-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting to correct a resolution related to a fire and EMS tax levy. The board rescinded resolution R-013-2024, which contained typos identified by both township staff and the prosecutor's office, and adopted a corrected replacement resolution R-015-2024 for a 4.4 mil fire and emergency services tax levy.

Rescinding and replacing fire/EMS levy resolution due to typos4.4 mil fire and emergency services tax levy

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Rescinded resolution R-013-2024 (4.4 mil fire/EMS levy resolution with typos, originally adopted July 16, 2024)
  • Approved resolution R-015-2024 (corrected 4.4 mil fire and emergency services tax levy resolution to proceed)
  • Adjourned the special meeting

Action Items (0)

  • None recorded

Financials

  • 4.4 mil fire and emergency services tax levy

BOT 7-16-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering HR policy updates, fire department matters including a fire levy resolution, Station 18 construction updates, and road maintenance agreements. Key actions included approving HR policy revisions for EEO compliance, authorizing a 4.4 mil fire/EMS tax levy for the ballot, approving a memorandum of understanding with the firefighters union, and entering agreements for road paving grants and road salt purchases.

HR policy revisions for EEO, ADA, and Pregnancy Worker Fairness Act complianceStation 18 design development completion and upcoming hard bid process4.4 mil fire and EMS tax levy for ballotMemorandum of understanding with IAFF Local 3932 regarding paramedic staffingDonation of surplus firefighter gear rack to Monroe Township Fire DepartmentOPWC grant pursuit for Gibson Road and Belfast Road pavingRoad salt purchase through SWAP 4G program with Cargill SaltCPAAA fundraiser at Sprinkles and National Night Out Stuff the Cruiser eventEmployee retention grant awarded

  • Approved minutes through July 15, 2024
  • Approved HR policy additions and revisions for EEO compliance
  • Accepted resignation of part-time fitness trainer Paul Hilliard effective July 12
  • Approved Resolution R-012-2024 transferring $47,239.85 from Levy Fund 2193 to Firehouse Construction Fund 4901
  • Approved Resolution R-013-2024 to proceed with a 4.4 mil fire and EMS tax levy
  • Approved memorandum of understanding with IAFF Local 3932
  • Approved Resolution R-014-2024 declaring firefighter gear rack as surplus and authorizing donation to Monroe Township Fire Department
  • Authorized Township Administrator to enter agreement with Choice One Engineering for OPWC grant for Gibson Road and Belfast Road paving in 2025
  • Authorized Township Administrator to enter agreement with Cargill Salt for road salt through SWAP 4G program
  • Meeting adjourned at 6:18 PM

Action Items (6)

  • Chief Myers to bring motion to accept $41,761 employee retention grant at next meeting
  • Chief Myers to coordinate Congre and MSP presentation for August 13 trustees meeting on Station 18
  • Resolution R-013-2024 fire levy to be submitted to Board of Elections by next day (ahead of August 7 deadline)

2024-07-15 Other routine
18m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 7-15-24

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2024-003, a request for an area variance to allow construction of a new single-family home at 155 square feet less than the 1,400 square foot minimum required by the R3 zoning district. The applicant, Robin Fleming, sought to replace a fire-damaged home on Parker Road with a 1,245 square foot three-bedroom, two-bathroom ranch. Staff recommended approval noting the variance was only 1% beyond the administrative approval threshold and consistent with surrounding neighborhood character. The board unanimously approved the variance.

Area variance request for undersized single-family home (BZA 2024-003)Replacement of fire-damaged home on Parker RoadNeighborhood character and compatibility with surrounding smaller homesProperty maintenance violation on the subject property since the fireProposed home setbacks and site placement

Decisions (3)

  • Approved video minutes from the June 5, 2024 meeting (5-0)
  • Approved area variance BZA 2024-003 for a 155 square foot reduction from the 1,400 sq ft minimum for a new single-family dwelling (5-0)
  • Motion to adjourn at 6:47 PM (5-0)

Action Items (2)

  • Robin Fleming to coordinate with zoning staff on next steps to move forward with construction
  • Zoning staff to accept new applications for the next meeting by August 1st deadline

Financials

  • 1,400 square feet: minimum floor area required for single-family homes in R3 district
  • 1,245 square feet: proposed home size
  • 155 square feet: variance reduction requested (11% reduction from requirement)
  • 930 square feet: existing damaged ranch home on the property
  • 10%: administrative approval threshold for deviance from standards

2024-06-17 Special Meeting routine
16m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Special Meeting 6-17-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting primarily to discuss and approve a resolution of necessity for a 4.4 mill replacement fire and EMS levy to appear on the ballot. Chief Myers presented financial projections showing various mill rates and recommended 4.4 mills as a compromise that maintains staffing and safety while easing the tax burden on residents. The board also approved a $40,000 increase in fire apparatus maintenance appropriations due to unexpected vehicle repairs, and celebrated the start of a long-awaited environmental cleanup on Parker Road.

Fire and EMS replacement levy discussion and resolution of necessity (4.4 mills)Fire apparatus maintenance budget overrun due to catastrophic vehicle repairsOhio EPA Combs cleanup on Parker Road beginning after approximately 16 yearsStation 18 progress update pending capital billHomestead exemption consideration in deciding not to combine existing leviesFire staffing levels unchanged since 2003 despite anticipated township growth

Decisions (4)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve payment of bills for June 12–17, 2024 – passed unanimously
  • Motion to increase appropriations in repairs and maintenance (2111-220-323-0000) by $40,000 for fire apparatus repairs – passed unanimously
  • Resolution R-011-2024 approved: resolution of necessity to levy a 4.4 mill replacement tax for fire and emergency medical services – passed unanimously

Action Items (4)

  • Chief Myers to continue pursuing grants to offset capital cuts resulting from the 4.4 mill levy instead of 4.5
  • Chief Myers to continue progress on Station 18 pending capital bill approval
  • Board and community committee (Friends of the Fire Department) to develop a positive campaign strategy for the fire/EMS levy election
  • Director Meadows' team completed Gateway sign landscaping (acknowledged as done)

Financials

  • $40,000 increase in fire apparatus repairs and maintenance appropriations
  • $22,000 repair bill for 2015 engine rear end replacement
  • $10,000 in repairs for 2020 engines (brakes and related work)
  • 4.4 mill replacement levy proposed (down from current 4.5 mills)
  • 4.5 mill levy represents approximately 40% of the fire/EMS overall budget
  • $1.388 million projected shortfall after 5 years with a 4.5 mill renewal (not replacement)
  • $329,000 projected unencumbered funds after 5 years with 4.4 mill replacement
  • $564,000 projected unencumbered funds after 5 years with 4.5 mill replacement
  • $115,000 per payroll for fire/EMS department
  • $400,000–$500,000 needed in reserves to cover bills until first tax collection in April
  • $2 million recommended reserve by auditor's office (six months)
  • $86,627 projected unencumbered funds after 5 years with 4.3 mill replacement
  • ~$600,000 projected deficit after 5 years with 4.0 mill replacement
Trustee Bob HowlandTrustee Lisa AllenTrustee HodgesChief Myers (Fire Chief)Director Meadows (Service Department)Chad (Service/Roads representative)Melinda (Fiscal Officer/Clerk)Brian Shrive (Prosecutor's Office, mentioned)

BOT 6-11-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering zoning text amendments for shipping containers, acceptance of an AED donation, scheduling a special meeting for fire levy discussion, and approval of ARPA funds for construction of a pole barn at the service department. The board also approved a replat of six lots into one to facilitate the building permit, declared surplus items for sale, and entered executive session regarding personnel matters.

  • Motion to approve Resolution OR-10-2024 replat of lots 1-6 of Town of Goshen creating lot 1A – passed
  • Motion to appropriate $74,950 into ARPA service department account for pole barn construction – passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:31 PM – passed
  • Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 6:56 PM – passed

  • Advertise the special meeting on June 17 at 7:30 PM and the cancellation of the June 25 meeting
  • Trustee Allen and Administrator Rose to attend TIRC meeting on August 7 from 1-3 PM
  • Chief to prepare fire levy resolution of necessity for the June 17 special meeting
  • Resolution to proceed for fire levy scheduled for July 16 meeting
  • Service department to proceed with pole barn construction using Mullins Maintenance and WES Electric

  • Chief Myers to continue work with Conger and architect on fire station estimate (expected in approximately 4 weeks)

  • $48,000.20 increased appropriation for police body worn camera contract renewal

  • $37,570 estimated cost for the pole barn building

BZA 6-5-24

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a regular meeting to administer the oath of office to new member John Purcell, approve previous meeting minutes from April 15, 2024, and finalize findings of fact and decisions on three related BZA cases from 2022. The board denied the applicant's administrative appeal and variance requests in cases BZA 2022-011, 012, and 013, ordering that automotive repair heavy use of the property cease immediately.

Oath of office administered to John Purcell as new BZA memberFinalization of findings of fact and conclusions of law for BZA 2022-011, 012, and 013Denial of administrative appeal and variance for automotive repair heavy use of propertyOrder for automotive repair heavy use to cease immediatelyCancellation of June 17 regular meeting

  • Approved decision on BZA 2022-013 denying variance and ordering automotive repair heavy use to cease immediately (5-0, Purcell abstained)
  • June 17 regular meeting canceled

2024-06-04 Regular Meeting routine
11m ▶ Vimeo

ZC 6-4-24

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a brief meeting to consider text amendments related to storage/shipping containers and accessory structure definitions (Zoning Case 2024-005). The amendments, initiated by the trustees and already recommended for approval by Clermont County, clarify the permitted and accessory use tables, add shipping containers as a permitted accessory use with specific standards, and clean up redundant definitions. The commission unanimously recommended approval of all proposed text amendments to the Board of Trustees.

Text amendments to Goshen Township Zoning Resolution regarding shipping containers as accessory structuresClarification that unlisted uses in permitted and accessory use tables are prohibitedShipping container standards: hard surface foundation, solid color paint consistent with principal structure, no advertisements or signageCleanup of redundant definitions for accessory buildings, structures, and uses in Section 16.03Addition of shipping container definition to zoning resolution

Decisions (3)

  • Approved video meeting minutes from April 2, 2024
  • Motion to forgo chairman's statement passed (no audience or persons to be sworn in)
  • Motion to recommend approval of Zoning Case 2024-005 text amendments related to storage containers and accessory structures passed unanimously

Action Items (2)

  • Staff to forward Zoning Commission recommendation of approval for Zoning Case 2024-005 to the Board of Trustees
  • Staff to confirm whether a Zoning Commission meeting will be held on July 2nd (to be determined by next day)

Financials

  • 1 shipping container allowed on lots 1 acre or smaller
  • 2 shipping containers allowed on lots between 1 and 5 acres
  • 3 shipping containers allowed on lots 5 acres or greater

BOT 5-28-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on May 28, 2024, with two trustees present. Key actions included approving out-of-town travel for school resource officers, promoting Division Chief Kevin Lynch to deputy chief of the fire department, selecting Conger Construction as the construction manager for the demolition and reconstruction of Station 18, appointing a new full-time police officer, and setting the deputy fire chief salary. The board also recognized the success of the Memorial Day parade and service and announced an upcoming community cleanup day.

Memorial Day parade and service recognitionCommunity cleanup day scheduled for Saturday 8 a.m. to noonNew medic unit delivery expected late June/early July (replacement for unit totaled last July)SRO training travel approval (grant funded, no cost to township)Promotion of Kevin Lynch to Deputy Fire ChiefSelection of Conger Construction for Station 18 demolition and reconstructionHiring of new police officer Robert Lyons to fill one of two vacanciesProperty maintenance code update in progressJoe Reynolds returned to full staff after gallbladder surgery

Decisions (9)

  • Adopted the agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for May 15–28, 2024
  • Approved out-of-town travel for SRO Groves and SRO Alcorn to attend NASRO training in Phoenix, AZ, July 14–19, at no cost (grant funded)
  • Promoted Division Chief Kevin Lynch to Deputy Chief effective June 3, 2024
  • Selected Conger Construction as construction manager for demolition and reconstruction of Station 18 and approved the associated contract
  • Adjourned into executive session at 6:09 p.m. per ORC 121.22 G1
  • Appointed Robert Lyons as full-time police officer effective June 17, 2024, with salary commensurate with experience
  • Set deputy fire chief salary at $93,691.20
  • Adjourned the regular meeting at 6:24 p.m.

Action Items (4)

  • Alyssa to finalize new property maintenance code contract based on Julia's markup
  • Chad and service department to prepare for community cleanup day on Saturday
  • Chief Myers to proceed with Conger Construction on Station 18 demolition and reconstruction
  • Chief Rose to onboard Robert Lyons as full-time police officer effective June 17, 2024

Financials

  • $93,691.20 — Deputy Fire Chief salary

BOT 5-14-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering personnel changes, new employee policies, infrastructure improvements, and several appointments. The board accepted three fire department resignations due to competitive full-time job offers elsewhere, adopted revised part-time PTO and exempt accrual time policies, approved resolutions for credit card payment acceptance and fund transfers for fire station construction, renewed a five-year body camera contract largely covered by a grant, approved ARPA-funded concrete and parking lot improvements, and made several appointments following an executive session.

Police Memorial Week and remembrance of the Durbin familyFire department staffing losses due to competitive full-time job marketRevised part-time PTO leave policy and new exempt accrual time policyCredit card payment acceptance for township servicesFund transfer for fire station construction accountingCPAAA gift card donationSurplus firearm disposal from court forfeitureAxon body camera renewal with grant fundingARPA-funded building and parking lot maintenance projectsBoard of Zoning Appeals alternate appointment (John Purcell)Volunteer zoning consultant appointment (Kevin Maloff)Intern promotion (Evan Cordray)Upcoming Memorial Day ceremony and Cleanup Day June 1Chamber dinner and police officer recognition for Officer Kyle HowellStation 18 contract pending insurance reviewMedic unit expected back mid-June to mid-July after liquid spring parts repair

  • Approved payment of bills from April 24 to May 14, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Jacob Myrick effective May 11, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Rylan Childs effective May 19, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of full-time firefighter paramedic Tim Harrison, changing to part-time at $19.50/hour effective May 27, 2024
  • Adopted revisions to part-time PTO leave policy
  • Adopted exempt accrual time policy for salaried employees
  • Approved Resolution R-008-2024 authorizing acceptance of payments by financial transaction devices (credit cards)
  • Approved Resolution R-009-2024 for transfer of funds between funds ($2,193 into account 4901 for fire station construction)
  • Accepted $800 gift card donation from CPAAA for police, fire, service, and administration departments
  • Declared surplus Rossi .22 caliber rifle for sale on govdeals.com to licensed FFA dealer
  • Approved five-year Axon body camera renewal for $54,962.76
  • Approved Embree Construction Services for concrete replacement at admin building and PD not to exceed $6,000 from ARPA funds
  • Approved Feral Seal Coating for parking lot work not to exceed $6,500 from ARPA funds
  • Approved purchase of rubber parking blocks and fasteners from CLEAM not to exceed $1,800 from ARPA funds
  • Adjourned into executive session at 6:23 PM under ORC 121.22(G)(1)
  • Appointed John Purcell as alternate on Board of Zoning Appeals with term expiring 5/14/25
  • Appointed Kevin Maloff as volunteer zoning consultant effective upon acceptance of job description
  • Promoted Evan Cordray from intern to temporary part-time administrative assistant at $10.50/hour effective May 14 through August 24, 2024
  • Adjourned meeting at 9:08 PM

BOT 4-23-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering several personnel matters, a nuisance property resolution, the school resource officers MOU renewal, and Memorial Day parade planning. The board accepted the resignation of Division Fire Chief Jake Rose and two probationary police officers, approved three new part-time firefighter/EMT hires, and designated all Purple Heart recipients in Goshen Township as grand marshals for the 2024 Memorial Day Parade. A nuisance abatement resolution was approved for a property at 7000 Willow Ridge Court that has been in violation for four years.

2024-04-15 Other routine
36m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 4-15-24

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2024-002, a use variance request to allow an accessory dwelling unit (guest lodging) in the basement of an existing light manufacturing building on Charles Schneider Road. The applicant, Jerry Henline of HACO (formerly Henline Adhesive Equipment Company), sought to convert approximately 2,300 square feet of basement space into guest accommodations for customers and employees visiting the facility. After discussion of stipulations regarding temporary use and prohibition of for-profit rental, the board approved the use variance unanimously.

Use variance for accessory dwelling unit/guest lodging in M1 manufacturing zoneHACO's plan to convert basement space into guest accommodations for customers and employeesDiscussion of stipulations to prevent permanent residence or for-profit rental useZoning code limitations on mixed uses in manufacturing districts vs. business districtsPotential future text amendment to address guest lodging in the zoning codeVacancy concerns with BZA and Zoning Commission membership

Decisions (3)

  • Approved minutes from the February 26th meeting
  • Approved use variance BZA 2024-002 with stipulations that the property not be used for for-profit rental and remain as a temporary use
  • Motion to adjourn approved

Action Items (3)

  • Max Merritt/zoning staff to prepare the approved variance documentation and deliver to the applicant by end of the week
  • Applicant to go through Clermont County for building permits after receiving variance documentation
  • Township to consider a text amendment to address guest lodging facilities within the zoning code

Financials

  • Approximately 2,300 square feet proposed for the accessory dwelling unit
  • Approximately 3,600 square feet total in the lower basement area
  • 800 square feet or 25% of floor area is the typical ADU maximum (this proposal exceeds that)
  • 10,000 square feet minimum lot area requirement (met by the property)
  • 42 years the business has been in the township

2024-04-09 Regular Meeting routine
45m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 04.09.2024

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering zoning text amendments, a proposed new pole barn for the service department, fire department promotions, and the chief's acceptance to the National Fire Academy executive officer program. The board approved temporary dwelling zoning text amendments and initiated text amendments on accessory structures including shipping container regulations. Two part-time firefighter/paramedics were promoted to full-time, and a fund transfer for Station 18 architectural fees was tabled pending confirmation of restricted fund transfer rules.

Zoning text amendments for temporary dwelling regulations (ZC-2024-003) approvedZoning text amendments for accessory structures and shipping container regulations initiatedProposed new 5-bay pole barn for service department discussed with ARPA fundingPromotion of two part-time firefighter/paramedics (Hyland and Hargis) to full-timeChief Myers accepted into National Fire Academy Executive Fire Officer ProgramCapital budget bills for new fire station update – passed House, pending SenateNew generation 9-1-1 system contract signed for Clermont CountyStation 18 building fund transfer tabled for verificationARPA fund explanation for public transparency

Decisions (10)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (March 27 – April 9, 2024) – passed unanimously
  • Zoning case ZC-2024-003 (temporary dwelling regulations text amendments) – approved unanimously
  • Resolution R-006-2024 to initiate zoning text amendments on accessory structures – approved unanimously
  • Motion to promote part-time firefighter/paramedic Elizabeth Hyland to full-time effective April 21, 2024 – passed unanimously
  • Motion to promote part-time firefighter/paramedic Harley Hargis to full-time with lateral transfer of sick leave at step three effective April 21, 2024 – passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve Chief Edward Myers to attend the Executive Fire Officer Program at the National Fire Academy at no cost – passed unanimously
  • Item I (transfer of $8,785.18 from Fund 2193 to Fund 4901 for Station 18) – tabled pending verification of restricted fund transfer rules
  • Item J (related to Station 18 architectural fees) – tabled
  • Motion to adjourn at 6:48 PM – passed unanimously

Action Items (7)

  • Chad Meadows to obtain gas line estimate for the proposed pole barn and report back at the next meeting
  • Chad Meadows to verify with Permit Central that the pole barn construction is compliant with all requirements
  • Chad Meadows to check with wastewater department on wash bay regulations
  • Lisa/fiscal officer to verify whether transferring from restricted Fund 2193 to Fund 4901 is permissible before next meeting
  • Bob Rose (Administrator) to be present at next meeting to discuss pole barn funding and fund transfer items
  • Liz (zoning) to submit accessory structure text amendments to Zoning Commission for review
  • Liz to revise zoning resolution for temporary dwelling amendments, effective 30 days from April 9

Financials

  • $40,000 for 31x81 pole barn ordered and installed
  • $25,000 for 35x85 concrete pad (7-inch thick)
  • $14,000 for electrical installation to the pole barn
  • $85,000 total proposed cost for pole barn project ($80,000 building + $5,000 contingency)
  • $210,115 currently available in ARPA fund
  • $61,615 remaining in ARPA fund if all undecided projects are approved
  • $1.8 million originally received in ARPA federal funds
  • $8,785.18 proposed transfer from Fund 2193 to Fund 4901 for Station 18
  • $2.5 million capital budget request in House bill for fire station
  • $2.5 million capital budget request in Senate bill for fire station
  • $0.40 per wireless device 9-1-1 fee (increased from $0.25)
  • 67% of 9-1-1 funds to Clermont County Commissioners, 27% to Union Township, 6% to Northeast division

2024-04-02 Regular Meeting routine
55m ▶ Vimeo

ZC 4-2-24

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases. The commission re-approved an expired preliminary development plan for the Pebble Grove PUD subdivision (306 units on 112 acres) and then approved the final development plan with staff conditions. The commission also approved a text amendment establishing regulations for temporary dwellings during new construction or major renovations, originally prompted by tornado-related needs.

Re-approval of expired preliminary development plan for Pebble Grove PUD subdivisionFinal development plan review for Pebble Grove subdivision with 306 unitsStormwater management and retention/detention pond designTraffic study results showing no improvements required for Linton RoadPotential connector road from Linton Road to Goshen Road through school/park propertyPublic sewer and water service from Clermont CountyText amendment for temporary dwellings during construction or after disasterResident concerns about school capacity, property taxes, and failed school leviesPond safety and fencing concerns from residents

Decisions (5)

  • Approved video meeting minutes from February 6, 2024
  • Approved extension/re-approval of the expired preliminary development plan for Pebble Grove subdivision (ZC 2024-004)
  • Approved final development plan for Pebble Grove subdivision (ZC 2024-004) with five staff-recommended conditions
  • Approved text amendment ZC 2024-003 regarding temporary dwellings during construction or major renovations
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:25 PM approved

Action Items (7)

  • Developer (Grand Communities) to submit HOA legal documentation for maintaining open space
  • Developer to update final development plans with detailed list of proposed amenities in open space areas
  • Developer to meet all national and local requirements for modification of wetlands and soils
  • Developer to provide additional details for proposed home models including site plans, elevations, and materials
  • Developer to submit a lighting plan meeting zoning resolution requirements
  • Staff to look into the status of the Appalachian grant for the Goshen Road to Linton Road connector road
  • Developer to provide private covenants before construction begins

Financials

  • 112 acres total site for Pebble Grove subdivision
  • 306 total residential units (220 detached single-family, 86 attached/duplex)
  • 45.7% open space proposed (exceeding 35% requirement)
  • 2.7 lots per acre density
  • 1,400 square feet minimum floor area for dwelling units
  • $200,000 starting price for paired/attached units
  • $300,000 range for Maple Street single-family homes
  • 50 foot minimum lot width, 30 foot front yard setback
  • Up to 1 year temporary dwelling permit for new construction, 6 months for major renovations

2024-03-26 Regular Meeting contentious
28m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 3-26-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on March 26, 2024. Key topics included the resignation of two full-time firefighter/paramedics to part-time status due to uncompetitive pay, authorization to issue an RFP for a construction manager for Station 18, discussion of property insurance appraisal discrepancies (particularly Station 19's valuation), and authorization to negotiate with a new IT company that could save approximately $19,000 annually. The board also discussed the failed fire levy and plans to place it on the November ballot.

Fire levy failure and plans for November ballotLoss of full-time firefighter/paramedics due to uncompetitive payRFP for construction manager for Station 18Insurance property appraisal discrepancies, especially Station 19 valuationIT services cost reduction negotiationDetective McAllister instructor training approvalRescheduling November meeting due to Election DayNext generation 911 operations updateAnnual Board of Health meetingExecutive session for personnel matters

Decisions (12)

  • Approved payment of bills for March 13–26, 2024
  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Accepted Captain Cody Martin's full-time resignation, changing to part-time firefighter/paramedic at $19.50/hour effective April 1, 2024
  • Accepted firefighter/paramedic Emily's full-time resignation, changing to part-time at $19.50/hour effective April 2, 2024
  • Changed volunteer firefighter EMT Jack Pegram to part-time status at $16/hour effective April 7, 2024
  • Authorized issuance of RFP for construction manager for Station 18
  • Cancelled November 5, 2024 trustee meeting (Election Day) and rescheduled to November 12 at 6 PM
  • Tabled motion to decrease insured blanket property values pending further investigation
  • Authorized staff to negotiate lower IT costs with a new company and sign related paperwork
  • Approved out-of-town travel for Detective McAllister for instructional skills training at Ohio Peace Officers Training Academy, April 8–19, registration cost $1,000
  • Adjourned into executive session at approximately 7:24 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1)
  • Adjourned the regular meeting

Action Items (5)

  • Chief Myers to contact CBIZ appraiser to determine how they arrived at the $1.3 million valuation for Station 19
  • Administrator Rhoads and Chief Myers to investigate insurance appraisal discrepancies and report back to the board
  • Administrator Rhoads and Melinda to negotiate with new IT company for lower costs and report back
  • Fire department and trustees to prepare fire levy for November ballot
  • Chief Myers to potentially contact Conger for an independent appraisal of Station 19

Financials

  • $19.50/hour – part-time pay rate for Captain Cody Martin and firefighter/paramedic Emily
  • $16.00/hour – part-time pay rate for Jack Pegram
  • $1,000 – registration cost for Detective McAllister's training
  • $19,000/year – potential IT cost savings with new company
  • $143,000 – current insurance coverage for salt storage (appraised at $50,000)
  • $228,000 – current insurance for service garage (appraised at $405,000, underinsured by $176,000)
  • $5,500,000 – current insurance for administration building (appraised at $5,860,000)
  • $4,000,000 – current insurance for Station 19 (appraised at only $1,300,000 by CBIZ)
  • $1,700,000–$1,900,000 – insurance company's own evaluation range for Station 19
  • $2,300,000+ – original construction cost of Station 19
  • $460/sq ft – estimated current construction cost per square foot for fire stations

2024-03-12 Regular Meeting routine
36m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 3-12-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on March 12, 2024, covering routine business including bill payments, personnel matters, and community planning. The board approved bringing the Memorial Day parade back in-house, scheduled a cleanup day for June 1st, adopted a resolution requesting a speed study on Staggy Road, approved purchasing crash investigation equipment, appointed a new full-time police officer, and established a minimum checking account balance policy to maximize interest earnings in a money market account. Public comments addressed COVID-19 vaccine concerns and a congressional campaign.

Condolences for Phyllis Smith, wife of longtime road supervisor Bill SmithCOVID-19 vaccine concerns presented by Advocates for Medical Truth groupCongressional campaign speech by Larry KiddBZA filing fee refund concern raised by Dave Math regarding Tom Mackey caseMemorial Day parade brought back in-house from Chamber of CommerceGoshen cleanup day planningDerek Holmes resignation from BZASpeed limit evaluation for Staggy Road (currently unposted at 55 mph)Officer Tyler Smith completed crash reconstructionist trainingHiring of Gary Moen as full-time police officerFinancial management - money market account strategy with CF BankSCBA compressor moved to Station 19 by service department saving significant costMedic unit delivery pushed back to July due to spring system back orderMarijuana business resolution 004-2024 clarificationPart-time employee Connor leaving for City of Cincinnati full-time positionExecutive session regarding public employee matters

Decisions (13)

  • Adopted the agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for February 28 through March 12, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of part-time employee Connor moving to City of Cincinnati full-time
  • Clarified intent of resolution 004-2024 regarding marijuana businesses was to adopt and approve
  • Approved Memorial Day parade on Monday, May 27th at 10 a.m.
  • Approved Goshen cleanup day for Saturday, June 1st
  • Accepted resignation of Derek Holmes from the BZA effective 3-5-24
  • Adopted resolution 005-2024 requesting Clermont County Engineer and ODOT evaluate speed limit on Staggy Road
  • Approved purchase of MLID Reach RX network rover kit for crash investigations not to exceed $2,800
  • Adjourned into executive session at 6:31 p.m. under ORC 121.22G1
  • Appointed Gary Moen as full-time police officer contingent upon completion of pre-employment screening
  • Approved maintaining minimum balance of $2.25 million in LCNB primary checking account with remainder in CF Bank money market account
  • Adjourned at 7:28 p.m.

Action Items (6)

  • Donna Rosick to spearhead Memorial Day parade planning, coordinating with the Chamber
  • Administrator Rose and staff to plan Goshen cleanup day for June 1st
  • Chad (service department) to install speed limit signs on Staggy Road once study is complete
  • Chief Rhodes to complete pre-employment screening process for Gary Moen
  • Laura (fiscal officer) to transfer excess funds above $2.25 million from LCNB to CF Bank money market account
  • Clermont County Engineer and ODOT to conduct speed study on Staggy Road

Financials

  • $2,800 - not-to-exceed cost for MLID Reach RX network rover kit for crash investigations
  • $2.25 million - minimum balance to maintain in LCNB primary checking account
  • $1 million - initial deposit into CF Bank money market account on February 13
  • $1,957.79 - interest earned on $1 million in 16 days (approximately $4,000/month)
  • $200 - BZA filing fee discussed by public speaker Dave Math
  • $6,000 - original quote from Breathing Air to move SCBA compressor (done cheaper in-house)
  • $34.5 trillion - national debt referenced by congressional candidate Larry Kidd

2024-03-11 Special Meeting routine
1h 13m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Special Meeting Workshop 3-11-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting with a zoning workshop to discuss potential text amendments to the township's zoning resolution. Key topics included reviewing the outdated growth management plan (last updated in 2000), considering increasing R1 minimum lot sizes from one to two acres, and addressing accessory structure definitions—particularly regarding shipping containers. The boards discussed how to regulate shipping containers as accessory structures following a recent BZA decision that approved them, with emphasis on appearance standards, single-story limitations, and limiting the number allowed per property.

Review of surrounding townships' land use plans (Wayne, Jackson, Batavia, Stone Lake, Miami)Need to update Goshen Township growth management plan (last updated in 2000)Consideration of increasing R1 minimum lot size from 1 acre to 2 acres or higherAccessory structure definition amendments - removing the word 'other'Shipping container regulations as accessory structures (paint, foundation, single story, number limits)Discussion of shipping containers potentially used as garagesRelationship between sewer/water availability and appropriate zoning densityIncorrectly zoned properties (R2/R3 without sewer/water infrastructure)

Decisions (2)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
  • Motion to adjourn the special meeting at 7:13 PM passed

Action Items (6)

  • Liz Fields / Max to create a more detailed map of R1 parcels with street names and better resolution for review
  • Evan / staff to complete research on county water, sewer, and septic coverage across the township
  • Liz Fields to draft text amendment language removing 'other' from accessory structure definition and adding shipping container provisions with specific standards (single level, painted consistently with house, on a pad, neutral color palette)
  • Liz Fields to research how other communities regulate shipping containers (including as garages and limiting number per property based on acreage)
  • Liz Fields to clean up overlapping definitions for detached accessory buildings/structures, accessory building, and accessory structure into one definition
  • Staff to distribute the old growth management plan to all members of all three boards

Financials

  • $76,000 spent on the downtown overlay/gateway district plan developed years ago
  • Shipping containers cost approximately $2,500 delivered
  • Custom-built sheds estimated at $10,000-$20,000
  • 10,700 acres currently zoned for agriculture in Goshen Township
  • 3,250 acres zoned for residential or PDUD use in Goshen Township
  • 77% of Goshen Township is R1 agricultural zoning
  • Only 7% of R1 parcels are under 2 acres

2024-02-27 Regular Meeting ceremonial

BOT 2-27-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring ceremonial swearing-in of new police officer Maggie Sykes, promotions of firefighter Nick Messer to Lieutenant and Todd Estep to Captain, and police awards including Officer of the Year for Kyle Hauk. The board approved the purchase of a new police SUV, accepted multiple grants including a body-worn camera grant and BWC grants, passed a resolution to limit marijuana dispensaries to the one existing location while prohibiting cultivators and processors, and initiated zoning text amendments for temporary dwellings during home construction or renovation.

  • Accepted OCJS grant of $48,421.20 for body-worn camera program
  • Scheduled special trustees meeting zoning workshop for March 11 at 6 p.m.
  • Passed Resolution 004-2024 to prohibit medical and adult use marijuana cultivators and processors and limit retail dispensaries to existing one

  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Robert Webster effective 3-1-2024

2024-02-26 Other contentious
1h 13m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 2-26-24

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on Case BZA 2024-001, an appeal by Tom Mackey of the zoning inspector's denial of his application to place two 20-foot shipping containers as permanent accessory storage structures on his property at 6873 Westcourt Lane. Township staff argued shipping containers are not customarily found as residential accessory structures and more closely match the definition of temporary portable storage units. After testimony from the applicant, supporters, and a neutral neighbor, the board voted to reverse the administrative denial and approve the appeal with stipulations including painting all containers gray, placing them on gravel pads, and completing all work within 120 days.

Appeal of zoning inspector denial of shipping containers as permanent residential accessory structuresWhether shipping containers qualify as customary accessory structures under township zoning resolutionDistinction between temporary portable storage units and permanent accessory structures in zoning codeTownship zoning resolution lacking specific language addressing shipping containersProcedural concerns about applicants being denied even the ability to apply for shipping container permitsProperty maintenance and neighborhood aesthetics concernsNomination of new BZA secretary to replace Thomas CurryUpcoming zoning workshop on proposed text amendments scheduled for March 11th

Decisions (3)

  • Approved video minutes from January 22nd meeting
  • Nominated and approved new secretary for the BZA board
  • Approved appeal BZA 2024-001 reversing the administrative denial of two shipping containers as accessory structures, with stipulations (amended site plan, painting containers gray, gravel pads, 120-day completion deadline)

Action Items (6)

  • Tom Mackey must paint all three shipping containers solid gray per letter of intent
  • Tom Mackey must install gravel pads before placing new containers
  • Tom Mackey must complete all work and have it inspected within 120 days of application issuing
  • Tom Mackey must file the amended site plan with updated setbacks as the official site plan
  • Township staff to send email about March 11th zoning workshop
  • Township to recruit alternate members for BZA and Zoning Commission boards

Financials

  • Two 160-square-foot shipping containers proposed (each 20 feet, 9'6" height)
  • $200 extra cost mentioned by applicant for BZA appeal process
  • 2.5-acre parcel at 6873 Westcourt Lane
  • 5,000 pounds approximate weight per container mentioned by supporter
  • 120-day deadline for completion of all work

NA Other urgent
52m ▶ Vimeo

Fire and EMS Levy 2024 "Chat with the Chiefs"

This was a public informational session titled 'Chat with the Chiefs' regarding the 2024 Goshen Fire and Ambulance replacement levy. Fire Chief Pegram and Chief Meyers presented the history of fire/EMS levies in Goshen Township, explained the current 4.5-mil levy expiring December 31, 2024, detailed rising operational costs due to inflation, and answered questions from residents about the proposed replacement levy and its impact on taxes and future services.

History of failed fire/EMS levies in 2008, 2010, and 2012 and resulting service cuts2024 replacement levy for expiring 4.5-mil fire and EMS levyInflationary cost increases for medications, equipment, vehicles, and fuelFixed-rate levy mechanics and how property value increases reduce millageStaffing challenges with part-time personnel recruitment and retentionAffordable Care Act impact limiting part-time hours to 1,500/yearConsequences of levy failure including loss of 40 part-time employees and closing a fire stationMutual aid billing implications for residents if levy failsComparison of call volume with neighboring departments (3,000+ runs with 2 stations)Township growth projections and impact on fire/EMS servicesGeneral fund limitations and inability to sustain fire department operationsCompetitive pay challenges versus neighboring departments like Miami Township and KetteringNew construction revenue lag and minimal budget impact

Decisions (0)

  • None recorded

Action Items (2)

  • Chief Meyers to email Rich Starnow (and potentially other interested voters) year-over-year budget details, growth forecasting projections, and staffing plans for the next five years
  • Fire department leadership to provide year-over-year actual budget expenditure data since 2018 to resident Dave who requested it

Financials

  • $2.9 million total annual tax budget revenue for fire department
  • $600,000+ annual EMS billing revenue
  • $3.5 million approximate total annual fire department budget
  • $1,427,720.80 current levy revenue certified for 2024
  • $2,122,835 projected new levy revenue if approved
  • $695,114.20 increase in levy revenue (48.85% increase on this levy, 22.72% total budget increase)
  • $325,000/year in mortgage payments for Station 19, ambulances, and fire truck
  • $625,000 annual part-time employee costs
  • $400,000/year from fire levy dating back to volunteer era
  • $500,000/year from EMS levies (1974 and 1994)
  • $400,000/year fire department share of safety services levy (2002)
  • $1.6 million approximate current fire and EMS levy revenue
  • $250,000 ambulance cost in 2020, now $340,000
  • $625,000 fire engine cost in 2020, now $900,000
  • $340,000 estimated SCBA replacement cost
  • $70,000 per cardiac monitor (doubled in price)
  • $4,200 per set of fire gear (up from $2,000-$3,400)
  • $72,000 budgeted for fuel (up from $40,000-$42,000)
  • $2 million+ in grants received over past 10 years
  • $184,000 grant received with Loveland for physicals and health/wellness
  • $115.55 annual cost increase for average $204,300 home
  • $34,000 new construction revenue increase 2019-2024
  • $64,000+ saved by chief absorbing deputy chief role

40m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 2-13-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering several resignations, mutual aid reimbursements from the 2022 tornado, and the announcement of Homorama 2024 at the Reserves at Stone Pillars Farm. The board approved a contract with MSP Design for engineering and design work on Fire Station 18, established a five-minute community forum policy, and addressed multiple personnel changes including resignations of police and fire department staff.

Homorama 2024 proclamation at Reserves at Stone Pillars Farm (August 24 - September 8)Tornado mutual aid reimbursement for 35 agencies from July 6, 2022 tornadoStation 18 engineering/design contract with MSP Design approvedStation 18 bill progressing from House to Senate; Senate Finance Committee testimony scheduled Feb 27Service garage improvements and cleanup by service departmentMultiple personnel resignations (police officer, firefighter/paramedics, BZA member)Community forum policy established (5-minute limit, any topic, address the chair)Leave of absence policy revisionTragic recent community deaths affecting first respondersBusiness recovery from 2022 tornado - Wage War reopenedZoning workshop held previous nightChat with Chiefs levy event scheduled for next Tuesday at 6:30 PMVolunteer intern Evan Cordray appointed (high school senior)John Langenheim promoted from BZA alternate to full member$48,000 body camera grant secured by part-time grants coordinator Donna Rosick

Decisions (18)

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills from January 24 through February 13, 2024
  • Approved proclamation announcing Homorama 2024 at the Reserves at Stone Pillars Farm (August 24 - September 8, 2024)
  • Approved revision to leave of absence without pay policy granting township administrator authority to approve absences up to two weeks
  • Approved increase in appropriations of $351,546.51 in tornado expense fund to reimburse 35 mutual aid partners
  • Approved increase in appropriations of $8,725.07 for police/fire retention grant with unused funds to be refunded to Ohio
  • Accepted resignation of Tom Curry from Board of Zoning Appeals effective February 6, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of Officer Matt Richwine effective February 20, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic David Gray effective January 11, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Stephen Hill effective January 25, 2024
  • Accepted resignation of firefighter EMT Grant Rue effective January 13, 2024

  • Approved community forum policy: five-minute limit per person, at beginning of meeting, on any topic, addressing the board chair
  • Approved contract with MSP Design for engineering and design of Station 18
  • Adjourned into executive session at 6:37 PM per ORC 121.22(G)(1)
  • Approved moving John Langenheim from BZA alternate to BZA full member effective February 13, 2024 with term ending December 31, 2025
  • Appointed Evan Cordray as volunteer intern effective February 14, 2024 through May 14, 2024
  • Adjourned regular meeting at 7:25 PM

Action Items (10)

  • Administrator Rose to coordinate Homorama 2024 details including Goshen Township booth and Goshen Township day with discounted admission
  • Post Homorama fact sheet on township website
  • Chief Meyers to develop bids for construction manager at risk for Station 18 after MSP Design contract is signed
  • Chief Meyers to speak before Senate Finance Committee on February 27 regarding Station 18 bill
  • Chief Meyers to put remaining Bureau of Workers' Compensation Grant items (gear dryer, hoods, gloves) into service
  • Director Meadows to get road mileage certification signed by trustees and submitted
  • Refund $8,725.07 in unused police/fire retention grant funds to the state of Ohio
  • Disburse $351,546.51 to 35 mutual aid partners for tornado assistance reimbursement
  • Trustee Allen offered to help paint/clean at service garage
  • Include community forum policy announcement at beginning of each year when setting meetings

  • $263,659.88 - amount provided by Ohio EMA (75% of mutual aid costs)
  • $87,000 approximately - township's share of mutual aid reimbursement (25%)
  • $8,725.07 - police/fire retention grant unused funds to be refunded to Ohio
  • $4,000 - maximum per employee for police/fire retention grant
  • $48,000 - body camera contract grant secured by Donna Rosick for police department
  • 47.100 miles - updated 2024 road mileage for Goshen Township

2024-02-12 Work Session routine
1h 20m ▶ Vimeo

Special BOT Zoning Workshop 2-12-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special zoning workshop with the BZA and Zoning Commission to discuss potential text amendments to the Township Zoning Resolution. Key topics included temporary dwelling regulations for new construction and major renovations, shipping container regulations, tiny homes, and a proposal to increase the minimum lot size in R1 districts to two acres to help preserve rural character and slow development.

Temporary dwelling regulations for new construction and major renovations (RV/camper use during building)Shipping container regulations for residential and commercial useTiny homes - both as primary dwellings and accessory dwelling unitsMother-in-law suite / accessory dwelling unit regulationsProposal to increase R1 minimum lot size to two acres to preserve rural characterRV parking regulations for visitors on residential propertiesNeed for industrial zoning to offset residential tax burdenLand use plan review and preservation of agricultural landConcern about rapid development similar to Lebanon, Mason, and West Chester

Decisions (2)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:21 PM passed unanimously

Action Items (9)

  • Liz Field to finalize temporary dwelling text amendment language by February 27 trustee meeting
  • Liz Field to tweak the major renovation definition to be clearer and potentially include hardship language
  • Liz Field to research existing code provisions for temporary RV parking/use by visitors on residential properties
  • Liz Field to research shipping container regulations, including contacting Lucas County/Springfield Township planning director
  • Liz Field to look into accessory dwelling unit regulations and potential expansion for mother-in-law suite type structures
  • Staff to prepare land use plan maps showing R1 parcels of two acres or more for next workshop
  • Administrator Rose to collect cell phone numbers from BZA and Zoning Commission members for text notifications
  • Staff to send out survey to schedule a March zoning workshop
  • Lisa to handle the February 27 trustee meeting presentation of temporary dwelling language in Liz's absence

Financials

  • Average tiny home size: 225 square feet
  • Tiny homes generally under 600 square feet
  • Average tiny home cost: just over $50,000
  • Current minimum dwelling size: 1,400 square feet
  • Shipping container cost approximately $1,500
  • Current accessory dwelling unit minimum lot area: 10,000 square feet
  • Toledo tiny home lots: 40 by 80 feet (3,200 square feet)

2024-02-06 Regular Meeting routine
34m ▶ Vimeo

ZC 2-6-24

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held its first meeting of 2024, administering oaths of office, electing officers, and setting the 2024 meeting schedule. The Commission then heard and approved two zoning cases: ZC 2024-001 to add eight attached single-family homes to the Lakefield Place PUD subdivision, and ZC 2024-002 to change six end units to a different model at the Retreat at Villages of Belmont PUD. An upcoming zoning workshop was announced to discuss tiny homes, shipping containers, storage units, camper living, and a potential agricultural district.

Oath of office for Zoning Commission membersElection of Zoning Commission officers for 20242024 meeting schedule approvalZC 2024-001: Minor adjustment to Lakefield Place PUD to add 8 attached single-family homesZC 2024-002: Minor modification to Retreat at Villages of Belmont PUD to change 6 end units to different modelUpcoming zoning workshop on tiny homes, shipping containers, storage units, camper living, and agricultural districtDeerfield Road traffic concerns raised by public member

Decisions (8)

  • Approved minutes from December 6, 2023 meeting
  • Steve Pegram elected as Chairperson
  • Dave Dinwiddie elected as Vice Chair
  • Dave Curry to continue serving as Secretary
  • 2024 meeting schedule approved (first Tuesday of every month)
  • Zoning case 2024-001 approved as recommended by staff (add 8 attached single-family homes to Lakefield Place PUD)
  • Zoning case 2024-002 approved as recommended by staff (change 6 end units at Retreat at Villages of Belmont PUD)
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:05 PM approved

Action Items (2)

  • Zoning workshop scheduled for Monday at 6 PM to discuss tiny homes, shipping containers, storage units, camper living, and agricultural district
  • Staff to notarize and retain oath of office documents on file

Financials

  • Lakefield Place PUD: 110.20 acres total area
  • Lakefield Place PUD: 207 detached single-family lots plus 56 attached units for 263 total units
  • Attached single-family lots no less than 4,703 square feet
  • 8.5 acres for attached single-family portion of Lakefield Place
  • Minimum lot width standard of 50 feet (some attached lots between 26-40 feet)

2024-01-23 Regular Meeting routine
47m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 1-23-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering budget true-ups for 2023, a presentation by Goshen Local Schools Superintendent Brian Bailey on a $26 million bond levy for school expansions, acceptance of a police body camera grant, facility appraisal authorization, and fire department promotions. The board also scheduled a special workshop on zoning text amendments and reviewed strong financial performance across township funds with $421,000 in total unencumbered growth.

Goshen Local Schools $26 million bond levy presentation for March ballot2023 budget true-up and financial review across all fundsAcceptance of $48,421.20 body camera grantCPAAA community service contributions update ($94,482 since 2011)Station 18 architect contract and construction manager process updateFacility appraisals for insurance adequacyClermont County 36% property value increase concernsNew construction windfall tax revenue explanationUSDA loan application approved for school bond at 3.75% interest rateZoning text amendment workshop schedulingFire department promotions: Todd Eastet to Captain, Nick Messer to LieutenantDoug Englund status change from part-time to volunteer firefighterTownship investing unencumbered funds for first time in historySenate bill for firehouse financing legislation updateARPA fund expenditures review including $832,000 in road paving

Decisions (13)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (Jan 10–23, 2024) – passed
  • Motion to accept $48,421.20 grant from Ohio Office of Criminal Justice Services for Police Body Worn Cameras Program – passed
  • Motion to schedule special Board of Trustees workshop for zoning text amendments on Monday, February 12, 2024 at 6 PM – passed
  • Motion to appropriate $4,500 into ARPA Fund 2272 for facility appraisals – passed
  • Motion to hire CBIZ Valuation Group for facility appraisals at cost not to exceed $4,500 from ARPA Fund 2272 – passed
  • Motion to declare surplus and list wall-mount data rack for sale on GovDeals – passed
  • Motion to move Firefighter Paramedic Doug Englund from part-time to volunteer status effective 1-23-24 – passed
  • Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Chief Myers to National Fallen Firefighters Cast Meeting in Baltimore, MD (March 21-22, 2024) at no cost – passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:45 PM per ORC 121.22 g1 – passed
  • Motion to promote Lieutenant Todd Eastet to Captain effective 1-28-2024 – passed
  • Motion to promote Firefighter Paramedic Nick Messer to Lieutenant effective 1-28-2024 – passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 7:19 PM – passed

Action Items (10)

  • Chief Myers to follow up on Station 18 architect contract expected by next meeting for construction manager process
  • Administrator Rose and Chief to follow up on Senate bill related to firehouse financing around the 27th/17th date
  • Administrator Rose to coordinate zoning text amendment workshop with consultants for February 12, 2024
  • CBIZ Valuation Group to conduct appraisals of township-owned facilities
  • Staff to list surplus wall-mount data rack on GovDeals for sale
  • Administrator Rose to work through obstacles distributing $263,000 Ohio EMA funds to mutual aid partners
  • Laura and team to continue investing township funds and explore putting more money in the bank
  • Donna (grants writer) to continue grant research and writing efforts
  • Celebrate Kyle Houck as Officer of the Year 2023
  • Get plaque ordered for tornado-surviving flag display

  • $4,500 – cost for CBIZ Valuation Group facility appraisals funded from ARPA
  • $26 million – Goshen Local Schools bond levy on March ballot at 2.9 mils (potentially 2.68 mils with USDA loan)

  • $421,000 – total unencumbered growth across all township funds in 2023
  • $158,000 – total unencumbered increase across all funds
  • $533,000 – police department unencumbered growth
  • $300,000 – fire department unencumbered increase
  • $171,000 – service department unencumbered growth
  • $595,000 – ARPA funds spent on road paving in 2023
  • $832,000 – total paving expenditure (ARPA + $237,000 grant)
  • $263,000 – received from Ohio EMA for mutual aid partners

2024-01-22 Special Meeting contentious
1h 33m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 1-22-24

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held public hearings on three remanded cases involving property at 6823 Goshen Road: two administrative appeals (fence violation and stop work order for an unpermitted automotive repair shop) and a use variance request. After hearing testimony from staff, the applicant Jeff Kelly, former zoning director Bob Seifert, supporters, and opponents, the Board denied all three applications. The meeting also included oath of office ceremonies for board members and election of officers for the new year.

Administrative appeal of fence violation (height, transparency, and orientation violations)Administrative appeal of stop work order for unpermitted automotive repair shop in R-3 residential zoneUse variance request to operate automotive repair heavy in R-3 residential districtDispute over original building permit and whether it authorized commercial use vs. agricultural barnOath of office for Board members and election of 2024 officersCourt remand of previously heard cases from 2022Setback requirements for commercial buildings near residential lotsTestimony from former zoning director Bob Seifert contradicting applicant's claims

Decisions (8)

  • Approved minutes from October 16th meeting
  • Ed Sigla elected as Chairperson
  • John Langenheim elected as Vice Chairperson
  • Tom Currie nominated and elected as Secretary
  • BZA-2022-011 (fence administrative appeal) denied - administrative decision upheld
  • BZA-2022-012 (stop work order administrative appeal) denied - administrative decision upheld
  • BZA-2022-013 (use variance for automotive repair shop) denied per staff recommendation
  • Motion to adjourn approved

Action Items (2)

  • Staff to report back to the Court of Common Pleas judge after Board determinations on all three cases
  • Further court proceedings to follow Board decisions

Financials

  • $3,300 per year in tax revenue from the subject parcel cited by applicant
  • 4,800 square foot pole barn garage at issue
  • 100-foot minimum setback required from adjacent residential lot vs. 28 feet actual distance
  • 6-foot privacy fence constructed where 4-foot maximum with 50% transparency is required

2024-01-09 Regular Meeting routine
37m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 1-9-24

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their first regular meeting of 2024, electing new board leadership with Trustee Hodges as chair and Trustee Allen as vice chair. The board approved moving forward with MSP Design as the architect for Fire Station 18, approved a $50,000 ARPA contribution to the Clermont County Land Bank for cleanup of the contaminated Combs dump properties on State Route 28, and conducted several annual housekeeping items. A retirement presentation was made for firefighter Bill Waits for 22 years of service, and nine new part-time firefighter EMTs were hired.

Election of 2024 Board Chair (Hodges) and Vice Chair (Allen)Station 18 fire station architect selection - MSP Design chosen via RFQ processCombs dump cleanup partnership with Clermont County Land Bank using ARPA fundsRetirement recognition for firefighter Bill Waits (22 years of service)Hiring of nine part-time firefighter EMTs from Scarlet Oaks adult classClermont County 9-1-1 Next Gen (NG911) commission appointmentBrownfield grant rule changes affecting township eligibilityAnnual housekeeping items (mileage rate, surplus sales, zoning compensation, road mileage)Upscale Motors theft case investigation noted by Trustee HausermanPassing of Tom Haywood notedBasement floor renovation project completedBZA and Zoning Commission membership changes

Decisions (21)

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for December 12, 2023 through January 9, 2024
  • Nominated and approved Trustee Hodges as Board Chair for 2024
  • Nominated and approved Trustee Allen as Vice Chair for 2024
  • Adopted the 2024 schedule for regular Board of Trustee meetings (second Tuesdays at 6 PM)
  • Approved moving forward with negotiating a contract with MSP Design as architect for Station 18
  • Accepted resignation of firefighter EMT Clayton Newman effective 1-3-24
  • Hired nine part-time firefighter EMTs at $15.50/hour effective 1-14-2024
  • Set 2024 standard mileage rate at 67 cents per business mile
  • Approved sale of surplus items via govdeals.com (Resolution R-001-2024)
  • Set 2024 zoning commission and BZA member compensation at $50/month, not to exceed $599 annually
  • Approved 2024 road mileage of 47.062 miles per Clermont County Engineer
  • Approved refund of $470 in zoning fees to Don Cornwell
  • Passed Resolution R-002-2024 appointing Trustee Hauserman to Clermont County 9-1-1 program review committee
  • Increased appropriations in ARPA account 2272-130-322-0001 by $50,000
  • Approved agreement with Clermont County Land Bank to assume cleanup of Combs dump properties on State Route 28
  • Approved $50,000 ARPA contribution to Clermont County Land Bank for Combs dump remediation with reimbursement request upon property sale
  • Adjourned into executive session at 6:35 PM per ORC 121.22(g)(1)
  • Reappointed Jesse Shaw to the BZA with term expiring December 31, 2028
  • Moved Steve Pegram from alternate to member of the Zoning Commission with term expiring December 31, 2028
  • Adjourned at 7:32 PM

  • Prosecutor's office to review MSP Design contract once negotiated

  • Administrator Rose to sign related documents for Land Bank agreement
  • Melinda to distribute message to employees about hard copies of ADP tax documents
  • Chief Rose and team to begin construction manager at risk RFQ process after architect contract is signed

  • $3,000,000 estimated cleanup cost for Combs dump properties on State Route 28
  • $500,000 estimated local match required for brownfield grant above $1,000,000
  • $1,000,000 tentatively guaranteed to land bank from brownfield funds in 2024
  • $387,700 brownfield grant received in early 2023 for environmental studies

  • $100,000 approximate unpaid property taxes on Combs dump properties

  • $0.67 per mile IRS mileage reimbursement rate for 2024
