2023-04-25 Regular Meeting routine
56m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 4.25.23

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering cleanup day planning, zoning refunds, solar energy restrictions discussion with County Commissioner Painter, personnel actions, and the Combs dumps remediation. The board went into executive session twice—once for legal matters with the prosecutor and once for personnel interviews. Key decisions included promoting a service foreman, appointing a zoning commission alternate, and requesting Land Bank intervention for the Combs dumps properties.

Cleanup day planning for June 3 at Station 18 with traffic management concernsZoning fee refunds for sign changes that should not have been chargedClosure of dormant bicentennial bank accountResignation of firefighter Tyler ReadAdoption of iWorQ zoning software for permits and property maintenance trackingSenate Bill 52 solar energy restrictions discussion with Commissioner PainterSolar farm restriction options for townships (large-scale 50kW+ and community-scale 5-49.5MW)Promotion of Joe Reynolds to service foremanAppointment of Steve Pegram as zoning commission alternateAdvertising for BZA vacanciesCombs dumps remediation and Land Bank intervention requestTornado recovery update including firehouse and building roof repairsCounty road paving and infrastructure projects updateState property re-evaluation concerns (43% increase proposed)Cancellation of May 9 trustee meeting

Decisions (15)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (April 11–24, 2023) passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:07 PM for legal conference passed
  • Motion to set cleanup day for June 3, 2023, 8 AM to noon at Station 18 passed
  • Motion to refund $200 to Grayson Motorsports from zoning account 2181 passed
  • Motion to refund $200 to Glenn Grooms from zoning account 2181 passed
  • Motion to close bicentennial account (5553) and move funds to primary account (3054) passed
  • Motion to accept resignation of Tyler Read effective April 2023 passed
  • Motion to approve service agreement with iWorQ for zoning software passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:38 PM for personnel matters passed
  • Motion to promote Joe Reynolds to service foreman effective May 7, 2023 at $1 above highest paid service worker with one-year probationary period passed
  • Motion to appoint Steve Pegram as alternate on zoning commission with term expiring April 25, 2025 passed
  • Motion to advertise for BZA members passed
  • Motion to request Clermont County Land Bank intervention with Combs dumps and for county treasurer to seek relief from bankruptcy stay passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:54 PM passed

Action Items (9)

  • Administrator Rose to work with Director Meadows and Chief Meyer on traffic management plan for June 3 cleanup day at Station 18
  • Administrator Rose to coordinate contingency traffic plans including use of Old 28 and police officer traffic control for cleanup day
  • Lisa (fiscal officer) to coordinate with two trustees to visit LCNB bank with valid driver's licenses to close bicentennial account
  • Board to cancel May 9 meeting; next meeting to be May 23
  • Board to address local government fund at Township Association meeting on May 18
  • Administrator Rose to advertise for BZA member vacancies
  • Administrator Rose to coordinate with Clermont County Land Bank regarding Combs dumps intervention
  • Board to schedule solar energy restriction discussion for a future meeting agenda (likely May)
  • Jen Farmer to facilitate cleanup day event administratively; Director Meadows to handle physical setup

Financials

  • $200 refund to Grayson Motorsports from zoning account 2181
  • $200 refund to Glenn Grooms from zoning account 2181
  • $33,065.05 in bicentennial donations received
  • $45,850.65 in bicentennial appropriations paid out

  • $4,500 annual fee for iWorQ zoning software

  • $25 million+ total for highway maintenance garage and administrative building

2023-04-18 Special Meeting routine
1h 20m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Special Meeting 4.18.23

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special Zoning Workshop with the Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals to discuss potential text amendments to the zoning code. Key discussions included administrative relief approval processes, minimum lot sizes for PUDs, the need for a land use plan before making major zoning changes, and various topics such as food trucks, corner lot standards, fencing, tiny homes, barndominiums, Airbnbs, solar panels, and temporary businesses. The group prioritized pursuing a future land use plan as a foundational step and agreed to tackle simpler code fixes in the meantime.

Administrative relief approval process and ORC compliance for zoning inspector authorityMinimum lot sizes and PUD standards for residential developmentsNeed for a comprehensive land use plan before major zoning changesSolar panel farm regulations and upcoming multi-township meetingFood truck and temporary business regulationsCorner lot standards and fencing/privacy fence requirementsAirbnb/short-term rental regulation authority for statutory townshipsTiny homes, barndominiums, and container homesSmall livestock and chicken regulations under ORC agricultural definitionsArchitectural standards for planned unit developmentsPotential creation of separate agricultural and residential zoning districtsEV charging stations, breweries/distilleries, and artisan industrial uses

Decisions (5)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
  • Agreed that the 10% administrative relief language would reference 'zoning inspector and zoning supervisor' instead of 'township administrator' to align with ORC requirements
  • Agreed to proceed with previously discussed text amendments through the Zoning Commission
  • Agreed that a future land use plan should be completed before making major zoning changes like lot sizes and rezoning
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:20 PM passed

Action Items (10)

  • Liz to finalize language on administrative relief text amendments incorporating 'zoning supervisor' terminology
  • Liz to review residential, industrial/business, and mixed-use PUD standards and provide expert opinion for next meeting
  • Liz to develop a proposal/scope for a future land use plan process, potentially starting with goals/objectives and future land use map
  • Liz to consult with Alyssa on common complaint items to help prioritize zoning code fixes
  • Liz to draft proposed language for corner lot standards and fencing regulations
  • Liz to draft food truck and temporary business regulations
  • Liz to look into parking lot requirements for businesses and nonconforming use issues
  • Julia to research whether statutory townships have authority to regulate Airbnbs/short-term rentals
  • Liz to check with Alyssa on businesses approved during change of command that have not completed agreed-upon requirements
  • Trustees/staff to attend solar panel meeting at Bethel Township on Thursday

Financials

  • Current minimum PUD size is 10 acres
  • Current R1 minimum lot size is 4,000 square feet, discussion of increasing to 2 acres
  • Current minimum house size is 1,400 square feet in R1
  • PUD minimum lot size is 5,000 square feet
  • 8,000 square foot lots require rear alley loaded garages in PUDs
  • Residential PUD with 35% open space allows up to 6 units per acre
  • Residential PUD with 50% open space allows up to 8 units per acre
  • Mixed-use PUD requires minimum 25% open space
  • Business PUD cannot exceed 70% paved/hard surface
  • Miami Township land use plan references 5 acres for large lot/low density residential
  • Administrative relief threshold is less than 10%
  • Front yard fence height limit discussed at 4 feet versus 8 feet
Trustee AllenTrustee HodgesTrustee HausermannLiz (zoning consultant)Julia (legal counsel)Dan (township administrator)Lisa (township staff)Bobby (township staff)Lee Lewis (Zoning Commission member)David Seid (Zoning Commission member, 2231 State Route 132)

2023-04-11 Regular Meeting routine
1h 1m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 4-11-23

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting, approving a rezoning of two Valley Lane properties from B1 (business) to R6 (high-density attached residential) for proposed duplexes. The board discussed township signage placement challenges due to gas and water mains at the preferred location near State Route 28 and Goshen Road. They also adopted an administration building use policy, approved a new service department job description, declared a 2001 Ford Ranger surplus, and appointed a new full-time zoning inspector.

Rezoning of Valley Lane properties from B1 to R6 for duplex construction (ZC2023003)Township signage placement challenges due to underground gas and water mainsFillmore Construction started OPWC grant road milling and paving on Gibbs, Thompson, and McHenryStation 18 code questions pending with risk managementAdministration Building Use Policy adoptionService department job description approvalSurplus disposal of 2001 Ford RangerAppointment of new full-time zoning inspectorSolar panel zoning authority discussion - Clermont County Township Association meetingNew township decorative signs and corp signs installation completedFirehouse Magazine national PR feature on Goshen TownshipEMS Agency of the Year recognition in Clermont Sun

Decisions (9)

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for March 28 through April 10, 2023
  • Approved rezoning of two Valley Lane properties from B1 to R6 (Zoning Case ZC2023003)
  • Set special zoning workshop meeting for April 18 at 7 PM
  • Adopted Administration Building Use Policy
  • Approved service department job description
  • Declared 2001 Ford Ranger (VIN ending 3471) surplus and authorized disposal
  • Appointed Kayla Witt as full-time zoning inspector at $19/hour with one-year probationary period
  • Adjourned at 7:55 PM

Action Items (9)

  • Officer Lara Item to investigate moving township sign location into parking lot area near 28 and Goshen Road, 18 inches from gas main
  • Chad Meadows to test parking lot asphalt depth and potentially remove a corner section for sign placement
  • Administrator Rose to consult with legal counsel regarding no parking signs for the parking lot
  • Officer Item to pursue BZA variance for sign placement before considering lot consolidation
  • Pine tree location to serve as Plan B for sign if primary location fails
  • Chief Myers to follow up with risk management on Station 18 code questions with Brian Shrive
  • Chad Meadows to take 2001 Ford Ranger to FIPS for scrap after fire department training
  • Fire department to use surplus Ford Ranger for vehicle extrication training
  • Kayla Witt appointment contingent upon successful pre-employment screening

Financials

  • $6,500 for one-ton truck transmission, cooler, radiator, and cooling line repairs
  • $35 per ton for 304 aggregate (avoided by reusing road grindings)
  • $1,200 approximate previous offer on 2001 Ford Ranger before engine failure
  • $19 per hour starting rate for new zoning inspector Kayla Witt

2023-04-04 Regular Meeting contentious
29m ▶ Vimeo

ZC 4-4-23.mp4

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on zoning case ZC 2023-003, a request to rezone two lots on Valley Lane from B1 (business) to R6 (residential) for construction of two duplexes (four total units) near the intersection of Goshen Road and State Route 28. Staff recommended approval based on the existing residential character of Valley Lane and the growth management plan. The commission discussed concerns about setting a precedent for high-density R6 zoning and potential future implications for adjacent properties. The rezoning was approved 3-1, and the regular meeting addressed old business including a pending zoning case awaiting an impact study.

Rezoning of two lots on Valley Lane from B1 to R6 for duplex constructionConcerns about setting a precedent for high-density R6 zoning in the townshipHistory of R6 zoning issues on Snyder Road referenced as cautionary exampleConsistency with Township comprehensive/growth management plan recommending 2-4 residential unitsExisting residential character of Valley Lane despite B1 zoning designationPotential future lot combination and higher density development concernsZC 2023-001 still pending impact study

Decisions (5)

  • Approved minutes from February 21st meeting
  • Approved opening of public forum
  • Approved closing of public forum
  • Approved ZC 2023-003 rezoning from B1 to R6 with staff recommendations (3-1 vote: Currie-Yes, Dinwiddie-Yes, Lewis-No, Otkey-Yes)
  • Approved adjournment

Action Items (3)

  • Continue monitoring ZC 2023-001 pending impact study completion
  • Liz Fields / McBride Dale Clary to discuss potential R4 alternative zoning with applicant if needed
  • Applicant (Mr. Taylor) to proceed with meeting R6 code requirements for proposed duplexes

Financials

  • 4 total residential units proposed (two duplexes) on two lots
  • Adjacent property is approximately 3.1 acres
  • Applicant's two lots combined are approximately 1 acre
  • Estimated 1-year construction timeline for the proposed homes

BOT 3.28.23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering several topics including recognition of the fire/EMS department as Ohio's EMS Department of the Year, a Brownfield grant update on the Combs dumps property, proposed zoning text amendments, and various departmental updates. The board approved payment of bills, zoning text amendments, out-of-town training for a detective, and an authorized credit card users list before adjourning to executive session regarding employee matters.

  • Motion to approve out-of-town travel for Detective McMillan for homicide investigations advanced training ($1,375 registration) - passed
  • Motion to approve authorized credit card users list - passed
  • Motion to adjourn to executive session under ORC 121.22 G1 - passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:59 PM - passed

2023-03-21 Special Meeting routine
1h 32m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Special Meeting 3-21-23.mp4

Goshen Township Trustees held a special meeting and zoning workshop with the Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals to discuss potential text amendments to the Goshen Township Zoning Code. Key topics included allowing primary-use structures (barns, garages) on lots without a residential dwelling, permitting accessory structures in front yards under certain conditions, updating flag lot (panhandle lot) regulations to align with county standards, granting staff minor modification authority for small variance requests, and speeding up the BZA resolution signing process. Consultant Liz Fields facilitated the discussion and gathered direction from all boards.

Allowing primary-use structures (barns/garages) on vacant lots without a residential dwellingFront yard setback requirements for structures on 2+ acre R1 lotsAccessory structures in front yardsFlag lot / panhandle lot regulation reformMinor modification authority for staff to handle small variance requestsSpeeding up BZA resolution signing processBarndominiums and Clermont County building code requirements (crawl space)Agricultural use definition under Ohio Revised CodeTemporary living in campers during construction - code gap identifiedLot consolidation vs. separate lot accessory structuresProperty maintenance enforcement for structures without residences

Decisions (8)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
  • Direction given to not pursue allowing accessory structures on separate adjacent lots; instead require lot consolidation
  • Direction to allow a primary-use storage/garage structure on R1 lots of minimum 2 acres not in a platted subdivision
  • Direction to set front setback at 75 feet for such structures
  • Direction to not require a time limit for building a residential structure on the property
  • Direction to adopt county standards for flag/panhandle lots (25-foot minimum width, 500-foot maximum length) instead of current 150-foot frontage requirement
  • Direction to implement minor modification authority for staff at approximately 10% variance with two signatures required (zoning inspector and administrator)
  • Direction to speed up BZA resolution signing process to eliminate the 30-day wait

Action Items (10)

  • Liz Fields to draft text amendment language for primary-use structures on 2+ acre R1 lots with 75-foot front setback
  • Liz Fields to research lot sizes and verify setback compatibility with 75-foot front setback on 2-acre lots
  • Liz Fields to review county shared driveway regulations for flag lot stacking situations
  • Liz Fields to draft flag/panhandle lot code language matching county standards (25-foot width, 500-foot length)
  • Liz Fields to draft minor modification authority language at 10% with two-signature requirement
  • Legal counsel to research whether staff can issue permits after BZA approval before resolution is formally signed
  • Legal counsel to advise on speeding up BZA resolution signing process and appeal process implications
  • Staff to follow up on camper/temporary living code amendment that was previously discussed but never formally adopted
  • Julia to verify county regulations on flag lots and panhandle lots
  • Legal counsel to clarify agricultural use definition under ORC 519.01 and its administration

Financials

  • 2-acre minimum lot size proposed for primary-use structures
  • 75-foot proposed front setback for primary structures on qualifying lots
  • 150-foot current minimum lot frontage in R1 district
  • 25-foot minimum width for flag lot panhandle per county standard
  • 500-foot maximum panhandle length per county standard
  • 50-foot current front yard setback in R1
  • 35-foot current rear yard setback
  • 15-foot current side yard setback
  • 10% proposed minor modification threshold (reduced from initially discussed 15%)
  • 30-day current wait period for BZA resolution signing that trustees want eliminated

BOT 3-14-23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting featuring a significant police recognition ceremony honoring officers for drug interdiction, an armed robbery investigation, a shooting apprehension, tornado response leadership, and Officer of the Year. The board also discussed zoning code amendments with new consultant Liz Fields from McBride-Dale-Clarion, approved various motions including police extra duty pay increases and road vacation, and hired a part-time service worker.

Police recognition ceremony – Drug Interdiction Awards, Dollar General armed robbery investigation, State Route 28 shooting apprehension, tornado response leadership, Officer of the Year (Tyler Smith)Station 18 fire station funding – working through critical infrastructure code requirements with legal team; no levy or bond issue planned at this timeZoning code amendments discussion with new consultant Liz Fields (McBride-Dale-Clarion) – accessory structures on adjacent lots, accessory structures in front yards, panhandle/flag lot requirements, administrative relief for area standardsSolar farm development concerns across Clermont County – Farm Bureau briefing scheduledLucky Homes 26-home development on Oakland Road – potential 2024 Homearama siteVacation of Mustang Circle stub road for Drees Homes expansion of Villages of BelmontService garage repairs by Teasdale Fenton nearing completionBZA variance resolution process improvement to eliminate delaysScheduling of zoning workshop for March 21 with trustees, BZA, and Zoning Board members

  • Motion to approve payment of bills (Feb 28 – Mar 13, 2023) – passed
  • Motion to approve Police Policy 504 revisions raising extra duty detail pay to $50/hr for schools/mobile home parks and $65/hr for construction/other – passed
  • Motion to vacate public roadway stub on Mustang Circle for Drees Homes development – passed
  • Motion to sponsor a Salute to Leaders table at a cost of $700 – passed
  • Motion to approve ongoing payment of CDL renewals ($26.50) for service department employees – passed
  • Motion to buy back one grave from Barbara Rowland at Meyers Cemetery Section B, Lot 38, Grave 5 at $500 – passed
  • Motion to set special meeting zoning workshop for March 21 at 6:30 PM – passed
  • Motion to adjourn to executive session under ORC 121.22 G1 – passed
  • Motion to hire Chris Hilton as part-time service worker at $15.50/hr with one-year probationary period and CDL requirement – passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:19 PM – passed

  • Administrator Rose to put VVA veteran banner information on the township website

  • $50 to $65/hr increase for extra duty detail pay for construction and other details
  • $700 for Salute to Leaders table sponsorship
  • $26.50 per CDL renewal reimbursement for service department employees (every four years)

BZA 1-23-23

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held its first meeting of 2023, approving prior meeting minutes, adopting digital minutes going forward, signing resolutions for two previously approved variances, accepting the 2023 meeting schedule (moving meetings to the fourth Monday), and electing officers for the year. The board also noted openings for two alternate BZA members.

Approval of resolutions for two previously decided variance casesTransition from handwritten to digital meeting minutes2023 BZA meeting schedule adoption (fourth Monday of each month)Election of Chair, Vice Chair, and Secretary for 2023Discussion of quorum challenges and member availabilityOpenings for two alternate BZA membersBoard of Trustees working on revising the township zoning code and resolution languageZoning workshop process underway for updating land use maps and zoning resolution

  • Approved December 19, 2022 video meeting minutes (with two abstentions)
  • Approved motion to adopt digital minutes going forward
  • Approved Resolution R063-2022 (variance for Victoria Rodney Siebel at 6790 Gaynor Road for lot width relief)
  • Approved Resolution R-002-2023 (area variance for Troy E. Smith at 6676 Wood Street for accessory structure placement)
  • Accepted 2023 BZA meeting schedule moving meetings to the fourth Monday of each month
  • Elected Ed Sigla as Chair for 2023

2023-02-28 Regular Meeting contentious
1h 10m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 2-28-23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including the purchase of a digital township sign and gateway sign, police department equipment purchases, a lengthy discussion on the future of tornado-damaged Fire Station 18, and ended with a major restructuring of the zoning department including the termination of the Planning and Zoning Director and hiring of a consulting firm. A new firefighter/paramedic was sworn in and a promotion was conducted. A resident from Ryan Homes raised concerns about increased zoning permit fees and slow turnaround times.

Public safety incident earlier in the day involving police and fire departmentsNew Goshen Public Safety Scholarship announced by Steve Pegram through Goshen Education FoundationRyan Homes representative concerns about zoning permit fee increase and slow turnaround timesTownship digital sign and gateway sign purchase approvedIntoxilizer DMT purchase for police department DUI testingPolice vehicle replacement - 2023 Chevrolet BlazerSpring litter cleanup event coordination for April 22, 2023Fire Station 18 tornado damage rebuild/repair discussion - extensive code and funding issuesFEMA 50-50 rule requirements for state funding eligibilitySwearing in of firefighter/paramedic Adam Reed Daniel and promotion of Sean PhelpsTermination of Planning and Zoning Director Harry HolbrookHiring of McBride Dale Clarion as zoning consulting firmAuthorization for Administrator Rose to assist with zoning permit approvalsDecision to advertise for new full-time zoning inspectorSolar panel issues in Clermont County - Farm Bureau meeting March 16New decorative township entrance poles and signs deliveredRescue airbags and tools arriving Thursday for fire departmentEmployee Assistance Program launching March 1Goshen Township resident on American Idol - Mr. HatfieldComplimentary letter from resident about service department pothole response on Rollaway Drive

Decisions (12)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve payment of bills from February 14-27, 2023 passed unanimously
  • Motion to purchase digital sign and gateway sign from One Stop Signs with installation for $49,950 from ARP account passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve purchase of Intoxilizer DMT from Intoximeter Incorporated not to exceed $13,000 passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve purchase of 2023 Chevrolet Blazer from Dave Keil Chevrolet not to exceed $36,155 passed unanimously
  • Motion to set special meeting zoning workshop for 3-16-23 at 6 PM passed unanimously
  • Motion to enter executive session at 7:06 PM passed unanimously
  • Motion to terminate employment of Planning and Zoning Director Harry Holbrook effective immediately passed unanimously
  • Motion to hire McBride Dale Clarion as planning and zoning consultant beginning March 1, 2023 at estimated $3,500/month passed unanimously
  • Motion to authorize Administrator Rose to assist zoning inspector in roles and powers passed unanimously
  • Motion to advertise for full-time zoning inspector position passed unanimously
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:08 PM passed unanimously

  • Chief Rhoads to verify no underground utilities and have digital sign location surveyed before installation

  • Chief Myers to order Intoxilizer DMT as soon as possible

  • Chief Myers to post a synopsis on fire department Facebook page about Station 18 status and link to presentation on website

2023-02-27 Other routine
1h 3m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 2.27.23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on five variance cases. All five area variances were approved unanimously: a pole barn in a front yard that was permitted in error in 2017 (BZA 2023-001), a flag/panhandle lot split of approximately 8.5 acres from a larger parcel (BZA 2023-002), a permanent patio cover near a pool on a corner lot (BZA 2023-003), a non-permitted deck and pool too close to a property line discovered during a shed permit application (BZA 2023-004), and a building addition on an angled lot that cannot meet current residential setback requirements (BZA 2023-005). The board also noted two alternate vacancies and announced the next meeting for resolution signing on March 27th.

Pole barn built with permits issued in error in 2017 seeking retroactive varianceFlag/panhandle lot creation and stacking of panhandle lots regulationsCorner lot front yard setback issue for permanent patio cover over poolNon-permitted deck and pool discovered during shed permit applicationBuilding addition setback variance on angled lot near school propertySubdivision regulations and when public road dedication is requiredTwo alternate vacancies on the Board of Zoning Appeals

Decisions (7)

  • BZA 2023-001 approved: Area variance for existing pole barn in front yard at 6608 Goshen Road (Brandon Onkey)
  • BZA 2023-002 approved: Area variance for panhandle/flag lot split of approximately 8.5 acres (Michael Thomas, 6934 Goshen Road)
  • BZA 2023-003 approved: Area variance for permanent patio cover near pool at 648 Smith Road (Caroline)
  • BZA 2023-004 approved: Area variance for existing deck and pool too close to property line at 6773 (McKinley Jr.)
  • BZA 2023-005 approved: Area variance for building addition at 6779 Linton Road (Jamie Dyer/Geyer)
  • Minutes from January 23rd meeting approved
  • Motion to adjourn approved

Action Items (3)

  • Harry Hobart to guide approved applicants through resolution signing and permitting process
  • Applicants to attend March 27th meeting for resolution signing
  • Board seeking applications from Goshen Township residents to fill two alternate vacancies on the BZA

Financials

  • Approximately 8.5 acres proposed to be split from a 74.56-acre parcel (BZA 2023-002)
  • 25-foot wide panhandle lot frontage requirement referenced
  • 7 foot 2 inch variance requested for patio cover setback (BZA 2023-003)
  • 23.7 feet from property line for proposed patio cover
  • 10-foot setback requirement from property line for accessory structures
  • 35-foot rear setback requirement for residential properties
  • 12-foot building addition width proposed (BZA 2023-005)
  • 3-acre parcel referenced for Linton Road property

2023-02-21 Regular Meeting contentious
1h 17m ▶ Vimeo

ZC Meeting 2-21-23.mp4

The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on case ZC 2023-001, a proposal by Domino's (represented by Dryden Builders) to rezone a 2.17-acre vacant parcel at 6200 State Route 28 from residential (R3) to commercial use for a 2,036 square foot drive-thru restaurant. Residents raised significant concerns about traffic on Patricia Boulevard and State Route 28, as well as stormwater drainage issues. The commission voted to continue the case pending completion of a traffic impact study and better clarification on drainage plans. The commission also elected officers, approved previous meeting minutes, adopted 2023 meeting dates, and welcomed new member Brandon.

Rezoning of vacant land from R3 residential to B2 general business for Domino's drive-thru restaurantState Route 28 corridor plan goals and objectivesTraffic concerns on Patricia Boulevard and State Route 28 - difficulty turning left, no traffic signalStormwater detention/drainage concerns and impact on residential propertiesLot split proposal into two separate parcelsDistinction between detention vs retention ponds and underground detention alternativesODOT previously denied deceleration lane and road widening requestsElection of zoning commission officers for 2023Screening of rooftop mechanical equipmentChild safety concerns regarding detention pond near daycare/kinder care

Decisions (5)

  • Approved video/meeting minutes from November 1st hearing
  • Adopted 2023 zoning commission meeting dates
  • Elected chair (name unclear from transcript)
  • Elected vice chair (appears to be Lewis)
  • Motion to continue case ZC 2023-001 pending traffic study and drainage clarification - passed unanimously

Action Items (6)

  • Applicant (Dryden Builders/Choice One Engineering) to complete traffic impact study and submit to county engineer's office
  • Applicant to contact ODOT regarding potential impacts or access to State Route 28
  • Applicant (Caliber Engineering) to provide better clarification on stormwater drainage plan
  • Gary Holbrook to send new notification to parties regarding rescheduled Board of Trustees meeting
  • Applicant to address all 22 staff recommendations on next plan submission
  • Zoning commission seeking applications for one zoning commission member and one alternate

Financials

  • 2.17 acres total parcel size proposed to be split into 1.17 acres and 1.00 acre
  • 2,036 square foot proposed restaurant building
  • Building footprint 34 by 56 feet with 5-foot pop-out (39 x 56 overall)
  • Building height up to 18 feet
  • 7.45 miles overall corridor study length
  • Over 1,900 acres total in corridor study area
  • Approximately 4 feet of fall across the site

2023-02-14 Regular Meeting routine
1h 43m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Meeting 2.14.23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering a detailed presentation on digital signage options and locations along State Route 28, approved several personnel and training motions, selected a new Employee Assistance Program provider, accepted a firefighter's retirement, and discussed zoning changes including agricultural district designations and accessory structure regulations. The board also heard communications including commendation letters for police and zoning staff, and updates on EMS billing revenue increases.

Digital township signage - detailed presentation on locations, size, visibility, and costs for LED sign on State Route 28Gateway sign for Goshen Township at Deerfield Road/State Route 28 entranceEmployee Assistance Program provider selection (replacing Anthem with The Standard)Agricultural zoning designations (AG-1 and AG-2 proposals for farmland protection)Accessory structure zoning regulations and variance concernsPolice department CALEA accreditation process underwayRetirement of part-time firefighter Bill WaitsSRO conference training approvals for school resource officersCemetery grave buybackLocal Government Fund advocacy through Clermont County Township AssociationAmerican Regulators selected as Salute to Leaders honoree (March 29 at the Oasis)Village of Belmont development and State Route 28 access improvementsStation 18 building assessment findings forthcomingEMS billing revenue increase report from Medi-Count

  • Approved appropriation of $43.49 to line item 2251.210.1120000 for police salaries to close out federal account
  • Approved SRO Groves and SRO Alcorn to attend Ohio School Resource Officers Conference in Sandusky ($640 per officer)

  • Approved The Standard Option 2 as new Employee Assistance Program provider at $0.60 per employee per month effective March 1
  • Accepted retirement of part-time firefighter William (Bill) Waits effective 2-20-2023
  • Approved Chief Myers to attend Andy Frederick's Training Days in Lexington, KY March 20-22 (not to exceed $550)

  • Chief Rose to conduct plywood visibility test and investigate sign placement between utility poles near State Route 28/Goshen Road, including Duke Energy electric meter and ODOT coordination

  • $330 per call EMS billing collection rate (up $6.35 from $324)
  • 84 additional EMS transports over prior year

  • $50,000 bidding threshold for township purchases

  • $600 per officer registration for National SRO Conference

  • $0.35 per employee per month for Standard EAP Option 1 ($32.55/month)
  • $3,000 per year flat rate for Medical Mutual EAP alternative
  • $95 per month previously paid for Anthem EAP
  • $550 not-to-exceed for Chief Myers training in Lexington, KY

2023-01-31 Work Session routine
1h 1m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 1.31.22 2 of 2.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a zoning workshop focused on potential text and map amendments to the zoning code, particularly in the Northeast and Southeast quadrants. Key discussions centered on separating Agricultural (AG) and R1 zoning districts, establishing a two-acre minimum lot size for R1 in the Northeast quadrant, addressing solar farm regulations in coordination with Clermont County Commissioners, and increasing minimum lot sizes and setback requirements in Planned Unit Developments (PUDs).

Separation of Agricultural (AG) and R1 zoning districtsTwo-acre minimum lot size for R1 in the Northeast quadrantSolar farm regulation and Senate Bill 52 county authorityPUD lot size minimums and setback requirementsNortheast and Southeast quadrant zoning focus areasAffordable housing concerns vs. overcrowding preventionAgricultural preservation district and overlay district history

Decisions (4)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed
  • Direction given to pursue two-acre minimum lot size for R1 zoning in the Northeast quadrant
  • Direction given to separate AG and R1 zoning districts
  • Direction to add solar farm discussion to the next Zoning Commission meeting on February 7th

Action Items (5)

  • Director Holbert and staff to determine the best zoning label/approach for two-acre lots in the Northeast quadrant
  • Harry (Zoning) to add solar farm issue to the February 7th Zoning Commission meeting agenda
  • Fiscal Officer/staff to research legal authority on minimum lot sizes and AG zoning requirements under ORC
  • Staff to get direction from prosecutor's office on legality of five-acre and larger lot requirements and AG/R1 separation
  • Staff to examine PUD minimum lot size standards and side yard setback requirements for potential increases

Financials

  • 290 lots of 5 acres or more in the Northeast quadrant
  • 14 lots in R3 zone district ranging from 1.8 to 25 acres
  • Current PUD minimum lot size is 5,000 square feet
  • R3 lots are approximately 12,800 square feet
  • Current side yard setback is 10 feet
  • Current front setback is 50 feet with discussion of increasing to 60 feet
  • Goshen Township AG standard is 5 acres; ORC AG standard is 10 acres
  • Proposed R1 minimum lot size of 2 acres in Northeast quadrant

2022-01-31 Work Session routine
30m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 1.31.22 1 of 2.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a work session focused on zoning discussions, particularly PUD (Planned Unit Development) lot sizes, setbacks, and minimum house square footage requirements. Trustees and the zoning official worked through mathematical scenarios for lot dimensions, setbacks, and building footprints, ultimately discussing increasing the minimum PUD lot size from 5,000 to 6,600 square feet (60x110). They also prioritized upcoming zoning topics including accessory structures in front yards, the zone change process, and other hot-button issues for future meetings.

PUD lot size minimum increase from 5,000 to 6,600 square feetBuilding setback requirements and their impact on lot size and house sizeRanch vs. two-story home feasibility on smaller lotsAccessory structures in front yardsAccessory structures on vacant lots without principal structuresPrivacy fences in front yardsZone change process improvements to speed up approvalsTemporary structures during new home constructionFuture topics: tiny homes, Airbnb, food trucks, solar farms, corner lots, business in residential zones

Decisions (1)

  • Motion to adjourn at 7:47 PM passed unanimously

Action Items (8)

  • Harry to prepare presentations on lot sizes, setbacks, and house size scenarios for different lot configurations
  • Harry to provide his top five hottest zoning complaint topics via email to help prioritize future discussions
  • Accessory structures in the front yard to be the primary topic at the next trustees meeting
  • Zone change process to also be discussed at the next meeting
  • Harry to find two more priority topics beyond accessory structures and zone change process for next meeting
  • Research needed on allowing accessory structures on vacant lots without a principal structure
  • Harry to send out previously prepared language on temporary structures for trustee review
  • Determine whether to bundle temporary structure language with other zoning changes or process separately

Financials

  • 5,000 sq ft - current minimum PUD lot size considered too small
  • 6,600 sq ft (60x110) - proposed new minimum PUD lot size
  • 1,400 sq ft - current minimum house square footage requirement
  • 1,600 sq ft - maximum house footprint on a 6,600 sq ft lot with standard setbacks
  • 400 sq ft - approximate two-car garage footprint (20x20)
  • 35 ft - front and rear yard setbacks for township roads
  • 10 ft - proposed side yard setbacks
  • 50 ft - suggested minimum rear yard setback discussed by trustees
  • 1,800 sq ft - building footprint needed on a 60x120 lot including garage

2022-12-19 Other routine
39m ▶ Vimeo

BZA Meeting 12-19-22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2022-024, a variance request by Troy E. Smith to place an accessory structure (personal garage) in the front yard of a corner lot in the R3 zoning district. Staff explained the property's corner lot designation creates two front yards, requiring a 50-foot setback from the Ethel Lynn right-of-way. The board discussed topography concerns and ultimately approved a 32-foot variance allowing the structure to be aligned with an existing gazebo, 18 feet from the property line. The applicant was not present at the meeting.

Variance request for accessory structure placement in front yard of corner lot (BZA 2022-024)Corner lot definition and dual front yard requirementsEthel Lynn road status - dedicated county right-of-way but not built to county standards and unmaintainedTopography concerns with natural drainage on the lot affecting structure placementChristman Subdivision plat dedication of Plum Street, Wood Street, and Ethel LynnDiscussion of paper street concept and its implications for nonconforming properties

Decisions (3)

  • Approved video meeting minutes from previous meeting
  • Approved a 32-foot variance on BZA 2022-024 for Troy E. Smith, allowing the proposed accessory structure to be aligned with the existing gazebo at 18 feet from the Ethel Lynn right-of-way property line
  • Motion to close the Board of Zoning Appeals meeting approved

Action Items (2)

  • Hobart to send out meeting calendars once finalized
  • Troy E. Smith to comply with the approved variance conditions, aligning the proposed structure with the gazebo

Financials

  • 50-foot front yard setback required by zoning code
  • 32-foot variance granted (from 50-foot requirement to 18 feet from property line)
  • 18 feet from property line to gazebo (reference point for approved variance)
  • 25 feet from edge of gravel pavement to property line
  • 50-foot right-of-way on Ethel Lynn
  • 10-foot distance proposed by applicant from county right-of-way to structure
  • 20-foot height of proposed structure
  • 32-foot length of proposed structure
  • 16 by 18 foot existing shed to be removed

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held its first meeting of 2023, handling old business including approval of two resolutions for previously decided variances, adopting a new 2023 meeting schedule moving meetings to the fourth Monday of each month, transitioning to digital meeting minutes, and electing officers for the year. The board also noted openings for two alternate BZA members.

  • Approved Resolution R-002-2023 (area variance for Troy E. Smith at 6676 Wood Street for accessory structure placement)

BOT 01-24-23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering multiple agenda items including changing the 2023 meeting start time to 6 PM, appropriating ARPA funds for a roof project, purchasing a new police department copier, facilitating AED purchases for the school district, discussing solar farm concerns in the county, and making several fire department personnel appointments. The board also approved purchasing 12 decorative gateway signs for the township and discussed a larger digital monument sign for a future decision.

  • Approved payment of bills for January 10-23, 2023

  • Approved trade-in of old police copier and purchase of new copier not to exceed $6,400
  • Approved motion to increase revenue $10,301.40 for EMS supplies (AEDs for school district)
  • Approved motion to increase appropriations $10,301.40 for EMS supplies line item
  • Adjourned into executive session at 7:29 PM
  • Appointed Shawn Weutenant as full-time firefighter paramedic effective January 29, 2023 at $26.71/hour

  • Approved purchase of 12 decorative gateway signs with posts not to exceed $14,000 from ARPA funds

2023-01-17 Special Meeting routine
2h 4m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Special Meeting 1-17-23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special workshop meeting to discuss potential changes to the township's zoning code, focusing on protecting agricultural land and increasing minimum lot sizes. The discussion centered on creating a new agricultural zoning district (AG1) separate from the existing R1 residential classification, increasing minimum lot sizes for Planned Unit Developments (PUDs) from the current 5,000 square feet, and potentially raising minimum house sizes. Zoning Director Harry presented the current land use map, overlay districts, and zoning standards, while Prosecutor Julia Brandt advised on legal approaches including creating a new AG1 zoning district rather than using overlay districts.

Creation of a new AG1 (agricultural) zoning district separate from R1 residentialIncreasing minimum lot sizes for PUD developments above 5,000 square feetState Route 28 corridor overlay district reviewAgricultural preservation for northeast and southeast quadrants of the townshipMinimum house size requirements for PUDsDifference between overlay districts and standard zoning district changesNon-conforming lot concerns when changing zoning designationsSeparation of agricultural and residential zoning classificationsMound septic systems versus older leach line systems and lot size implicationsSubdivision regulations and lot split requirements for parcels over five acres

Decisions (1)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously

Action Items (7)

  • Harry to contact Clermont County GIS to obtain a parcel breakdown of all properties five acres and above in the northeast and southeast quadrants
  • Harry to research what other townships (Batavia, Pierce, and surrounding areas) have done with agricultural zoning standards
  • Harry to prepare site designs showing lot sizes, setbacks, and house footprints to determine appropriate minimum PUD lot sizes
  • Harry to provide enlarged quadrant maps with lot sizes for the next meeting
  • Julia (prosecutor's office) to research what other townships use for agricultural zoning districts and minimum lot sizes
  • Harry to work with the prosecutor's office on draft verbiage for proposed zoning text amendments
  • Harry to provide the board with numbers based on lot sizes and home sizes for PUDs

Financials

  • Current PUD minimum lot size: 5,000 square feet
  • Current R1 minimum lot size: approximately 40,000 square feet (about 1 acre)
  • 1 acre = 43,560 square feet
  • Current minimum house size: 1,400 square feet (referenced as 1,405 sq ft)
  • Proportional house size on 7,000 sq ft lot would be approximately 1,960 square feet
  • Quarter acre lot = 10,890 square feet
  • Half acre lot = 21,780 square feet
  • Pelosi property: 151.58 acres
  • One referenced property: 27 acres; another: 98 acres

2023-01-10 Regular Meeting routine
1h 5m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Meeting 1.10.23.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their first regular meeting of 2023, serving as the organizational meeting for the new year. Key actions included electing Bob Hauserman as Board Chairperson and Dan Hodges as Vice Chairperson, approving the 2023 meeting schedule, appropriating $100,000 for ongoing tornado-related expenses, and authorizing the Township Administrator to sign contracts related to three Brownfield assessment grants totaling $387,700. The board also discussed gateway signage improvements, phone system upgrades using ARP funds, and scheduled a special workshop for January 17th to discuss potential zoning code changes.

Organizational meeting: election of 2023 Board Chairperson and Vice ChairpersonGateway signage design selection and pricing discussionTownship branding improvements (signs, window stickers, building signage)Parking lot lighting pole installation updateTornado damage ongoing expenses and state reimbursement submissionStation 18 repair drawings and cost estimate timelinePhone system upgrade using ARP fundsBrownfield assessment grants ($387,700) awarded from Ohio Department of Development for three propertiesAppalachia grant application update ($29 million in township projects submitted)MARCS grant for radio replacements saving approximately $22,000Zoning code workshop scheduled for January 17 to discuss text/map changes and overlay districtsPersonnel changes: resignations of Chris Miller, Lt. Doug England (staying part-time), and Don AtkinsLiquid salt application system research for service trucks2023 meeting schedule and tax budget hearing date setExecutive session regarding public employee matters

Decisions (23)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (Nov 29, 2022 – Jan 9, 2023) – passed
  • Bob Hauserman nominated and elected as Board of Trustees Chairperson for 2023 (Hauserman abstained)
  • Dan Hodges nominated and elected as Vice Chairperson for 2023 (Hodges abstained)
  • Motion to adopt 2023 Board of Trustees regular meeting schedule – passed
  • Motion to set zoning meetings at 6 PM on trustee meeting nights – passed
  • Motion to schedule 2023 July tax budget hearing for June 27, 2023 at 6 PM – passed
  • Motion to set 2023 standard mileage rate at $0.655 per business mile – passed
  • Motion to approve sale of surplus items on govdeals.com (Resolution R001-2023) – passed
  • Motion to authorize Township Administrator to enter agreements with UltraFiber, CBTS, and ATC for phone/internet/cable and use ARP funds for phone system upgrades – passed
  • Motion to appropriate $100,000 in general fund for tornado-related expenses – passed
  • Motion to schedule special workshop meeting for January 17, 2023 at 6 PM for zoning discussion – passed
  • Motion to accept $200 donation from Garrett Boyd to the fire department – passed
  • Motion to accept resignation of firefighter/paramedic Chris Miller effective 12-26-22 – passed
  • Motion to accept resignation of Lieutenant Doug England as full-time and appoint as part-time firefighter/paramedic – passed
  • Motion to table item O (Sergeant West travel to hostage negotiations training) – passed
  • Motion to set 2023 Zoning Commission members' compensation at $50/month (not to exceed $599) – passed
  • Motion to set 2023 Board of Zoning Appeals members' compensation at $50/month (not to exceed $599) – passed
  • Motion to authorize Township Administrator to sign Brownfield grant engineering contracts – passed
  • Motion to approve 2023 road mileage for Clermont County Engineer at 47.062 miles – passed
  • Motion to accept resignation of part-time service employee Don Atkins effective 12-22-22 – passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session per ORC 121.22 G1 – passed
  • Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 9:27 PM – passed

Action Items (12)

  • Administrator Rose and Director Meadows to research pricing for larger gateway signs (option A style) and present firm numbers/images at next meeting
  • Administrator Rose and Director Meadows to map out recommended locations for gateway signs
  • Director Meadows to get pricing on a large welcome sign (e.g., across from the Bowling Alley)
  • Administrator Rose to follow up with Wage War on window stickers for branding
  • Director Meadows to contact vendor about parking lot light pole installation
  • Chief Myers to provide Station 18 drawings to construction manager for cost estimate (expected mid-February)

2022-12-06 Regular Meeting routine
1h 0m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 12.6.2022.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their final regular meeting of 2022, covering dental insurance renewal, digital signage design, FEMA grant acceptance for fire department PPE, purchase of tactical shields for police, zoning fee schedule updates, and a year-end employee bonus. The board renewed dental insurance with Superior Dental Care at a decreased rate, approved purchasing two tactical shields using ARP funds, updated zoning fees excluding certain BZA residential categories, and approved a $300 one-time bonus for all township employees in recognition of their work during COVID-19 and the July 2022 tornado response.

Digital signage design selection for township buildingDental insurance renewal with Superior Dental Care - in-network vs out-of-network issuesFEMA COVID-19 supplemental grant for fire department PPETactical shield purchase for police officers using ARP fundsZoning fee schedule update with residential BZA fees excluded from increasesAppalachian Grant application including Linton Road connector and sidewalksBrownfield grant update - favorable ruling from Judge BrockStation 18 updateOPWC grant delay for Gibson and Thompson Road paving2022 revenue true-up2023 health care premium split increase to 12% employeeCommunity room improvements with donated tablesOne-time $300 employee bonus for COVID and tornado responseAnnual organization meeting set for January 10, 2023December 20 meeting canceled

Decisions (24)

  • Adopted the agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for November 29 - December 5, 2022
  • Tabled recognition of a hero to a later date due to illness
  • Accepted $100 cash donation from Jamie Ramsey for green shifts (fire department)
  • Accepted FEMA AFG-S COVID-19 supplemental grant of $11,964.58
  • Increased revenue in fire EMS fund by $11,964.58
  • Increased appropriations in fire EMS fund by $11,964.58
  • Approved out-of-town travel for Fire Chief Edward Myers to attend instructors conference in Indianapolis April 24-29, 2023, not to exceed $1,500
  • Accepted $125 Kroger gift card donation for fire department from anonymous donor
  • Amended agenda to move dental insurance item up
  • Renewed dental insurance with Superior Dental Care for 2023 per revised renewal (decrease of $3,034)
  • Accepted $125 Kroger gift card donation for service department from anonymous donor
  • Appropriated $14,400 in ARP funds for police equipment
  • Approved purchase of two tactical shields and cases from Baker Ballistics not to exceed $14,400
  • Accepted $125 Kroger gift card donation for police department from anonymous donor
  • Updated zoning fee schedule excluding BZA residential property maintenance appeal, appeals increase, and variance increase
  • Set annual organization meeting for January 10, 2023 at 7 PM
  • Set 2023 health care premium split at 12% employee / 88% employer
  • Accepted 10 tables for community room donated by Goshen CPAAA and Goshen Lions Club
  • Requested advance in taxes collected by Clermont County Auditor for 2023
  • Decreased appropriations in road and bridge fund by $237,449 due to OPWC grant not received in 2022
  • Approved 2022 township revised revenue true-up with overall increase of $380,301.63
  • Approved one-time $300 bonus for all township employees from department funds payable before end of 2022
  • Adjourned at 8:10 PM

Action Items (9)

  • Administrator Rose to request larger version of second digital sign design from vendor for comparison
  • Administrator Rose to share Dan's original sign column photos for reference
  • Amanda Wickersham / Michaela to distribute link for Superior Dental Care in-network provider search to employees
  • Harry Holbrook to submit Appalachian Grant application by Friday deadline
  • Harry Holbrook to continue working on streetscape and Main Street improvement pricing with county and Chad
  • Administrator Rose to follow up on Brownfield grant email regarding 1826 Main Street
  • Chad to get new truck ready and order tools
  • Laura/Fiscal Officer to process $300 employee bonuses before end of 2022
  • Administrator Rose to purchase chair carts and new chairs for community room after January 1 using $2,000 ARP funds

Financials

  • $47,250 for digital sign (either design option)
  • $11,964.58 FEMA AFG-S COVID-19 supplemental grant for PPE/gloves
  • $1,500 maximum for Fire Chief travel to instructors conference
  • $14,400 in ARP funds for two tactical shields and cases from Baker Ballistics
  • $100 cash donation from Jamie Ramsey to fire department
  • $125 Kroger gift cards donated anonymously to fire, service, and police departments (3 total)
  • $3,034 decrease in dental insurance renewal with Superior Dental Care
  • $237,449 decrease in road and bridge appropriations (OPWC grant delayed to 2023)
  • $380,301.63 overall increase in 2022 revised revenue
  • $300 one-time bonus for all township employees
  • 12% employee / 88% employer health care premium split for 2023
  • $2,000 budgeted in ARP funds for community room chairs and carts
  • $54 million opened by governor for cleanup sites

BOT 11.29.22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering police department recognition for an aggravated murder case arrest, retirement of K-9 unit 6, approval of tactical shield purchases for officer safety, termination of the tornado state of emergency from July 2022, adoption of revised cemetery fees, renewal of healthcare benefits with a 0% increase, and approval of a new FOP labor agreement. The board also discussed a temporary dwelling zoning text amendment and approved various administrative travel and appointments.

Recognition of police department for November 10 aggravated murder/shooting arrestK-9 Unit 6 retirement ceremony after spinal injuryBallistic vest donation for K-9 Kircher from Blue Line Canine AssociationTactical shield purchase for officer safety due to uptick in violent crimeZoning text amendment for temporary dwelling units during home construction and acts of natureTermination of state of emergency from July 6, 2022 tornadoRevised cemetery fee schedule to address fund shortfall2023 healthcare benefits renewal with 0% premium increaseFOP police sergeants labor agreement 2023-2025Elected officials and non-union wage schedule for 2023Service department snow plow painting program with local high schoolStation 18 renovation update with architect meetingLight Up Goshen parade participationClermont County Township Association meeting topics including indigent burials

2022-11-29 Special Meeting routine
17m ▶ Vimeo

BOT Special Meeting 11.29.22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting public hearing on zoning case ZZ2022-006, a major adjustment to an approved PUD (Planned Unit Development) for the Lakefield Place development on State Route 132. The township zoning staff presented the case, noting the Zoning Commission had recommended approval with conditions by a 3-0 vote. The Board discussed lot sizes, a proposed 2.4-acre pond feature, and ultimately approved the major adjustment. The meeting adjourned at 6:20 PM.

Public hearing on zoning case ZZ2022-006 - major adjustment to approved PUD for Lakefield Place developmentZoning Commission recommendation of approval with conditions (3-0 vote)Lot size consistency and compliance with PUD standardsProposed 2.4-acre pond/lake feature and amenity recommendations including water fountain and aeratorsDrainage considerations related to Clermont County clay soil

Decisions (3)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed (Hodges yes, Hauserman yes)
  • Zoning case ZZ2022-006 major adjustment to PUD for Lakefield Place approved with conditions (Hodges yes, Hauserman yes)
  • Motion to adjourn passed (Hodges yes, Hauserman yes)

Action Items (3)

  • Applicant (Second Lakefield Place Development LLC / Abercrombie & Associates / Scott Humphries) to follow steps required by Section 6.02 for preliminary and final development plan approvals
  • Applicant to ensure all proposed buildings meet already approved building setback lines
  • Previous conditions of approval and layout to remain for lots other than 115 through 157

Financials

  • Minimum PUD lot size standard: 5,000 sq ft
  • Minimum lot size in this development: approximately 8,000 sq ft
  • Lot sizes range from approximately 8,000 sq ft to over 20,000 sq ft
  • Proposed pond/lake feature approximately 2.4 acres

2022-11-21 Other contentious
31m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 11.21.22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) held a public hearing to address several zoning matters including a rezoning request for a property at 6823 Goshen Road, a variance request (BZA 22-021) for a property at 1330 Ansel Road, and a finding of fact and conclusion of law regarding a school project conditional use request (BZA 2022-022) at 6696 Goshen Road. Public comments were heard, particularly from residents concerned about safety and notification issues related to the school district's plan to lease space for business use. Multiple resolutions were voted on and approved by the board.

Rezoning request for 6823 Goshen RoadVariance request BZA 22-021 for 1330 Ansel RoadSchool project conditional use request BZA 2022-022 at 6696 Goshen RoadPublic concerns about school district leasing building space to businessesConcerns about inadequate public notification of the school project zoning matterSafety concerns regarding students accessing off-site leased facilities

Decisions (4)

  • Minutes from prior meeting approved
  • Resolution regarding rezoning of 6823 Goshen Road (parcel 2207I272) approved
  • Resolution approving variance request BZA 22-021 for 1330 Ansel Road approved
  • Finding of fact and conclusion of law for BZA 2022-022 (school project conditional use at 6696 Goshen Road) approved

Action Items (2)

  • Next meeting scheduled for December 19th
  • Board to consider public safety concerns raised about the school district leasing space for business use

Financials

2022-11-08 Regular Meeting routine
1h 29m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 11-8-22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including a presentation by an Eagle Scout candidate for a flag retirement box, updates on commercial development along State Route 28, a detailed discussion of the Appalachian Community Grant Program ($500 million state program), and presentation and approval of the 2023 township budget. The board also canceled the December 20 meeting, approved credit card authorization changes, promoted Alyssa Hundley to zoning inspector, and discussed plans for a digital sign and ongoing infrastructure projects.

Eagle Scout flag retirement box project approved for administrative buildingAppalachian Community Grant Program ($500M) - partnership with school district and regional counties2023 township budget presentation and approval across all departmentsCommercial development updates along State Route 28 corridorDigital sign project for corner of SR 28 and Goshen RoadStreet sign replacement program progress and issuesSalt barn roof installationStation 18 fire station project moving forward with architectRadio tower generator installation and cutover planningService garage door additionsNew HR manager Michaela Young startedPolice FOP sergeants union contract negotiationsSnowplow painting contest with school students for Holly Fair paradePrivate drives vs township roads clarification effortPotential state public assistance funding for tornado reimbursementCancellation of December 20 meetingPromotion of Alyssa Hundley to zoning inspectorHolly Fair event November 19Field fires and burn ban concernsOver 1,000 homes in development pipeline across subdivisions

Decisions (10)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (Oct 25 - Nov 7, 2022) passed unanimously
  • Motion to allow Eagle Scout flag retirement box to be built inside the administrative building passed unanimously
  • Motion to cancel the December 20, 2022 regular board of trustees meeting passed unanimously
  • Motion to remove Steve Pegram and Cheryl Allgaier and add Alyssa Hundley and Michaela Young as authorized credit card users passed unanimously
  • Motion to direct Administrator Rose to attend Sunshine Law certified training on behalf of elected officials passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve FOP extension agreement waiving ORC 4117.14G11 provisions retroactive to January 1, 2023 passed unanimously
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:48 PM passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve the 2023 adopted budget as presented passed unanimously
  • Motion to promote Alyssa Hundley to zoning inspector effective November 20, 2022 at $22/hour with six-month probation passed unanimously

Action Items (12)

  • Administrator Rose to reach out to Goshen Local Schools to partner on Appalachian Community Grant application
  • Harry and Bob to work together with the school district on the grant pre-pre-application due December 9
  • Administrator Rose to get pricing from Choice One Engineering for grant writing assistance

  • Administrator Rose to get additional digital sign design options incorporating township logo
  • Dan and Harry to work with prosecutor Julia on clarifying dedicated right-of-way vs dedicated road for private drives
  • Administrator Rose and Chief Rose to coordinate radio tower cutover from Patricia Tower
  • Nathan Rose (Eagle Scout) to build flag retirement box inside administrative building by end of January
  • Ed to coordinate face-to-face meeting on Station 18 project with architect on Nov 17 or 18
  • Chad to follow up with Kleens on street sign errors (e.g., Angelwood Drive vs Lane)

BOT 10-25-22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting that included an executive session to discuss employee appointment/compensation, addressed multiple property nuisance resolutions with some confusion over resolution numbering, heard community announcements about tornado recovery efforts, and discussed upcoming community events. The board also accepted a firefighter resignation, appointed a volunteer firefighter, and heard a presentation from a resident interested in starting a winery/event venue on Smith Road.

2022-10-17 Special Meeting contentious
2h 23m ▶ Vimeo

BZA 10.17.2022.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on three cases: BZA 2022-011 (an appeal regarding an illegal six-foot privacy fence in a front yard at 6823 Goshen Road), BZA 2022-012 (an appeal of a stop work order on an unauthorized commercial truck repair business operating in an R3 residential zone at the same address), and BZA 2022-023 (an area variance request for a panhandle/flag lot split at 6790 Gainer Road to allow a family member to build a house). The board denied both appeals for the Kelly property cases and began hearing the lot split variance case.

Appeal of fence denial - six-foot privacy fence installed without permit in front yard at 6823 Goshen RoadAppeal of stop work order on unauthorized truck repair business operating in R3 residential zoneDispute over prior zoning director Bob Seifried's interpretation and approval of agricultural exemption for commercial useWhether a new zoning director can reverse a prior director's interpretation and enforcement decisionsZone change from R3 to M2 previously denied by Clermont County Planning Commission, Goshen Township Zoning Commission, and Board of TrusteesArea variance request for panhandle/flag lot split at 6790 Gainer RoadStacking of panhandle lots and road frontage requirements in R1 zoningDifference between county auditor property classification and township zoning classification

Decisions (3)

  • Motion to approve previous meeting minutes passed (Curie: Yes, Seagla: Yes, Schall: Abstain, Theodore: Yes)
  • Motion to deny appeal for BZA 2022-011 (fence at 6823 Goshen Road) passed unanimously (Curie: Yes, Siegler: Yes, Shaw: Yes, Theodore: Yes)
  • Motion to deny appeal for BZA 2022-012 (stop work order on business at 6823 Goshen Road) passed unanimously (Curie: Yes, Seagla: Yes, Shaw: Yes, Theodore: Yes)

Action Items (2)

  • BZA to continue hearing and deliberate on BZA 2022-023 (Seabolt/Edwards lot split variance at 6790 Gainer Road)
  • Jeffrey Kelly's attorney indicated intent to appeal adverse decisions to the Court of Common Pleas

Financials

  • $229 per year in taxes on Kelly property before business operation
  • $3,500 per year in taxes after business classification change
  • $4,300 in taxes for 2022 on Kelly property
  • 14,000 square feet of parking area at Kelly business
  • 60 by 80 foot building (plus 12 foot lean-to) at Kelly property
  • 70 acres total for Seabolt property at 6790 Gainer Road
  • 6-acre, 16-acre, and 48-acre proposed lot splits at Gainer Road
  • 150-foot minimum lot width required in R1 zoning
  • 28-foot setback of Kelly building from property line (100 feet required)
  • 75-foot setback required to residential district for commercial development

2022-10-11 Regular Meeting contentious
1h 1m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 10.11.22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering hiring of multiple part-time firefighters/EMTs, approval of the 2023 property casualty insurance renewal at $105,528 (a 3% increase), and a detailed presentation of the draft 2023 budget. The board also accepted the resignation of Township Administrator Steve, appointed Bobby Rose as both Township Administrator and Police Chief, and approved a new Human Resource Manager job description following the resignation of the finance and HR director.

Hiring of four part-time firefighters/EMTs to fill part-time ranks2023 property casualty insurance renewal with Ohio PlanDetailed draft 2023 budget presentation across all township fundsTornado recovery expenses and pending state disaster assistance reimbursementRoad and bridge fund financial concerns and declining revenue from gas tax and vehicle registrationsFire and ambulance levy expiring in 2024 requiring renewal on 2023 ballotLarge residential subdivision development off State Route 48 (1-3 million dollar homes)Barkley Wood Section 2 major PUD adjustment zoning case scheduledResignation of Township Administrator SteveAppointment of Bobby Rose as Township Administrator and Police ChiefResignation of Finance and HR Director and creation of separate HR Manager positionStation 18 temporary operations and generator installation pendingThree officers completing probationary period (Item, McMillan/Detective, Baron)CPA graduation ceremony October 19Holly Fair and parade November 19Street sign U-channel post replacement (silver color selected)Snowplow rodeo participation with OPWSO group

Decisions (14)

  • Motion to amend agenda to change item C dollar amount from $15.50 to $18 - passed
  • Motion to adopt agenda as amended - passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills from September 28 through October 10, 2022 - passed
  • Motion to hire part-time firefighter/paramedic Ian Smith at $18/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
  • Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Elijah Man at $15.50/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
  • Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Shaler Newman at $15.50/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
  • Motion to hire part-time firefighter/EMT Jerry Burns at $15.50/hour effective 10/23/22 - passed
  • Motion to accept $150 and food gift cards from Mounts Christian Church for police - passed
  • Motion to approve 2023 property casualty insurance renewal with Ohio Plan at $105,528 - passed
  • Motion to approve resignation of Township Administrator Steve - passed
  • Motion to approve job description for position of Police Chief and Township Administrator - passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-53-2022 appointing Bobby Rose as Township Administrator and Police Chief - passed
  • Motion to approve job description for Human Resource Manager position and proceed with interviews - passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 9:15 PM - passed

  • Chad to contact Hannah about firm date for debris pile removal
  • Chad to trim tree line hanging over property near firehouse
  • Chad to assess property cleanup once debris pile is removed

  • Harry to continue monitoring Claremont subdivision development at county level
  • Harry to prepare zoning case for Barkley Wood Section 2 major adjustment for November 1 zoning commission and November 29 trustees meeting

  • Steve/Cheryl to redo cemetery fee comparison with surrounding townships
  • Board to review draft 2023 budget over next two weeks for potential approval
  • Board to begin planning fire and ambulance levy renewal for 2023 ballot (levy expires 2024)
  • Post and interview candidates for Human Resource Manager position
  • Chad to purchase silver U-channel posts for street sign replacements

  • $18/hour - pay rate for part-time firefighter/paramedic Ian Smith

2022-09-27 Regular Meeting routine
42m ▶ Vimeo

Reg Mtg BPT 9.27.22

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on September 27, 2022. Duke Energy presented a $50,000 check to the Lions Club and a $10,000 check to the Goshen CPAAA for tornado relief efforts, with 48 families assisted to date. The board approved resolutions related to the Ohio opioid settlement, declared a surplus vehicle for sale, approved police training, rescinded a firefighter appointment, authorized a suspension, and designated October 31 as Halloween night. Updates were provided on ongoing tornado recovery including Station 18 repairs, fence repairs at the PD lot, and insurance work.

Duke Energy tornado relief donations to Lions Club and CPAAAOngoing tornado recovery and infrastructure repairs (Station 18, PD fence, roof, tower)Ohio opioid settlement participation and Region 14 governanceSurplus vehicle declaration (2002 Ford Ranger)Police advanced training approvalExecutive session - employee discipline mattersRescission of part-time firefighter paramedic appointmentTwo-shift unpaid suspension of full-time firefighterHalloween designation for October 31, 2022Linton-Goshen Road connector preliminary design discussionZoning inspector position posted with two applications receivedCemetery lot repurchase

  • Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills (Sept 14-27, 2022) - passed
  • Motion to declare surplus and sell 2002 Ford Ranger 4x2 pickup truck on govdeals.com - passed
  • Motion to approve repurchase of Lot 342, Grave Number 1, Section A at $500 - passed
  • Motion to approve out-of-town training for Detective McMillan and Officer Varon for advanced interviewing and interrogation techniques ($720 each) - passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-052-2022 approving participation in Region 14 governance structure under Ohio One Ohio MOU - passed
  • Motion to approve Resolution R-053-2022 approving initial Region 14 representative to Ohio One Ohio Recovery Foundation Inc. Board - passed
  • Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:39 PM - passed
  • Motion to rescind January 30, 2022 contingent appointment of part-time firefighter paramedic Cody Bryan - passed
  • Motion to authorize two-shift unpaid suspension of a full-time firefighter - passed
  • Motion to designate October 31, 2022 as Halloween night - passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:51 PM - passed

Action Items (9)

  • Harry to review zoning inspector position applications with the board
  • Dan to coordinate AC and breathing air system inspections at Station 18 before disconnecting generator and portable AC
  • Dan to follow up on generator delivery for the tower project
  • Dan to finalize temporary station setup (phone and cable pending)
  • Cheryl to continue working on property casualty insurance renewal and health care renewal
  • Cheryl to coordinate remaining roof and building repairs at administration building
  • Dan to get the new service department truck completed and in service by mid-October
  • Bob to follow up on Public Assistance Program reimbursement through Ohio EMA
  • Board to determine a safe location for Halloween event given tornado damage

Financials

  • $50,000 - Duke Energy donation to Lions Club for tornado relief
  • $10,000 - Duke Energy donation to Goshen CPAAA for tornado relief
  • $500 - repurchase price for cemetery Lot 342, Grave 1, Section A
  • $720 each - cost for Detective McMillan and Officer Varon training
  • $808 million - Ohio's total opioid settlement amount

2022-08-16 Special Meeting routine
35m ▶ Vimeo

Spc BOT Mtg 8.16.22.mp4

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a special meeting focused on tornado recovery updates, personnel actions for the fire department, and the purchase of property to expand the government campus. The board approved several new part-time firefighter hires, accepted donations for tornado recovery, discussed long-term recovery committee efforts affecting 298 damaged structures in Goshen Township, and authorized the purchase of property at 6765 Goshen Road for $125,000 to expand the township government complex. A temporary firehouse plan using a mobile home at the old police station site was also discussed.

Tornado recovery updates and long-term recovery committee progressDamage assessment: 451 structures affected, 298 in Goshen TownshipState aid threshold not met (16 of 25 needed heavily damaged/uninsured properties)Property purchase at 6765 Goshen Road for government campus expansionTemporary firehouse setup using mobile home at old police stationFire department staffing: one resignation and three new hiresMonument sign replacement options ($39K-$51K range)Radio tower repair timeline (4-5 weeks to power up)CPA class number 12 started with 13 participantsICMA conference travel approvalVintage baseball fundraiser game August 20thScene 75 bowling donation event for tornado relief

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for August 3-16, 2022
  • Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Brad Taylor effective 8/5/22
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT-B Colin Westman at $15.50/hour effective 8/28/22
  • Hired part-time firefighter EMT-B Scott Harley at $15.50/hour effective 8/28/22
  • Hired part-time firefighter paramedic (West) at $17/hour effective 8/28/22
  • Approved out-of-town travel for Administrator Steve Pegram to ICMA conference in Columbus not to exceed $800
  • Accepted $200 donation from Dillon Amstead for tornado recovery efforts
  • Accepted $2,032.23 donation from Goshen Lions Club for tornado recovery efforts
  • Appropriated $127,000 in ARP line item 2272-760-710-1000 for land purchase
  • Approved 3% pay raise for Zoning Administrative Assistant Alyssa Hudley retroactive to August 14, 2022
  • Approved Resolution R-047-2022 authorizing purchase of real property at 6765 Goshen Road
  • Approved purchase of property at 6765 Goshen Road from Van Landingham Brothers Inc. for $125,000

Action Items (9)

  • Steve Pegram to present formal monument sign schematics and options at next trustees meeting
  • Steve Pegram to continue negotiations on additional piece of property
  • Cheryl to collect bids for Meijer Cemetery fence repair
  • Cheryl to coordinate IT and phone line setup for temporary firehouse
  • Fire department to frame out garage doors in-house for temporary firehouse at old police station
  • Trustee Hodges to attend August Clermont County Township Association meeting and thank assisting townships
  • Steve Pegram to file for tax exemption on newly purchased property
  • Steve Pegram to pass along appreciation to Alyssa Hudley for her work
  • Aaron Ellerman to continue coordinating Long-Term Recovery Committee efforts

Financials

  • $125,000 for purchase of property at 6765 Goshen Road
  • $127,000 appropriated in ARP fund for land purchase
  • $800 not-to-exceed for Steve Pegram's ICMA conference travel
  • $200 donation from Dillon Amstead for tornado recovery
  • $2,032.23 donation from Goshen Lions Club for tornado recovery (reimbursing mutual aid lunches)

  • $17/hour probationary rate for part-time firefighter paramedic
  • $39,000 to $51,000 price range for monument signs
