  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills for Feb 24 - Mar 9, 2021
  • Approved out-of-town travel for Officer Greg McAllister for death investigation training at $550
  • Approved out-of-town travel for Sergeant Dylan West for subject control instructor training at $850
  • Approved fire and EMS budget true-up resulting in $46,975.20 reduction in appropriations
  • Approved Resolution R005-2021 for fire and EMS mutual aid agreement with Clermont County agencies
  • Approved Christmas and New Year's holiday office closings for Dec 23-24 and Dec 30-31, 2021
  • Approved $10,000 increase in general fund appropriations for administration building repairs and maintenance
  • Authorized Township Administrator to execute contract with Choice One Engineering for Charles Snyder Road/SR 28 widening project at $18,500 plus $3,500 allowance
  • Approved Community Cleanup Day for Saturday June 5, 2021 from 8am to noon
  • Authorized Township Administrator to execute contract with Edmonds Landscaping not to exceed $16,000 for demolition of 1910 Main Street
  • Approved collective bargaining agreement with Goshen Township Police Association for 2021-2023 retroactive to January 1, 2021
  • Approved increase of police extra duty employment rate to $40 per hour for school district and mobile home park details
  • Reappointed Lee Lewis to zoning commission for 5-year term (2021-2025)
  • Appointed David Frisbee as alternate to zoning commission for 2-year term (2021-2022)
  • Reappointed Ed Sigla to board of zoning appeals for 5-year term (2021-2025)
  • Appointed Tom Curry to board of zoning appeals to replace Mary Donna Elam for unexpired term (2018-2022)
  • Appointed Sean Burke to board of zoning appeals for 5-year term (2021-2025)
  • Appointed Derek Holmes as alternate to board of zoning appeals for 2-year term (2021-2022)
  • Adjourned meeting at 8:41 p.m.

1.26.2021 BOT MTG

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on January 26, 2021. Key business included approving road mile certifications, personnel actions including a resignation and a separation of a probationary firefighter, amending an IT contract, and authorizing pre-disciplinary proceedings against a firefighter paramedic following an executive session. The newly elected Clermont County Prosecutor Mark Dekovey introduced himself to the board.

Introduction of newly elected Clermont County Prosecutor Mark DekoveyCertification of 46.626 road miles for 2021 with three new road additionsPersonnel actions: resignation of Mark Peters, separation of Michael Krohn, hiring of Paul HilliardIT contract amendment from $2,350 to $2,560/monthTownship website redesign in progressColumbarium shelter improvements - lighting and fan installationCombs property pursuit requiring coordination with AG and EPANew police cruiser receivedSeven new fire department recruits onboardedZoning department 2020 permit recap - exceeded 2019 numbersExecutive session regarding employee compensation/disciplineAuthorization of pre-disciplinary process for a firefighter paramedic for alleged misconductFirst video-recorded meeting for the township

Decisions (10)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
  • Motion to approve payment of bills for January 13–26, 2021 - passed (Trustee Hodges abstained due to conflict of interest)
  • Motion to approve certificate of road miles (46.626 miles) for Clermont County Engineer for 2021 - passed
  • Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Mark Peters retro to December 17, 2020 - passed
  • Motion to release probationary part-time firefighter EMT Michael Krohn from employment effective 1-22-21 - passed
  • Motion to hire probationary part-time fitness instructor Paul Hilliard - passed
  • Motion to amend IT contract from $2,350/month to $2,560/month (originally approved January 12, 2021) - passed
  • Motion to enter executive session at 7:31 PM per ORC 121.22 regarding employee appointment/employment/compensation and bargaining sessions - passed
  • Motion to authorize Township Administrator/Fire Chief to initiate pre-disciplinary process for alleged misconduct of a Township Firefighter Paramedic which may result in discharge - passed
  • Motion to adjourn at 8:56 PM - passed

Action Items (7)

  • Steve Watkins to provide pricing for lighting/motion light and fan for columbarium shelter
  • Director Meadows to get road mile certificate signed and returned to Clermont County Engineer
  • Township Administrator to present redesigned website at the next trustees meeting
  • Township Administrator/Fire Chief to initiate pre-disciplinary process for a firefighter paramedic
  • Continue search for part-time zoning inspector
  • Follow up on Gaynor Road issue
  • Chief Pegram/Harry Holberg to reach out to Attorney General and EPA regarding Combs property

Financials

  • $2,560/month - amended IT contract cost (up from $2,350/month)
  • 46.626 road miles certified for 2021 (approximately 93 lane miles)
  • 252 zoning permits issued in 2020

BOT 2.23.2021

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held an abbreviated regular meeting before going into a lengthy executive session to interview police officer applicants. The board approved the payment of bills, passed a resolution requesting the Clermont County Commissioners vacate a portion of East Huntley Road, and acknowledged the road and safety services departments for their work during heavy snow. The executive session lasted approximately three hours, after which the meeting adjourned with no additional motions.

  • Approved payment of bills for February 10, 2021 through February 23, 2021

  • Adjourned into executive session at 7:11 PM per ORC 121.22 for appointment/employment/compensation of public employee and review of negotiations/bargaining sessions with public employees

2021-02-09 Regular Meeting routine
59m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 2.9.2021

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering police and fire department staffing, fleet purchases, zoning updates, and community development initiatives. Four new full-time police officers were appointed and two firefighter paramedic hires were authorized. The board approved the purchase of two new Ford police interceptor SUVs and a new collective bargaining agreement with the firefighters union. Discussions included the Combs Trucking legal case, reestablishing a Community Investment Corporation (CIC), the township branding logo, and proposed zoning text amendments.

Snow removal operations and service department truck breakdownCombs Trucking legal case update - 14-year civil/criminal case with Attorney General's officeCommunity Investment Corporation (CIC) reestablishment discussionFour new full-time police officer appointmentsTwo firefighter paramedic hiring authorizations2021-2023 firefighters union collective bargaining agreement approvalPurchase of two new police SUVsZoning text amendment regarding accessory structure lot coverage and height limitsDollar General approved with permit on Route 281260 Clarewell property abatement updateTownship branding logo discussion - disagreement on design elements2021 strategic plan update and adoption timelineDuke Energy community liaison meeting and potential sign fundingUAN bank reconciliation opt-in feature activationGainer Road project completionResignation of part-time firefighter EMT Logan Weisenberg

2021-01-12 Regular Meeting routine
1h 28m ▶ Vimeo

1.12.2021 Board of Trustees Meeting

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their first regular meeting of 2021, conducting annual reorganization including electing Dan Houser as chairperson and Lisa Hodges as vice chairperson. The board approved a new columbarium program at Goshen Cemetery with fee schedules, accepted resignations of Officer Tim Budai (20 years) and Lieutenant Daryl Roberts (26 years) due to medical issues, approved the adjusted 2021 budget showing improved financial position with projected ending unencumbered funds of $1.9 million, and approved a 2021-2023 collective bargaining agreement with the firefighters union. A resident praised the board's proactive approach to addressing a nuisance property on Clarawell Drive that had been vacant for 21 years.

Annual reorganization: chairperson, vice chair, meeting schedule, compensation approvalsColumbarium program launch at Goshen Cemetery with fee schedule and rulesResignation of Officer Tim Budai (20 years) and Lt. Daryl Roberts (26 years) for medical reasonsNuisance property enforcement on Clarawell Drive (vacant 21 years)Adjusted 2021 budget showing improved financial position2021-2023 collective bargaining agreement with firefighters union IAFF Local 3932Healthcare premium contribution increase from 10% to 12%BZA and Zoning Commission vacancies need to be filledLittle pantry placement for community food/supply donationsJoining CLOUT (Coalition of Large Ohio Urban Townships) - Goshen ranked 75th of 1,308 Ohio townshipsGoshen Road Bridge repair update - expected open by Saturday with future replacement pushed to 2022Discussion of forming an economic development committeeIT support contract renewalSurplus equipment sale authorization via govdeals.comPolice department 90-day fitness challengeZoning inspector position posting authorizedDuke Energy lighting district possibilities

  • Adopted the meeting agenda
  • Approved payment of bills from December 16, 2020 through January 12, 2021

  • Accepted resignation of Officer Tim Budai effective January 2, 2021
  • Accepted $50 in gift cards from anonymous donor for police officers

  • Accepted resignation of Lieutenant Daryl Roberts effective February 23, 2021
  • Rescinded December 15, 2020 motion for biweekly trustee pay (ORC requires monthly)
  • Approved Trustee Allen compensation at $1,469.83/month
  • Approved Trustee Hodges compensation at $1,469.83/month
  • Approved Trustee Hauserman compensation at $1,548.33/month

  • Set 2021 Board of Zoning Appeals member compensation at $50/month not to exceed $599/year
  • Approved Resolution R-001-2021 for surplus equipment sales on govdeals.com
  • Accepted resignation of property maintenance inspector Jeff Grebel retroactive to 12-31-2020
  • Approved vacation carryover in excess of 120 hours for Division Chief Kevin Lynch
  • Authorized renewal of IT support contract for 2021 at $2,350/month
  • Approved placement of a little pantry on township administration building property
  • Approved Resolution R-002-2021 to join CLOUT at $200 annual cost
  • Accepted $450 in gift cards from Wage War Apparel for Fire and EMS Department
  • Approved adjusted 2021 budget for Goshen Township
  • Approved 2021-2023 collective bargaining agreement with IAFF Local 3932
  • Approved adjustment of employee healthcare premium contribution from 10% to 12% effective January 1, 2021
  • Approved unpaid leave of absence for Captain Danny Vanover effective January 31, 2021 for up to six months
  • Authorized Harry to proceed with posting zoning inspector position

2026-03-24 Regular Meeting routine
1h 44m ▶ Vimeo

BOT 3-24-26

The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting covering a wide range of topics including a presentation on the Clermont County Senior Services Levy renewal, adoption of a resolution to create a new M-3 Limited High Impact Industrial zoning district (primarily targeting data centers), a discussion on Duke Energy's proposed electric distribution rate increase filed with PUCO, and a comprehensive presentation on the township's updated future land use plan. The board also received updates on the ongoing construction of Fire Station 18, the Quint fire apparatus build, cemetery and signage projects, and community events.

Clermont County Senior Services Levy renewal presentation (May 5th ballot)Creation of M-3 Limited High Impact Industrial Zoning District targeting data centers and high-impact industrial usesDuke Energy proposed electric distribution rate increase filed with PUCOComprehensive future land use plan update presentation by county plannerFire Station 18 construction nearing completion with May 8th grand opening plannedQuint fire apparatus build starting with late June/early July delivery expectedMedic 218 repairs underway at Ford following October accidentMain Street building wall collapse and safety meeting with county and ownerGoshen Township Historical Society fundraising events including flag donations and April 18th horse race fundraiserCommunity survey results emphasizing rural character preservation and managed growthState Route 28 corridor development and traffic concerns (34,000+ daily trips at Buckwheat Road intersection)Cluster subdivision concept and agricultural preservation programs discussedInfrastructure needs including sewer capacity limitations near Deerfield Road

Decisions (3)

  • Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
  • Motion to approve payment of bills for March 10-24, 2026 passed unanimously
  • Resolution R-0122026 adopted unanimously to initiate zoning text amendments establishing the M-3 Limited High Impact Industrial District

Action Items (9)

  • John Langenhorn to continue refining the M-3 zoning district resolution as a living document
  • Admin staff to formulate a letter opposing Duke Energy's proposed rate increase for submission to PUCO and Ohio Township Association
  • Trustee Hauserman to coordinate with Clermont County Township Association and Alan Freeman at Pierce Township to organize a collective response to Duke Energy rate increase
  • All officials and residents encouraged to submit public comments to PUCO regarding Duke Energy rate case (26-0132-EL-AIR)
  • Drew (county planner) to meet with Goshen staff around April 4th to address comments on the future land use plan
  • Drew to obtain updated demographic data from school district consultant and incorporate into the land use plan
  • Final draft of future land use plan to be ready by April 7th with trustee presentation on April 14th
  • Chad to coordinate installation of new sign poles once delivered to complete the sign grant
  • Melinda/admin team to continue getting written clarification from Duke Energy (Samantha) on exact rate impact details

Financials

  • $26/year cost per $100,000 home value for Senior Services Levy renewal
  • $78/year cost for average $300,000 Clermont County home for Senior Services Levy
  • 90+ cents of every Senior Services levy dollar goes back into community services
  • Senior Services served over 4,000 individuals, provided 40,000+ rides and nearly 70,000 meals last year
  • 703 Adult Protective Services referrals last year
  • Duke Energy proposes residential customer charge increase from $8 to $14/month (75% increase on that component)
  • Duke Energy claims overall residential bill increase will be 3-5%
  • $2,000 per acre cap ($500,000 max per farm) for Clean Ohio Agricultural Easement Purchase Program
  • Home prices in north Clermont submarket rose 51% from 2019-2024
  • Over 3,800 new housing units reviewed countywide in past 12 months
  • Smith Bay and Charles Snyder repaving OPWC award of nearly $500,000
  • Linton Road bridge replacement OPWC award of nearly $600,000
  • $2.5 million awarded for Combs Dump Site remediation
  • ODOT Safer Routes to School grants reimburse up to 100% of eligible costs with $1 million project limit
  • M-3 district requires minimum 100-acre lot size with 300-foot setbacks and 1,000-foot separation from residential
